Title: Arrest
Summary:
In November 2025, the Regional Explosive Unit was advised of a possible location-based pattern of Arson calls for service by Colorado Springs Fire Department Station 11.  CSFD Station 11 personnel reported several fires set inside dumpsters, and grassy areas in the 2000 Block of Carmel Dr.

Through investigation, the Regional Explosives Unit identified 24 year-old Michael Lopez as a suspect in several of the fires.  After contacting additional witnesses, Lopez was identified as specifically starting two dumpster fires, on October 21st, 2025, and on November 2nd, 2025.  

On March 26, 2026, Lopez was contacted and arrested without incident.  He was served and released on two counts of Second Degree Arson, both misdemeanors.

Title: Assault
Summary: On Thursday, March 26th, at approximately 6:51 AM, the Colorado Springs Police Department communications center received numerous calls of a female on I-25 attempting to cross the interstate near Nevada/Tejon. The Real Time Crime Center (RTCC) arrived in the area with a drone and located the female in the middle of I-25 just north of the MLK interchange. RTCC was able to guide responding officers into the area. As officers were responding, the female darted in and out of traffic on I-25 and was nearly struck by several vehicles. Additionally, the female picked up road debris and threw it at several vehicles, causing damage. The female picked up a rock and threw it at one of the officers, striking the officer’s driver’s side window. The female was eventually apprehended by officers in the middle of I-25 just south of the MLK bypass. The female was arrested for Attempted Second Degree Assault on a Peace Officer along with several misdemeanor charges related to the incident. There were no injuries in this call and traffic interruption was minimal.

Title: Other
Summary: Officer Matt Kirby and Officer Justin Cropper observed a male party wanted on felony warrants in America the Beautiful Park.  The male was walking a dog that was limping.  Officer Kirby identified himself and advised the male he was under arrest.  The male became argumentative and postured as though to flee.  Officer Kirby physically restrained the male, during which time he was bit twice by the male's dog, leaving a puncture injury to the inside of his left forearm and scratches to his left shoulder.  The male was taken into custody and the dog was released to the Human Society.  Officer Kirby received treatment at Memorial Hospital and returned to work.

Title: Warrant
Summary:
Detectives with the Metro Theft and Burglary Unit investigated a criminal pattern involving money laundering, identity theft, burglary, and motor vehicle theft. Through a collaborative investigation, detectives identified the suspect as Carl Goodwin, and a warrant was obtained for his arrest.

On 03/24/2026, detectives located Mr. Goodwin and contacted him along with two other individuals who had unrelated outstanding warrants for their arrest. During contact, detectives discovered that Mr. Goodwin was in possession of a felony amount of narcotics and mail theft burglary tools. The on‑scene investigation also led to the recovery of a stolen vehicle. Mr. Goodwin and the two individuals he was contacted with were taken into custody without incident.
Adults Arrested: Carl Goodwin, Katie Kimmett, and Robert Scott

Title: Warrant
Summary: On March 24, 2026, at 3:14 pm, officers from the Falcon Patrol Division were dispatched to an alternate address in response to an ongoing neighbor situation located in the 9100 block of Bellcove Circle. The reporting party (RP) told officers the neighbor was caught on camera damaging their house, which was now for sale due to the extensive / escalating problems. The RP told officers the son of the neighbor made threats to cause physical harm and stated, “I want to see your grandkid’s intestines all over the road.”  During the officer’s investigation, they developed probable cause for criminal mischief and also noted (2) a mandatory protection order (MPO) violation related to the address he was residing at, stemming from a previous domestic violence case. The officer researched the premise's history and noted prior history involving the father and a revolver in the house and signs of aggression toward PD. The officer gathered resources and attempted to make contact with the suspect at the known address. Upon contact, they confirmed the suspect was there in violation of the active MPO and were met with immediate aggression by the suspect and his father as well. Both were verbally hostile with officers, uncooperative, would not listen as officers attempted to explain the active warrants, made hand gestures to shoot at officers, and repeatedly told officers to take out their guns and shoot themselves in the mouth and do the world a favor. Officers maintained a safe perimeter with adequate vehicle cover / ballistic shields while a search/arrest warrant was applied for and obtained. Subsequently, the father surrendered peacefully and was taken into custody without incident, served, and released for misdemeanor Interference. The suspect, however, remained inside the residence. The suspect eventually presented at the side garage door, where a less-lethal 40mm round of pepper spray was utilized to cast over his head. Unfortunately, once it struck the door, the round shattered in several pieces and struck the suspect in the face, causing minor injuries. This allowed officers to use tactics to advance with K9, push him to the ground, and take him into custody without further incident. The suspect was transported to a local hospital for treatment and then booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center on his warrants, the new case for criminal mischief, and another case for an assault on an officer and obstruction.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary:
On March 22, 2026, at approximately 8:10 p.m., the Colorado Springs Police Department received a call for service regarding a two‑vehicle traffic crash near the intersection of Briargate Boulevard and Lexington Drive. Responding officers arrived and determined that the two vehicles had collided head‑on in the westbound lanes of Briargate Boulevard. Preliminary information indicated that the eastbound vehicle had been engaged in a speed contest with another vehicle prior to the collision.

Both drivers were transported to local hospitals with serious but initially non‑life‑threatening injuries. It was later reported that the driver of the westbound vehicle died as a result of complications related to surgery stemming from the crash.

Speed is suspected to be a contributing factor. This remains an open and active investigation and, due to its nature, it is being handled by the Major Crash Team.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary:
On 3/20/2026 at approximately 2211 hours, CSPD officers from the Sand Creek Division were dispatched to a vehicle vs bicycle traffic crash at Academy Bl and Pace Dr. Officers arrived on scene and discovered a female with serious injuries in the roadway. Through their investigation it was discovered the injured female was riding an electric dirt bike northbound on Academy Bl approaching Pace Dr. A black SUV was traveling southbound on Academy Bl, and executed a left turn to eastbound Pace Dr, failing to yield to the dirt bike, resulting in a collision. The black SUV failed to stop and fled the scene. During the investigation, information about the suspect vehicle was obtained, and relayed to other officers. 
While responding to an unrelated call for service, Sand Creek patrol officers found the suspect vehicle parked at an apartment complex. Officers were able to contact the female driver of the SUV, and she was ultimately arrested for multiple offenses related to the hit and run traffic crash. The Major Crash Team was notified of this incident.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 3/21/2026, at approximately 2140 hours, Stetson Hills patrol officers responded to an address in the area of 3600 block of Austin Bluffs Parkway for a reported stabbing. Upon arrival, officers learned a party on scene sustained a wound consistent with being stabbed. The victim stated they had been stabbed in the neck via a machete during a disturbance at a nearby apartment complex parking lot. The victim was transported to a local hospital to be treated for their injuries. The victim's injuries were not life- threatening. No suspects have been identified at this time.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 03/20 at approximately 6:17 p.m., officers were dispatched to the 6900 block of Peyote Way in northeast Colorado Springs regarding a reported family disturbance involving a juvenile male armed with a knife. Prior to officers’ arrival, a third party intervened and subdued the juvenile. The suspect continued to struggle with the third party until officers arrived and took him into custody without incident. The suspect’s mother was identified as the victim of a misdemeanor assault, and the third party was identified as the victim of felony menacing. The suspect was later treated for minor injuries at a local hospital and placed on a mental health hold by medical personnel.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary:
Update: As of 12:01am all lanes are open to traffic. 

On 03/20/26 at 10:11PM the Colorado Springs Police Department received a call regarding a vehicle vs dirt bike crash at the intersection of S Academy Bl and Pace Dr. The investigation ongoing at this time, and due to the scope of the investigation the following road closures apply:

Northbound S Academy Bl is closed at Pace Dr

Westbound Pace Dr is closed at S Academy Bl

This entry will be updated when the roadway is re-opened. Citizens are advised to avoid the area at this time.

Title: MVT
Summary: On 03/19/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a reported stolen black Hyundai Santa Cruz, stolen from the 100 block of Swope Avenue. On 03/20/2026, CSPD Motor Vehicle Theft Unit Detectives located the stolen Hyundai occupied by a male driver, John Espinoza, age 25. During the investigation, John stole a license plate from another parked vehicle and replaced the registered plates from the Hyundai with the stolen license plate. John parked the stolen vehicle in the 3000 block of East Platte Avenue and began to walk away from the area. John was contacted by units while he was on foot and placed into custody. He was charged with motor vehicle theft and theft of license plate.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: At approx. 0106 hours, Officers were dispatched to a domestic violence related, in-progress assault in the area of 10900 Mist View.  The reporting party stated that they were actively being assaulted, to include being struck with a firearm, by their husband.  The suspect fled the apartment just prior to officers arrival and was believed to possibly still be in the area. Containment was set and K9, tactical assets and a drone responded to assist with a search. An extensive search was conducted but the suspect was not located at that time.  The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On March 19 2026, at approximately 3:02 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a pedestrian vs vehicle crash at the intersection of Fountain Blvd and Circle Drive.  Preliminary investigation revealed a pedestrian was crossing on a scooter when they were struck by a vehicle.  The pedestrian was transported to a local hospital in critical condition.  Due to the nature of the collision, the CSPD Major Crash Team assumed the investigation.  At this time, speed and impairment do not appear to be factors.  The intersection will be restricted for several hours while detectives investigate and process the scene.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary:
On March 18, 2026, at approximately 6:20 a.m., the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) received a call for service regarding a traffic crash involving a vehicle and pedestrian near the 5800 block of N. Academy Blvd. Officers responded and determined that the pedestrian was attempting to cross Academy Blvd. mid-block when he was struck by the northbound vehicle. 
Despite immediate on‑scene medical intervention the pedestrian was pronounced dead on scene.
Due to the circumstances of the crash, the CSPD Major Crash Team responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation. Currently, impairment is not suspected. All lanes of northbound and all but one southbound lane of Academy Blvd were closed for several hours while investigators processed the scene.
This investigation remains open and ongoing.

Title: Other
Summary: On 3/17/2026 at approximately 5:28 AM, CSPD Communications Center received a call for service for a theft from a vehicle in progress in the 6900 block of Granite Peak Drive.  The reporting party woke up to find at least one suspect breaking into a vehicle parked in front of their residence. The victim confronted the suspects. The suspects made verbal threats toward the victim, then fled the area in a white SUV or hatchback. The victim heard at least two gunshots as the vehicle fled the area. The suspects have not been located.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 3/16/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department received a report of shots fired in the 6200 block of Barnes Rd. Upon arrival, officers located shell casings and discovered a residence had been fired into, resulting in water damage to the building. With the assistance of the Real-Time Crime Center and ALPR cameras, officers with the Stetson Hills Division were able to locate the suspect's vehicle and take Alaric Von Royeaux into custody approximately one hour after the initial incident.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
In February of 2026, CSPD’s Metro Burglary Unit began an investigation into a pattern of garage burglaries occurring throughout the city and in other jurisdictions along the Front Range. In early March, investigators identified the primary suspect in those crimes as Eric Vasquez, and it was discovered that Vasquez had been arrested (and subsequently released) for similar crimes in Castle Rock, Colorado, in December of 2025.
 
On the evening of March 15th, Metro Burglary investigators located Vasquez’s vehicle parked in the 6400 block of Showhorse Ct., in Colorado Springs. When Vasquez was observed entering the vehicle, operators with CSPD’s Tactical Enforcement Unit made contact with Vasquez. Vasquez attempted unsuccessfully to flee in his vehicle and eventually fled on foot. He was apprehended a short time later in the yard of a neighboring residence. 

The investigation into the scope of Vasquez’s criminality is ongoing, but he was booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center on charges including burglary, criminal mischief, and obstruction.

Title: Cold Accident Reporting
Summary: ****UPDATE***** As of 03-15-2026 at 14:00 PM CSPD is no longer on Crash Alert. The Colorado Springs Police Department is on crash alert status as of 3/15/2026 at 7:45 AM due to road conditions. Drivers are reminded if they are involved in a traffic accident with no fatality or injury requiring medical attention, no driver involved is suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, all drivers are present with license, registration and insurance information, and no damage to public property such as a street sign or utility pole has occurred, drivers should exchange their information and follow the procedure for completing a counter (cold) report. When damage to any vehicle requires a wrecker, a counter report can still be made providing the previous criteria is met. Drivers can either pick up a traffic accident report form at any of the police department substations or they can go online at coloradosprings.gov/police, click on “Online Reporting” and select “Cold Reporting: Report a Minor Traffic Accident” for instructions. Drivers should also be reminded to take the appropriate steps to avoid being involved in a traffic accident during adverse weather and road conditions. Drivers should check road and travel conditions before heading out and leave more time to get to their destination. Drivers need to travel at a safe speed for the conditions and leave an increased and safe following distance from the vehicle in front of them. Drivers should use streets and roadways less hazardous in winter driving conditions. Drivers also need to remove snow and ice obstructing their vision and have adequate snow tires on their vehicles. Please Drive Safely!

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 3/14/2026, at approximately 0845 hours, CSPD patrol officers were dispatched to the intersection of W. Colorado Ave and 18th Street regarding a hit and run traffic crash. Upon arrival, officers identified many witnesses and learned there was a disturbance involving a firearm between several vehicles that preceded the crash. Evidence was collected at the scene, and further follow-up is being conducted to identify all involved parties. One of the parties from the traffic crash was transported to a local hospital with non-life-threatening injuries.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 3/13/2026 at approximately 11:05 pm, officers responded to the 3800 block of Radiant Dr. for the report of a robbery in progress. Upon arrival, officers located a group of individuals in the parking lot physically restraining a male. Officers were able to detain the male and recover a 9mm Beretta handgun. The investigation later revealed that the suspect flashed a gun from his waistband and later pointed the gun at the victim while demanding money. No injuries occurred or items of value taken.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 03/13/26, G.H. Officers were dispatched to 331 S. Hancock Av on a Domestic Disturbance.  While en route, officers observed the suspect, who ran from the officers, in the area of S. El Paso St. and E. Costilla St.  With the help of RTCC, the suspect was located in the 500 block of E Pikes Peak Ave. Upon contact, the suspect became physically aggressive.  While being taken into custody, the suspect bit the arresting Sgt., causing pain. With the help of a citizen, the suspect was taken into custody and booked into CJC for Assault, VPO, and other related charges.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 3/13/26, officers with the Sand Creek Division were dispatched to the 2900 block of Delta Dr in regards to people setting a brush fire in the creek bed behind the location. A CSPD drone was able to confirm there was a brush fire at the location and was able to monitor an individual walking away from the immediate area of the fire. Multiple officers were able to set containment in the area, and with the assistance of the drone, were able to contact the suspect. After an investigation, it was determined that the suspect would be taken into custody for felony arson charges. CSFD was able to access the fire and extinguish it before it spread. The fire did not result in any damage to personal property.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 03/12/26 at 4:39PM, officers from the Sand Creek Division were in the area of the 00 block of University Drive on an unrelated call. The officers were flagged down by occupants in a vehicle, who said someone had been stabbed and were on the way to the hospital. An officer escorted the vehicle to the hospital, and determined there were two stabbing victims in the vehicle. The suspect reportedly was only someone they knew of but not by name. The suspect left the area before officers arrived. Officers were able to locate a scene outside of an apartment building. We are actively working to identify all involved parties.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On March 6, 2026, at 0207 hours, CSPD officers responded to a reported robbery at a gas station / convenience store in the 5900 block of Dublin Boulevard. The suspect had fled on foot prior to officers’ arrival. The clerk reported that the suspect brandished a revolver and demanded multiple vape products. Surveillance footage depicted the suspect as a white male, late teens to early 20s, approximately 5'7", wearing all black clothing, a hooded sweatshirt, a mask, reflective glasses, and carrying a dark-colored backpack used to conceal the stolen items. The suspect was not located.

On March 9, 2026, the Metro Division / Robbery Unit assumed the investigation and identified the incident as part of a six case pattern involving three convenience stores in the same area dating back to December 2024. Area canvassing and surveillance review led to the identification of a juvenile male suspect.

On March 11, 2026, detectives obtained an arrest warrant for the juvenile suspect. Working collaboratively with the Tactical Enforcement Unit, K9 Unit, and Real Time Crime Center (RTCC), detectives located and arrested the suspect in northeast Colorado Springs without incident. He is being held on five counts of Aggravated Robbery and one count of Criminal Attempt–Aggravated Robbery.

Title: Warrant
Summary: Starting in February of 2026, CSPD Metro Narcotics Unit and the FBI began a joint investigation into a drug trafficker who had a history of vehicular assaults, aggravated robbery, and felony menacing. On March 11, 2026 at 5:00 AM the FBI SWAT team, with the assistance of CSPD Narcotics, executed a search warrant at the suspect’s residence. Initially several residents exited the home however the suspect refused to come out.  The suspect began to set several fires within his residence before fleeing on foot. After a short foot chase, he was safely taken into custody thanks to our RTCC drone pilot. The suspect was treated at a local hospital for burns and smoke inhalation and later transported to CJC. CSFD was quickly on scene to extinguish the house fire, however extensive damage had been done. With the assistance of the CSPD EOD team, it was determined that the suspect would be charged with arson along with several other charges. No members of the FBI, CSPD, or CSFD were injured during this call for service.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On March 10, 2026, at approximately 8:00 a.m., detectives from the Colorado Springs Police Department’s Strategic Investigations Unit, Robbery Unit, Real Time Crime Center, and Tactical Enforcement Unit served search warrants in the 2300 and 2400 blocks of Lexus Drive as part of multiple ongoing robbery and weapons investigations involving multiple suspects. These warrants were related to separate warrants served on March 9 in the 900 block of Zamora Circle and in the 4300 block of Morning Sun Avenue.
As a result, officers arrested 25-year-old Christopher Colzie, 19-year-old Adrian Rivas-Loya, 36-year-old Ashley West, and 22-year-old Jeremiah Joslin. Detectives recovered a handgun, an AR-style pistol, methamphetamine, and cocaine during the operation.  
The Strategic Investigations Unit continues to conduct follow-up in connection with this investigation.
Adults Arrested: Christopher Colzie (25), Adrian Rivas-Loya (19), Ashley West (36) Jeremiah Joslin (22)

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Monday, March 9, 2025, members of the Colorado Springs Police Department, El Paso County Sheriff’s Office and the Colorado State Patrol conducted a BATTLE operation in the City of Colorado Springs and El Paso County.

During the BATTLE deployment detectives located a stolen red GMC Sierra pickup, reported stolen out of Walsenburg, CO the morning of 03/06/2026. Information received advised the stolen vehicle had been involved in a pursuit with officers in Walsenburg. In the area of Austin Bluffs Blvd and Academy Blvd, tactical units conducted a contact block on the stolen vehicle. The driver, Odehlia Sauners, age 38 and the passenger, Riley Draper, age 44, were placed in custody. During the search of the stolen vehicle, detectives located two handguns and jewelry, both stolen from a burglary in Walsenburg. Odehlia was charged with motor vehicle theft and possession of weapon by previous offender. Riley was charged with possession of weapon by previous offender. 

On 03/08/2026, CSPD investigated a report of a stolen blue Ford Edge from the 6200 block of Corporate Dr. During the BATTLE deployment, detectives located the stolen Ford Edge in the 6200 block of Corporate Dr and the driver was identified as Louella Warren, age 50. Louella was charged with unauthorized use of a motor vehicle.

On 03/09/2026, CSPD investigated a report of a stolen silver Toyota RAV4 from 100 block of W. Las Vegas St. BATTLE officers located the stolen vehicle in the 2900 block of Galley Rd. two hours after it was reported stolen. The vehicle was occupied by a juvenile male driver and a female passenger identified as Isabella Mitchell, age 18. Tactical units contacted the stolen vehicle and both the juvenile male and Isabella ran on foot. Officers gave chase and quickly apprehended both individuals. The juvenile male and Isabella were both charged with motor vehicle theft and obstructing a peace officer.

Title: Select Incident Type
Summary: On 03/10/26 at approximately 1738 hours, CSPD was notified of a vehicle versus pedestrian traffic crash.  Officers responded to the scene; despite medical intervention, the pedestrian was pronounced deceased on scene.  Preliminary investigation determined the vehicle was turning left from eastbound Costilla St. to northbound Wahsatch Ave and struck a pedestrian.  At this time, speed and impairment do not appear to be factors.  The intersection was closed for several hours.  Due to the circumstances, the Major Crash Team responded and assumed the investigation.

Title: Burglary
Summary: On 03/08/26 Officers were dispatched to the area of S. Weber Street and E. Mill Street on a burglary in progress. A neighbor reported that the house was vacant and the owner recently deceased. The neighbor reported unknown suspects were in the backyard removing valuables. RTCC drone was first on scene and had visual on three male suspects. Officers arrived on scene and all three suspects fled on foot. RTCC drone was able to follow two of the parties to the Maverik gas station on S. Nevada Av and operator observed one of the suspects attempting to change his appearance. That suspect was contacted by patrol a short time later and taken into custody without incident. Two other suspects were positively identified through collaboration of RTCC and patrol. Arrest warrants are pending for two outstanding suspects.

Title: Other
Summary: On March 8, 2026, officers were dispatched to St Francis Hospital (6001 East Woodmen Road) for an individual who had sustained a gunshot wound.  Upon arrival, officers contacted the male and learned the male sustained a self-inflicted gunshot to the hand.  The male is in stable condition at the hospital.  The investigation is continuing.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 03/07/2026 Sand Creek officers were dispatched to a theft in progress call for service near the 400 block of Kitfield Vw. Call screen information indicated that two males were attempting to enter vehicles at the location and possibly pointed a gun at the reporting party’s security camera. Officers arrived on scene, and two males immediately fled the area toward a densely wooded creek bed. Officers set up containment around the area and requested assistance of a drone. RTCC deployed a drone to the area and quickly located two suspects fleeing through the creek and jumping property fences. The drone was also able to capture one of the suspects discarding a black backpack. Through the assistant of RTCC drone tracking, both suspects were contacted and arrested. The backpack was recovered and found to contain a firearm that had been stolen from a vehicle two days prior. Both suspects are juveniles and are facing felony level charges.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On 03/06/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a report of a stolen silver Mitsubishi Outlander from the 100 block of Coleridge Avenue. The stolen vehicle was located later in the day by patrol officers. CSPD Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives responded to the area and assumed the investigation. Detectives identified a male driver, Jose Garcia-Ruiz, age 27, driving the stolen vehicle. In the 1400 block of Chelton Road, Jose was contacted by tactical units and detectives. Jose fled on foot but was quickly apprehended and placed into custody. 

Detectives learned of an attempted motor vehicle theft incident that occurred on 03/05/2026 from the 3700 block of East Pikes Peak Avenue and a burglary of motor vehicle incident that occurred in the 4400 block of Edison Avenue on 03/04/2026. Detectives confirmed Jose was the suspect in those cases but had not yet been identified. Jose also used credit cards stolen from the vehicles and attempted to purchase items fraudulently.
 
Jose was charged with motor vehicle theft, five (5) counts of identity theft, attempted motor vehicle theft, criminal trespass of a vehicle, and obstructing a peace officer.

Title: Cold Accident Reporting
Summary:
The Colorado Springs Police Department is on Crash Alert status as of 3/6/2026 at 12:05 PM due to road conditions. Drivers are reminded if they are involved in a traffic accident with no fatality or injury requiring medical attention, no driver involved is suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, all drivers are present with license, registration and insurance information, and no damage to public property such as a street sign or utility pole has occurred, drivers should exchange their information and follow the procedure for completing a counter (cold) report. When damage to any vehicle requires a wrecker, a counter report can still be made providing the previous criteria is met. Drivers can either pick up a traffic accident report form at any of the police department substations or they can go online at coloradosprings.gov/police, click on “Online Reporting” and select “Cold Reporting: Report a Minor Traffic Accident” for instructions. Drivers should also be reminded to take the appropriate steps to avoid being involved in a traffic accident during adverse weather and road conditions. Drivers should check road and travel conditions before heading out and leave more time to get to their destination. Drivers need to travel at a safe speed for the conditions and leave an increased and safe following distance from the vehicle in front of them. Drivers should use streets and roadways less hazardous in winter driving conditions. Drivers also need to remove snow and ice obstructing their vision and have adequate snow tires on their vehicles. Please Drive Safely!

*****As of 11:10 AM on 3/7/26 CSPD is no longer on Accident Alert*****

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 3/5/2026, Custer County Sheriff's Office contacted the El Paso County Sheriff's Office for assistance with a wanted party from their agency. Custer County had obtained an arrest warrant for Jeremy Hockett for weapons and narcotic related charges including special offender, possession of an explosive or incendiary device, possessing un-serialized firearms, and possession of schedule 2 narcotics. EPSO requested the assistance of the Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force who began immediately working Hockett's apprehension. On 3/6/26, Hockett was observed exiting a residence near the intersection of S. Carefree Cir and N. Murray Blvd. As Hockett was walking on foot, he was contacted by VOFTF detectives and TEU Tac Team officers. Hockett was arrested without incident and booked into the El Paso County Jail.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On March 6th, 2026, at 2:57 AM, the public safety communications center was notified of a robbery at a Circle K gas station / convenience store. Officers responded but the suspect had already fled on foot. The clerk stated the suspect brandished a revolver and demanded multiple vapes from behind the counter. Surveillance video showed the suspect as a white male, late teens or early 20s, 5'7, wearing all black clothing with a hoodie over the head, a mask along with reflective glasses. The suspect was carrying a dark colored backpack which the stolen vapes were placed into. The suspect fled on foot and was not located.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 03/05/25, Sand Creek Patrol officers were dispatched to a residence in the 4200 block of Morley Drive for a report of a shooting. Officers arrived on scene and contacted the occupants, including a juvenile victim suffering from a gunshot wound. The victim was transported to a local hospital where he was treated for serious but non life-threatening wound. It was reported the victim was shot by an acquaintance who has yet to be identified. Due to the nature of the investigation, Assault, AVOU, and the Metro Crime Lab were notified and assumed the investigation.

Title: MVT
Summary: On 02/28/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a report of a stolen blue Dodge Ram 1500, stolen from the 300 block of South Cedar Drive. On 03/05/2026, Detectives with the Colorado Springs Police Department Motor Vehicle Theft Unit, located the stolen truck and identified Shawn Adkins, age 35, driving the stolen truck. Detectives attempted a traffic stop on the vehicle, but Shawn eluded detectives. Shawn abandoned the truck in the 1400 block of Tesla Drive and walked away from the vehicle. Shawn was located and contacted by detectives and placed into custody in the 1500 block of Tesla Drive. Shawn is a prolific motor vehicle theft offender. He was charged with motor vehicle theft and felony eluding.

Title: Select Incident Type
Summary: On March 5, 2026, at approximately 5:55 p.m., the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a traffic crash involving a motorcycle and a pickup on Hwy 24 and 21st Street. Officers responded and learned the motorcycle was eastbound at a high rate of speed when the pickup made a right on red from 21st Street to eastbound Hwy 24.  The motorcycle struck the pickup in the rear, ejecting the motorcycle driver and passenger.  Medical intervention was performed on scene, and both motorcycle occupants were transported to a local hospital with critical injuries.  Based on the circumstances, the CSPD Major Crash Team responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation. At this time, impairment is not suspected, although speed is still being investigated. Hwy 24 eastbound was closed for several hours while investigators processed the scene.

Title: Death
Summary: On 03/05/2026 at approximately 3:07AM, officers were dispatched to the 4200 block of Date St. for a medical call for service.  Upon arrival officers located a deceased female inside the residence.  Based on the circumstances, the homicide unit was contacted and assumed the investigation.  At this time, it is believed that all parties are accounted for. This remains an active investigation, and anyone with information is asked to call the Colorado Springs Police Department at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you may call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On February 12, 2026, at approximately 9:10 p.m., Colorado Springs Police Department officers were dispatched to the 1800 block of North Circle Drive regarding a reported robbery. A male suspect dressed in black and wearing a mask entered a business, pointed a handgun at the clerk, and took an undisclosed amount of cash, assorted Colorado Lottery scratch tickets, and a cordless landline phone. The suspect fled northbound and was not located at the time of the incident. (ETACS #35984)

On February 16, 2026, the Metro Division Robbery Unit assumed the investigation. Detectives conducted an extensive review of surveillance footage from the business and surrounding area, developing investigative leads that resulted in the identification of a suspect, 26 year old Rakeen Ishmar.

On March 3, 2026, investigators obtained an arrest warrant for Mr. Ishmar, along with a search warrant for his residence. On March 4, 2026, Mr. Ishmar was taken into custody without incident with assistance from the Tactical Enforcement Unit and support from the K-9 Unit and the Real Time Crime Center. A search of his residence resulted in the recovery of evidence relevant to the case. Mr. Ishmar was transported to the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center and booked on the charge of Aggravated Robbery.

Title: Other
Summary: Officers were dispatched to investigate a report of a male wearing tactical gear who pointed a semi-automatic handgun at the reporting person. The location is within a busy shopping center.  Officers quickly located the suspect and determined he did not have a firearm, but did display a cigarette lighter fashioned in the shape of a handgun. There were no injuries.  At least one business and a nearby school were placed on lockdown while the investigation ensued. The suspect was served and released with misdemeanor charges.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On March 2, 2026, at approximately 07:53 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a traffic crash in the 600 Block of South Circle Drive.  When officers arrived on scene, they located a full size pick up had driven into a building.  Video surveillance was located, which revealed the vehicle had been southbound through the parking lot and drove into the building.  One party in the building was injured but is in stable condition.  At this time, impairment and speed appear to be contributing factors.  The parking lot was closed for several hours while investigators processed the scene.

Title: Disturbance
Summary:
On 03/01/2026 at approximately 3:02 AM, officers from the Sand Creek Division were dispatched to the 3700 block of Astrozon Blvd. to investigate a disturbance.  Initial details in that call included that several people were involved in a verbal and physical disturbance and that some of the participants had guns.  While officers were on scene and in contact with people involved in the first disturbance, they heard gunshots coming from the parking lot.  Other citizens also heard the gunshots had called 911 to report the second disturbance.  
Officers contacted the occupants of one car that was leaving the parking lot, and learned that one of the vehicle’s occupants had suffered a gunshot wound.  That person was transported to a local hospital with serious, life-threatening injuries.  Officers also contacted a second vehicle that was leaving the parking lot. Occupants of that vehicle were detained and later interviewed by detectives from the Violent Crimes/Homicide Unit.  Two of those individuals were later arrested for attempted murder charges and transported to the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center.  Their identities are not being released at this time, as detectives are continuing their investigation.
 
This remains an active and ongoing investigation, and anyone with information is asked to call the Colorado Springs Police Department at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you may call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: MVT
Summary: On 02/24/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a report of a stolen 2013 Acura RDX, stolen from the 6000 block of Snowbird Drive. On 02/27/2025, Colorado Springs Police Department Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives located the stolen Acura in the 3600 block of Airport Road. The vehicle was occupied by a single male driver, identified as Nicolas Roberts, age 33. A patrol officer attempted a traffic stop on the stolen Acura, but Nicolas eluded the officer. Tactical units located the Acura in a nearby apartment complex and placed Nicolas into custody without incident. Nicolas was in possession of a .380 handgun and felony amounts of narcotics. Nicolas was charged with motor vehicle theft, felony eluding, special offender and felony possession of a controlled substance.

Title: MVT
Summary: On 02/25/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a report of a stolen 2016 black Subaru Forester from the 3900 block of Loma Vista Point. On 02/27/2025, Colorado Springs Police Department Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives located the stolen Subaru in the 1700 block of West Uintah Street. The vehicle was occupied by a single male driver, identified as Mauro Loper, age 54. Detectives conducted a traffic stop on the stolen Subaru and Mauro stopped in the 2500 block of West Kiowa Street and was placed in custody. Mauro was charged for motor vehicle theft.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On 02/25/2026, CSPD’s Metro Theft Unit conducted investigative follow up and identified the whereabouts of a metal theft suspect, Taylor Sanford, in the area of 300 Iowa Ave. Sanford had an active warrant for the theft of approximately $15,000 worth of metal from local businesses and city parks. During further follow up, detectives learned that another offender with an active warrant was in the same area. The offender was identified as Jaquavis Marthrel, who had an active warrant for second-degree burglary and has been associated with approximately 10 additional cases involving identity theft and money laundering.

In coordination with detectives from CSPD’s Burglary Unit and tactical officers, Taylor and Jaquavis were taken into custody without incident.

Title: Death
Summary:
At approximately 7:53 AM on February 26, 2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department received a call for service regarding a deceased individual in the 100 Block of East Vermijo Ave. Officers responded to the scene and located one deceased adult male. 

The CSPD Homicide Unit was notified, responded to the scene, and started an investigation into the death. The cause and manner of the death will be determined by the El Paso County Coroner's Office. 

This is an ongoing investigation, and anyone with any information related to it is asked to contact CSPD at 719-444-7000.

Title: Other
Summary: On February 25, 2026, at approximately 7:30 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department received a report of a disturbance in the 100 block of Audubon Drive involving a male with a baseball bat.  Upon arrival, officers learned that the male with the baseball bat went into his home.  Officers attempted to contact the male, identified as 43-year-old Benjamin Bryant.  Upon knocking on his front door, Benjamin Bryant refused to come out and threatened to shoot officers.  Officers continued and were unsuccessful in their attempts to get Benjamin Bryant to exit his home.  Members of the Tactical Enforcement Unit responded to assist.  A search warrant was obtained and after approximately 6 hours, Benjamin Bryant exited his home and was taken into custody.  Benjamin Brant was arrested for felony menacing resulting from this incident.  Benjamin Bryant was also wanted on an arrest warrant resulting from an earlier disturbance involving a neighbor.

Title: Other
Summary: On February 25, 2026, at 7:29 PM, a resident at a multifamily dwelling in the 600 block of West Van Buren reported a disturbance with a neighbor who was holding a handgun and attempting to break into the apartment.  The suspect had an outstanding felony warrant and a history of threats toward law enforcement.  The suspect ran into his apartment when officers arrived.  The suspect was heard inside his apartment, making threats to an unknown person. Officers forced entry to ensure the safety of the individuals within the apartment. They located the suspect inside the apartment and took him into custody.  Officers located two replica firearms in the apartment.  Officers’ investigation revealed the suspect made racially biased comments and threats towards his neighbors.  The suspect was booked into CJC related the warrant and this case.  No one was injured during the call for service.

Title: Other
Summary:
****UPDATE***
On 3/03/26 US Marshalls working with Metro RPM located and arrested both suspects. Both were taken into custody without incident and transported to the criminal Justice Center.



On 02/24/26 at approximately 1928 hours, Metro RPM received information that a wanted prolific retail crime offender was in the Home Depot located at 7120 N. Academy Blvd. Sgt. Tedeschi responded but the suspect left in his vehicle prior to arrival. A check of the ALPR system developed a pattern of travel for the suspects leading up to their arrival at Home Depot. Using knowledge of existing patterns involving the suspect and lack of ALPR hits after leaving Home Depot, Metro RPM detectives were able to predict a handful of stores the offender would travel to. Detectives worked with Stetson Hills Patrol officers who set up in the area of several of the predicted retail stores. Sgt. Bergstresser located the vehicle near on of those retail stores, noted it was unoccupied and deployed Piranha tire deflation device. As he moved to a better area to observe, he saw the wanted suspect and another wanted prolific offender come running out of the store with merchandise they just stole. Sgt. Bergstresser warned them of the deflation device and ordered them to stop but they refused to obey commands. The suspects got into the vehicle and took off at a high rate of speed. No pursuit was initiated, with the help of RTCC and patrol, the vehicle was located a short time later on Villa Loma Dr. with a blown tire; however, the suspects had fled. K9 and MVT detectives assisted in searching for the suspects. A significant amount of retail merchandise was recovered from the vehicle. Both suspects are still outstanding.
A warrant will be issued for the suspect for Felony Eluding

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary:
On February 24, 2026, at approximately 4:46 p.m., the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a traffic crash involving a motorcycle and a box van on Union Blvd at Woodland Hills Drive.  Officers responded and learned the motorcycle was northbound when the van turned left in front of the motorcycle.  Despite medical intervention, the driver of the motorcycle was pronounced on scene.  Based on the circumstances, the CSPD Major Crash Team responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation. 
At this time, impairment is not suspected, although speed is still being investigated. Union Blvd was closed for several hours while investigators processed the scene.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 2/24/2026 at 12:39AM the communications center received a report of shots fired at a closed business in the 700 block of Citadel Dr. N. As officers were responding, additional information was received that suspects had been burglarizing a business and had fired shots at security officers who intervened. When officers arrived at the scene, they located two uninjured victims, both of whom were working security. Investigation revealed that three suspects gained access to a business from a neighboring, vacant business. As the suspects were burglarizing the business, a security officer confronted them and they fled. During flight, at least one of the suspects fired multiple shots. As the suspects neared a getaway vehicle, one of the suspects fired additional shots toward a second security officer before fleeing in a vehicle. The suspects are unidentified at this time and the investigation is ongoing.

Title: Narcotics - Vice Activity
Summary: On 02-22-26, at approximately 1637 hours, officers were dispatched to the 2200 block of W. Bijou St. to investigate a reported trespassing in-progress.  Upon their arrival, Their investigation showed 3 subjects fraudulently occupied an Air BNB house and detained them.  During a search , officers located large amounts of narcotics, cash, and a firearm.

Title: Assault
Summary:
On 02/23/26, Sand Creek Division officers responded to a residence in the 300 block of University Drive for a report of a shooting. Officers learned that the residents, a young couple and their 5-year-old daughter, were awoken by an unknown female suspect pounding on the door. The suspect attempted to force her way into the residence while making statements about wanting to find someone who did not reside there. The homeowner was able to force the door closed and armed himself with a handgun to protect his family. Multiple gunshots were then shot into the apartment, narrowly missing the homeowner and into a wall opposite, where, on the other side, the 5-year-old was sleeping. The homeowner returned fire in the direction of the suspect, but no one was hit. The homeowner was able to provide a description of a suspect vehicle, along with a male standing nearby when the shots were fired. 
Approximately 14 minutes after the call was received, an officer observed a vehicle matching the description in the area of E Boulder St and Pitkin St. A traffic stop was conducted, and a male and female matching the suspect descriptions were contacted. Items related to the shooting investigation, along with a large amount of narcotics, were located inside the vehicle. Crystal Burke (33 years old) and Tristan Price (40 years old) were taken into custody and charged with multiple counts of attempted murder, weapons and narcotic violations.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On February 20, 2026, officers were dispatched to the 6700 block of Sleeping Giant Drive for a reported shooting.  Upon arrival, officers learned a male discharged a firearm in a home and was possibly injured. Officers set up containment and began communicating with the male.  The male surrendered peacefully to officers.  The male was charged with felony crimes to include weapons offenses.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 02/19/26 at approx. 1703hrs CDSPD received a call regarding a vehicle that had crashed into Prospect Lake. Upon CSPD/CSFD arrival a witness reported a child was in the vehicle that was completely submerged. Ofc's, and fire personnel immediately entered the freezing water to rescue any victims. Ultimately, only the driver was located and pulled to shore and was transported to Memo Central Hospital where he remains in critical condition.  Investigation determined the driver was the only occupant of the vehicle and there was not a child involved in the crash. The two officers involved, and two firefighters were also transported to the hospital due to hypothermia and other injuries.

Title: Arrest

Officers made contact with the vehicle occupants. Officers took the male driver into custody for felony narcotics and felony weapons violations. The female passenger was taken into custody for multiple warrants. A search of the vehicle led to discovering evidence that led to both individual's arrest for a burglary that occurred a week prior. Officers discovered evidence of a retail theft pattern during the investigation.

Title: Warrant
Summary:

Title: Assault

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 2/15/2026 at approximately 12:40am, Officers responded to the 7700 Block of Crestone Peak Trl in response to a domestic disturbance call for service. Upon arrival information was obtained that the reporting party was locked in their bedroom and the male suspect was attempting to enter. Officers made multiple announcements and observed movement inside the home when the suspect then turned out the lights and began to barricade the front door. Officers continued announcements and eventually forced entry into the residence due to the safety concerns of others inside. The suspect was contacted and subsequently arrested for felony menacing. It was discovered the male had taken a wooden baseball bat and threatened two females inside the residence. Title: Assault
Summary: On 02/15/2026 at 12:46AM the communications center received a report of a possible shooting in the area of Galley Rd. and N. Murray Blvd. Two reporting parties advised that a male party was in the roadway bleeding. Several officers responded to the area and located a male near Maxwell Rd. and N. Murray Blvd. who had multiple stab wounds. Investigation revealed that a disturbance inside the apartment escalated and, after the stabbing occurred, one of the injured males fled the scene and was located on Murray Blvd. while the other remained inside. Title: Traffic Crash

Summary: On Sunday 2/15/25 at 0044hrs officers responded to the traffic circle located at S Cascade Ave and S. Tejon St. for a reported single vehicle motorcycle crash. Title: Disturbance
Summary: On Friday 02/13/2026, at 8:30 PM, the public safety communications center received the report of a shooting in the 5000 block of Galloping Goose Way. The female was subsequently booked into the El Paso County Jail.

Title: Robbery
Summary: 0n 2/12/26 at 2110 hours Officers were dispatched to the 1800 block of N. Circle Dr in response to a robbery. Title: Arrest
Summary: On Sunday February 8th, 2026, at approximately 2:12 PM officers from the Sand Creek Division were dispatched to 600 N Murray Blvd. Walmart to assist an extra duty officer who was in need of assistance. Officer Ryan Valdez attempted to detain a suspect for shoplifting and the suspect fled from him. Officer Valdez gave chase and the suspect first ran into the store and then outside of the store. Officer Valdez was able to grab outside the front door. The suspect began to resist and assaulted the officer causing minor injury. Officer Valdez was assisted by two bystanders and took the suspect into custody. Officer Valdez received only minor scrapes and abrasions and was able to return to service. The bystanders who assisted Officer Valdez did not stand by and left the scene before they could be identified.  Mr. Arnaud was charged with Assault on a Peace Officer, Resisting Arrest and Shoplifting.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 02/08/2026 at approximately 0149 hours, a police officer was operating a marked cruiser. He was parked on the right curb on Canyon Wren Ln., west of Miller Rd., facing eastbound. The cruiser was stationary, as the officer sat in the driver seat. A Black Chevy Silverado pickup was northbound on Miller Rd., and executed a left turn to westbound Canyon Wren Ln. The Silverado failed to properly execute the turn, entered the eastbound side of the roadway and struck the front of push bumper of the police cruiser in a head on collision. Both vehicles suffered moderate damage to the front end. The collision was low speed, however the officer suffered minor injuries and sought treatment at a area hospital. A DUI investigation occurred. The driver of the Silverado, 24yo Bredin Perez Ramos, was arrested for DUI and several other traffic charges.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On 02/06/2026 at 01:46pm CSPD dispatch received a call for an injury traffic crash involving a motorcycle and a pickup truck in the 1400 block of S Academy Blvd. Officers responded and learned that the pickup was crossing S Academy Blvd at an intersection when it was T-boned by a motorcycle. Despite life-saving measures provided by officers and medical personnel, the motorcycle rider succumbed to his injuries on scene. The driver of the pickup truck was taken to a local hospital with minor injuries. Due to the severity of the crash, detectives from the Major Crash Team responded and took over the investigation. Speed is considered as a possible factor in the investigation.

Title: TEU - K9 Activity
Summary:
On February 6, 2026, at approximately 0900 hours, Detectives with the State of Colorado Parole Fugitive Apprehension Unit obtained information that a fugitive, wanted on a felony escape arrest warrant issued by the Colorado Department of Corrections Fugitive Extradition Unit, was in Colorado Springs. The Fremont County Sheriff’s Office further reported that Capek was unlawfully in possession of an AR-15 rifle and had made threats to shoot law enforcement officers if they attempted to arrest him.
Detectives located Capek near Garden of the Gods and Interstate 25 and began surveillance. Capek eventually parked in the west lot of Walmart at 8250 Razorback Road. Detectives requested assistance from the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) Tactical Enforcement Unit (TEU) to safely apprehend Capek. CSPD immediately responded with three tactical teams (two officers per team) and K9 Riggs. Additionally, the Colorado State Patrol Aircraft Unit provided aerial support to maintain visual contact.
Detectives, a CSPD Extra Duty Officer, and store security worked together to protect the business and ensure the safety of citizens while TEU prepared to intervene. Once the area was clear of civilians, TEU deployed specially equipped vehicles to pin Capek’s vehicle and used a vehicle PA system to announce that he was under arrest. Capek refused commands and attempted to escape by accelerating his vehicle, causing it to smoke. Officers warned Capek that chemical munitions would be deployed if he did not surrender. After repeated warnings and increased risk of fire, TEU introduced OC pepper powder into the vehicle. Capek exited and was confronted by K9 Riggs, at which point he complied with all commands.
Medical personnel treated Capek for OC exposure before he was turned over to the State of Colorado Parole Fugitive Apprehension Unit. No officers or citizens were injured during the operation. Ammunition designed to defeat bullet proof vests was located by Detectives in the vehicle.
This incident highlights CSPD’s commitment to public safety and its responsiveness in assisting outside law enforcement partners. By working collaboratively with state and local agencies, CSPD ensured a safe resolution to a high-risk situation, reinforcing its dedication to protecting the citizens of Colorado Springs.

Title: Arrest
Location: Zeppelin Road and S. Powers Blvd
Summary: On February 5, 2026, detectives from the Colorado Springs Police Department’s Metro Division Theft and Intel Units concluded an investigation into a prolific offender involved with identity theft, theft, burglary of motor vehicles, mail theft, and narcotics. Detectives learned of her location, and she was taken into custody without incident in the area of Zeppelin Road and S. Powers Blvd. As a result of the investigation detectives identified a possible 60 additional victims. Detectives also recovered burglary tools, a forged mailbox key, narcotics, and drug paraphernalia.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 2/5/2025 at approximately 1500 hours a Colorado Springs Police officer was working an extra duty shift at a local grocery store. A Loss prevention member of the store identified a suspect who was stealing and has stolen from the business previously. The Officer approached the suspect to detain him. The suspect attempted to flee from the Officer. The Officer was able to take him to the ground. The suspect continued to resist arrest and attempted to flee.  The suspect used force against the officer, striking him in an attempt to escape. The officer sustained minor injuries and was checked by medical personnel on scene. The officer was able to continue working. The suspect was booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center.

Title: Death
Summary: On February 5, 2026, at 4:50PM the Colorado Springs Police Department Communications Center received a call of a shooting in 2500 block of Gunnison St.  Upon arrival officers located a deceased male.  At this time, all involved parties are accounted for.  This remains an active and ongoing investigation, and anyone with information is asked to call the Colorado Springs Police Department at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you may call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 01/17/2026, a CSPD officer responded to a burglary of a storage unit at a commercial storage facility in Colorado Springs. The officer gleaned a preliminary suspect description during the initial investigation and conferred with members of CSPD’s Burglary Unit to further develop that information. Burglary unit detectives identified the suspect as a prolific offender and ComCor escapee with several outstanding felony and misdemeanor warrants. On 01/30/2026, the adult male offender was taken into custody, and a search warrant was executed at several addresses in Colorado Springs. Property from numerous storage unit burglaries (valued at more than $10,000) was recovered and subsequently returned to victims.

Title: Warrant
Summary:
On February 4, 2026, at approximately 9:00 AM, detectives from the Colorado Springs Police Department’s Metro Division Strategic Investigations Unit executed search warrants at residences located in the 6700 block of Mission Road and the 4100 block of Kayak Court. The operation was conducted with assistance from the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office SWAT Team, the Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force, and the Real Time Crime Center.
The warrants were related to an ongoing burglary and weapons investigation involving a residential break-in by two armed suspects. As a result of the investigation and subsequent search warrant service, detectives arrested two juveniles and one adult on multiple felony charges connected to the case. Investigators recovered a stolen handgun, a shotgun, and a unserialized “ghost” handgun capable of firing live ammunition.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
***Update to ETACS 35943 Dated January 31, 2026***

On January 31, 2026, at 5:06 PM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a 911 call from the 4100 block of Lacy Lane. Dispatchers were connected with a juvenile who reported that their parent had been shot in the parking lot.
Officers from the Sand Creek Division arrived and located the victim inside a vehicle with gunshot injuries. Members of the Colorado Springs Fire Department and American Medical Response provided immediate medical aid, and the victim was transported by ambulance to a local hospital.

On February 2, 2026, Assault Detectives assumed the investigation and quickly identified a suspect. On February 5, 2026, at approximately 8:00 AM, Detectives with the Assault Unit, SIU, AVOU, and RTCC located and arrested 24‑year‑old Colorado Springs resident Zackrey Snell. Snell was booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center on charges of Attempted Murder, Assault in the First Degree, Prohibited Use of Weapons, and Child Abuse. The investigation remains ongoing.

Title: TEU - K9 Activity
Summary:
On February 4, 2026, at approximately 7:00 p.m., patrol officers from the Stetson Hills Subdivision, along with CSPD K9 officers, responded to the area of Barnes Road and Marksheffel Road regarding a reported domestic disturbance. The reporting party stated that the suspect was armed with a handgun and had a rifle in his vehicle.
K9 officers recognized the suspect’s name as an individual with an active felony arrest warrant related to a prior violent domestic violence incident. They also knew the suspect was currently being sought by the CSPD/EPSO Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force (VOFTF). Detectives from VOFTF coordinated with the Metro Division Motor Vehicle Task Force (MVT) to assist with the patrol response.
Detectives located the suspect leaving the victim’s residence and maintained surveillance for several hours as he drove throughout the city. Around 10:00 p.m., the suspect began circling the area near the victim’s home. Detectives then contacted officers from the CSPD Tactical Enforcement Unit (TEU) for assistance in apprehending the suspect.
At approximately 11:00 p.m., detectives and TEU officers successfully stopped and pinned the suspect’s vehicle in the southbound lane of Marksheffel Road, just north of Barnes Road. The suspect was taken into custody without injury and transported to the Criminal Justice Center (CJC).
This incident demonstrates excellent collaboration between CSPD’s Patrol, Investigations, Metro, and Specialized Enforcement divisions in safely apprehending an armed and violent fugitive in the Colorado Springs area.
The suspect’s name is being withheld at this time due to the ongoing investigation.

Title: Disturbance
Summary:
On 2/3/26, between 9:00PM and 2:30AM, CSPD received multiple calls regarding domestic violence related criminal mischief and menacing. the suspect, Isaiah Salazar, repeatedly returned to his ex girlfriends residence to damage vehicles and at one point charged her with an unknown object in his hand.  

The suspect was located and immediately became non compliant and a taser was used to take him into custody for domestic violence, felony criminal mischief, menacing, and obstruction.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On 2/3/2026 at approx. 1648 hours, a subject in CSPD custody on felony charges, and being seen at Penrose Hospital, escaped custody by breaking through a restroom ceiling and running from the hospital premises.
Officers in the area were flagged down by a citizen who stated the suspect had broken into his house and, when confronted, continued to run south away from the hospital.
Falcon patrol units, CSPD K9, and an RTCC drone quickly set up containment in the area.  
Another citizen, who lived within the containment area, called in to the Communication Center and reported someone had broken into her back yard and was at that time hiding in her covered hot tub.
The suspect peacefully surrendered to officers with the assistance of K9.

Title: Other
Summary:
*** Update as of 11:46 AM on 2/3/26 ****

The officer has been released from the hospital. The suspect remains in critical but stable condition

**************************

Today at about 1:30 pm, the CSPD Tactical Enforcement Unit and the Colorado Parole Fugitive Apprehension Unit were in the area of East Bijou Street and Balfour Avenue conducting a fugitive apprehension operation. After the operation concluded, uniformed CSPD officers attempted to contact a suspicious male in the same area. The male ran away, produced a handgun, and shot a uniformed CSPD officer.
Two uniformed CSPD officers then returned fire, striking the suspect. Emergency medical aid was rendered to both the officer and the suspect, who were transported to an area hospital.
The officer sustained a serious but non-life-threatening gunshot injury. The suspect is in critical condition.
CSPD investigators recovered a firearm on scene. The identity of the injured officer and the suspect are not being released at this time.
Under Colorado state law, the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office will assume responsibility for the investigation. CSPD will release a significant event briefing video within 21 days.

Title: Other
