The subject had possible serious injuries and was transported to the hospital. Railroad traffic was stopped while the incident was reviewed by BNSF Railway and police. No other person/property was damaged. Subject was cited for trespassing.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Location: HWY 115 and E. Cheyenne Mountain Blvd
Summary: On February 2, 2026, at approximately 2:03 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a head-on collision on HWY 115 south of E. Cheyenne Mountain Blvd.  When emergency services arrived on scene, they discovered two deceased.  Due to the nature of the collision, the CSPD Major Crash Team assumed the investigation.  The initial investigation indicates a vehicle was southbound on HWY 115, crossed over into northbound traffic, striking a second vehicle head-on, killing both parties. A third vehicle collided, and that driver was transported to a local hospital with minor injuries.  At this time, speed and alcohol do not appear to be factors.  HWY 115 northbound will be closed for several hours while detectives investigate and process the scene.

Title: Other
Summary:
************Update***********
As of 02/02/2026 at 6:35 PM, CSPD is no longer on Priority Dispatch


The Colorado Springs Police Department is on priority dispatch as of 2:35 PM. Citizens with emergencies should call 911; citizens with non-emergency issues should wait to call CSPD until we return to normal operations. Drivers are reminded that if they are involved in a traffic accident with no fatality or injury requiring medical attention, no driver involved is suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, and all drivers are present with license, registration, and insurance information, drivers should exchange their information and follow the procedure for completing a counter report. When damage to any vehicle requires a wrecker, counter reports can still be made, provided the previous criteria are met. Drivers can either pick up a traffic accident report form at any of the police department substations or they can go online at Springsgov.com, go to the CSPD homepage, and select “report a minor traffic accident” for instructions.

Title: Other
Location: Mesa Avenue at Cache La Poudre Street
Summary: On February 1, 2026,  at 7:34PM,  BNSF Railroad reported a train hit a pedestrian at Mesa Road and Cache La Poudre Street.   AMR and CSFD arrived on scene and located the pedestrian who was alive, with serious injuries, who was transported to an area hospital for medical care.   Railroad traffic was stopped while the incident was reviewed by railroad and police.  The area is clearly posted no trespassing.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 02/01/26 just before 6:00AM Sandcreek patrol officers responded to an address in the 2600 block of Verde Dr. for a reported disturbance. Upon arrival, officers learned that one of the parties on scene sustained wounds consistent with being stabbed. It was determined that the victim was stabbed several hours earlier in the parking lot of a business in the 500 block of South Circle Dr. The victim was transported to a local hospital to be treated for his injuries. The victim's injuries were not life- threatening. No suspects have been identified at this time.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 2/1/2026 at approximately 0320 hours, Colorado Springs Police Department patrol officers were dispatched to a business in the 700 block of Cheyenne Meadows Rd. to investigate a reported armed robbery. Upon their arrival, officers learned an unidentified male suspect confronted the business employee with a firearm and stole an undisclosed amount of cash prior to fleeing from the area on foot. Officers searched the surrounding area but were unable to locate the suspect.

Title: Other
Summary: On Saturday, January 31, 2026, at about 10 PM, CSPD received a call for service for a disturbance involving a male reportedly chasing a female. One of the parties reportedly had a rifle. When officers arrived on scene, they contacted a female who matched the description of the female who was reportedly involved in the disturbance. She was armed with a knife and began running from officers and refusing their commands to stop. The female continued running and threw the knife, as officers continued to pursue her on foot. The female then got into the front seat of a CSPD cruiser and continued to refuse commands to exit the vehicle. As officers were trying to remove her from the car, she reached for the duty shotgun in the vehicle. At that time, one officer fired at least one shot, striking the female. Officers began rendering medical aid until EMS arrived and transported her to a local hospital with at least one gunshot wound. At last check, she was in serious but stable condition and is expected to survive her injuries. In accordance with Colorado Law, the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office is the lead investigative agency for the officer-involved shooting, and all investigative updates will come from them. Within 21 days, CSPD will release a significant event briefing video that includes body-worn-camera footage and other releasable facts about the case.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 01/31/26 at 5:06PM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a 911 call from someone in the 4100 block of Lacy Lane. They were contacted by a juvenile who told them their parent had been shot in the parking lot. When Officers from the Sand Creek Division arrived they located the victim in a vehicle, with gunshot injuries. Members of the Colorado Springs Fire Department and American Medical Response arrived promptly and rendered aid. The victim was transported by ambulance to a local hospital. The suspect is currently being searched for, and is reportedly unknown to the victim.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On Friday 1/30/2026 at approximately 2117 hours, the Colorado Springs Police Department received a report of a two-vehicle traffic crash in the intersection of N. Academy Blvd. and Agora Pt. It was determined that a silver Subaru SUV made a left turn in front of traffic traveling northbound and collided with a silver Honda SUV. Northbound N. Academy Blvd. was shut down for several hours because of the crash. Occupants of the Subaru were transported to Children's Hospital and Penrose Main Hospital with serious injuries related to the accident. Neither speed nor impairment appear to be a factor in this crash.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On Friday 1/30/2026 at approximately 2117 hours, the Colorado Springs Police Department received a report of a two-vehicle traffic crash in the intersection of N. Academy Blvd. and Agora Pt. It was determined that a silver Subaru SUV made a left turn in front of traffic traveling northbound and collided with a silver Honda SUV. Northbound N. Academy Blvd. was shut down for several hours because of the crash. Occupants of the Subaru were transported to Children's Hospital and Penrose Main Hospital with serious injuries related to the accident. Neither speed nor impairment appear to be a factor in this crash.

Title: Burglary
Summary:
On January 30, 2026, at 9:15 PM, the public safety communications center received the report of a burglary in-progress at a commercial business in the 1800 block of Palmer Park Blvd. Officers responded and determined a gray Kia Soul was used to crash through the business's steel overhead rollup door. The perpetrators stole two motorcycles and rode away on them fleeing the scene. 

The Kia Soul used in the commission of the burglary appeared to have been stolen during an earlier motor vehicle theft at the Kingsborough Apartments which occurred at 8:42 PM.

Title: Other
Summary: On 1/29/2026, at approximately 4:00 PM, members of Stetson Hills Patrol and the Regional Explosives Unit (REU) were alerted that missing person, Brandon Hugney, 22 years old, was in the area of the 1500 block of Space Center Dr. Hugney had been arrested for several arson cases in the recent months.  CSPD learned that there was a fire intentionally set in the 1500 block of Space Center Dr. At approximately 5:16PM while detectives were in the area searching for Hugney, a second fire was set in the Sand Creek Drainage near Galley Rd and Space Center Dr. Hugney was located approximately 300 feet from the second fire about one minute later.  After investigation of both fires, Hugney was charged with two counts of Firing Woods and Prairies.  He was booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center.

Title: Select Incident Type
Summary:
On 12/17/2025 at approximately  1747 hours, CSPD Stetson Hills Division officers were dispatched to a business in the 2800 block of N. Powers Blvd for a shots fired call for service. Initial investigation revealed a male and female suspect were shoplifting from a local business and the male suspect fired a handgun at a Victim inside their vehicle who had confronted them in the parking lot. The suspect fled the scene. The investigation was assumed by CSPD Armed Violent Offender Detectives. 

On 01/27/2026, the male suspect identified as Anthony Lopez was arrested in the 900 block of N. Circle Drive. A search of the vehicle the suspect was apprehended in resulted with the discovery of a handgun with an illegal extended magazine.  

The suspect was booked into the El Paso County Jail for Attempted Murder and weapons related charges as a result of this investigation. Mr. Lopez was determined to be a previously convicted felon who was actively out on bond for felony explosives-related charges at the time of his arrest.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Wednesday, January 28th, 2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a stolen red 2014 Dodge Grand Caravan which was taken from the1600 block of East Cheyenne Mountain Boulevard. As the day progressed the following activities occurred. 
Detectives from the CSPD Retail Theft Unit were actively investigating Steven Smith age 67, who is a prolific criminal offender. Retail Theft Detectives contacted the Colorado Organized Retail Crime Alliance group (CO/ORCA) and were notified Pueblo Police Department detectives were investigating Smith as he was currently stealing different items from retail stores in Pueblo. 
CSPD MVT detectives were notified of the stolen red caravan in the 3200 block of East Platte Avenue. During surveillance, MVT detectives observed Smith remove a temporary license plate from a vehicle and place it on the stolen red van. Detectives followed Smith as he drove the stolen vehicle to the 2500 blk of East Boulder Street. As detectives watch Smith, Smith was trying to sell the stolen merchandise he took from Pueblo stores to individuals in the area. Detectives contacted Smith and later transported him to the Criminal Justice Center, charging him with MVT and Theft. Detectives learned Smith is a statutory motor vehicle theft prolific offender.  All the stolen merchandise was recovered and returned to the owners. This was a great cooperative effort between multiple units with CSPD and multiple jurisdictions up and down the Front Range, holding Smith accountable for his criminal behavior.

Title: Select Incident Type
Location: 123 I-25; NORTH OF PPIR EXIT - I-25
Summary:
(El Paso County, CO) - At approximately 3:45 am, Colorado State Troopers responded to a wrong-way driver traveling southbound in the northbound lanes on Interstate 25 near mile marker 113. Troopers located the vehicle near mile marker 117. A trooper attempted to stop the driver at this location, but the driver did not initially pull over. Troopers stopped the vehicle near mile marker 123. Based on the dangerous driver's actions, a secondary Trooper attempted to make a u-turn in his patrol car in front of the driver in an attempt to perform a vehicle block. The trooper was struck by a northbound vehicle in the left lane of I-25. The driver of that vehicle was transported to a local hospital with an unknown extent of injuries. The trooper was also transported for medical evaluation.

The Colorado Springs Police Department Major Crash Team was notified and responded to assume the investigation of the crash. The left northbound lane of I-25 was closed for a short period while the investigation was completed. 

Driver impairments are unknown at this time and this is an ongoing investigation. Please contact the CSPD Public Information Office for further information.

Title: Warrant
Location: 1600 Block of Howard Ave & 6000 Block of Dancing Sun Way
Summary:
On January 28, 2026, at approximately 7:45 a.m., Detectives from the Metro Division’s Strategic Investigations Unit and Robbery Unit, in coordination with the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office and supported by the CSPD Real Time Crime Center, executed search warrants at two locations: the 1600 block of Howard Avenue and the 6000 block of Dancing Sun Way.
To enhance officer and occupant safety, the drone unit was deployed to assist with clearing the interior of the Howard Avenue residence.
As a result of the search warrants, detectives recovered a sawed-off shotgun and four handguns, one of which had been reported stolen.
Two individuals were taken into custody:
•	Tony Westfall (20) 
•	Joshua Palmer (20) 
Both subjects were booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center on weapons related charges.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 01/27/26 Detectives with CSPD RPM Unit were contacted by an area retailer in reference to a male who had pulled a knife on the store Asset Protection and soon after about the suspect returning and committing a theft from the location. RPM responded to take the report and believing the suspect was likely still in the general area, worked with the Falcon patrol officers that work the area and provided photographs of the suspect. The retail store later contacted RPM again that the suspect had returned for the third time, shoplifted and was currently walking south, away from the store. This information, along with an updated photograph was provided to patrol. Patrol officers that were finishing an unrelated call in the general area quickly spotted the suspect at the corner of Mount View Ln and Nevada Ave. The suspect was contacted and taken into custody without incident. The item stolen in the third incident was recovered.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 1/27/2026 officers with the Colorado Springs Police Department responded to the 2800 block of Flintridge Dr. for reports of a male firing a gun in front of a residence. Upon arrival on scene officers received another call, stating the suspect was nearby at a related residence in the 2500 block of Ranch Lane. A short time later officers with the Sand Creek Division, located and stopped the suspect vehicle. The suspect and a firearm were located in the vehicle. The investigation revealed a vehicle and an occupied residence both sustained damage from gunfire during this incident, no citizens were injured. The suspect, Armando Malacara-Colon, was taken into custody for multiple felony weapon offenses and domestic violence related charges.

Title: Other
Summary: Officers were dispatched to a Stormwater drainage tunnel to assist with a suicidal party.  Officers arrived, contacted the suicidal party, developed a rapport, and convinced her to come out of the tunnel. The female was taken to a local area hospital for treatment.

Title: Assault
Summary: On January 26, 2026, at approximately 2300 hours, officers conducted a traffic stop in the 500 block of North Murray Boulevard.  During the traffic stop, the driver was removed from the vehicle for further investigation.  Officers then observed the rear passenger of the vehicle, identified as 32-year-old Anthony Lofton, moving to the driver’s seat.  When contacted, Lofton attempted to drive off.  Two officers were briefly dragged several feet before subduing Lofton.  Both officers received injuries related to this incident.  Lofton was arrested for 1st Degree Assault on a Peace Officer and found to have active arrest warrants to include an arrest warrant for Assault on a Peace Officer.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 1/26/2026 CSPD received a report of a two-vehicle traffic crash in the intersection of Powers Blvd and Palmer Park Blvd. Southbound Powers Blvd was shut down for several hours as a result of the crash. Occupants of the second vehicle were transported to Children's Hospital with injuries from the crash. A driver was taken into custody for vehicular assault, reckless driving, DUI, and a weapons related offense.

Title: Cold Accident Reporting
Summary: CSPD is no longer on accident alert.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 01/24/2026 @ 1553 hours, an officer and his PTO with the Falcon Patrol Division were backing up in a private parking lot in the 7100 block of Commerce Center Drive. As the officer was backing out of a parking spot a large Ford 250 Crew Cab Pickup Truck was backing into the parking spot to the right of them simultaneously. The officer stated as he heard the warning tones, he noted the large pickup truck to his right and then the sound of the contact. CSPD Vehicle #2318 sustained minor damage to the right lower quarter panel / rear bumper. The citizen’s vehicle sustained minor damage to the passenger side rear crew cab lower door. Both vehicles are operational and the damage is considered to be only cosmetic.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 1/25/2026 at approximately 0314 hours, Officer Gonzalez 7916 and Officer Mitchell 7785 were dispatched to a family disturbance.  Upon arriving on scene Officers established misdemeanor charges for two intoxicated adult children assaulting their father.   Upon attempting to detain the female party she kicked both officers multiple times causing injury.  The female continued to actively resist officers during their contact.  Officer Wallace arrived to assist and the female was detained.  At the hospital the female spit and kicked officers.  The female was later transported to the EPSO CJC. The male was cooperative and served/released on scene.

Title: Cold Accident Reporting
Summary: The Colorado Springs Police Department is on Crash Alert status as of 1/23/2026 at 11:30 AM due to road conditions. Drivers are reminded if they are involved in a traffic accident with no fatality or injury requiring medical attention, no driver involved is suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, all drivers are present with license, registration and insurance information, and no damage to public property such as a street sign or utility pole has occurred, drivers should exchange their information and follow the procedure for completing a counter (cold) report. When damage to any vehicle requires a wrecker, a counter report can still be made providing the previous criteria is met. Drivers can either pick up a traffic accident report form at any of the police department substations or they can go online at coloradosprings.gov/police, click on “Online Reporting” and select “Cold Reporting: Report a Minor Traffic Accident” for instructions. Drivers should also be reminded to take the appropriate steps to avoid being involved in a traffic accident during adverse weather and road conditions. Drivers should check road and travel conditions before heading out and leave more time to get to their destination. Drivers need to travel at a safe speed for the conditions and leave an increased and safe following distance from the vehicle in front of them. Drivers should use streets and roadways less hazardous in winter driving conditions. Drivers also need to remove snow and ice obstructing their vision and have adequate snow tires on their vehicles

Title: Arrest
Summary:
Pursuant to an ongoing investigation undertaken by detectives with CSPD’s Metro Property Section, an adult male was taken into custody on the evening of 01/21/2026. The male was the subject of an investigation into the theft of multiple motor vehicles and trailers in the Front Range area. CSPD Investigators observed the suspect driving a stolen vehicle on I-25 and attempted a traffic stop. The suspect eluded officers in the vehicle but was later located in Pueblo by detectives from CSPD’s Motor Vehicle Theft Unit and taken into custody by Pueblo Police Department officers.

Investigators from CSPD’s Metro Burglary Unit then seized two storage trailers believed to have been stolen by the offender and executed a search warrant at a storage unit in Colorado Springs. During the execution of the warrant, detectives recovered two stolen ATV’s, two stolen motorcycles, and other stolen property. The suspect was booked into the Pueblo County Detention Center on the strength of outstanding warrants, and new charges were filed related to motor vehicle theft, vehicular eluding, and possession of a controlled substance.

Title: Other
Summary: At approximately 8:51 PM, Colorado Springs Police Department officers were notified of a vehicle fire in the parking lot of a business in the 700 block of S. 8th Street. Officers responded and found a vehicle fully engulfed in flames. Colorado Springs Fire Department responded and extinguished the fire. Officers discovered a space heater was being used to heat the interior of the van while the vehicle was in motion. The space heater fell over which caused the interior of the van to ignite. The occupant of the vehicle was able to exit the car. No one was injured and the damage was contained to the single vehicle. The vehicle sustained significant damage.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 12/13/2025 a bank located in the 1800 block of S. Nevada Ave was robbed by a single male, who presented a demand note and threatened having a weapon.  The suspect fled the scene with an undisclosed amount of cash.  The same suspect tried to rob the bank again on 1/3/2026, but this time was unsuccessful in obtaining money and fled the scene prior to police arrival.  The Colorado Springs Police Department Robbery Unit took lead on the investigations and on 1/21/2026, an arrest warrant was obtained for 31-year-old male, Joshua Criswell.  Criswell was arrested by Robbery detectives without incident in the 600 block of S. Nevada Ave.

Title: Other
Summary: On January 20, 2026, at 9:03PM, CSPD responded to assist on a suicidal subject in the 1800 block of Old Stage Road.  The subject had cut his wrists and the other family members were locked in another room.  The subject fled in a vehicle prior to police arrival.  Officers checked the area for the subject and located his vehicle at 9:48PM.  Officers confirmed the subject had cut his wrists and when he was advised he was detained, he fled in his vehicle.  Officers obtained an arrest warrant for eluding.  At 1:01AM, an officer located the vehicle at Lower Gold Camp Road and Millstream Terrace.  The subject stopped, but as commands were issued the subject fled.  A few minutes later officers located the vehicle stopped the subject out of his vehicle the subject was cutting himself, causing serious injury.  Officers used less lethal force to gain control of the subject as he was causing himself serious harm.  Officers rendered emergency medical aid until medical arrived.  The subject was transported to an area hospital and was admitted for treatment.

Title: Other
Summary:
On Tuesday, January 20, at about 2:43, CSPD received a call for service into the 9-1-1 communication center of a kidnapping and a stolen 2021 black Kia Seltos, from an apartment in the 2700 block of King Street. A 2-year-old child, Giovani Salazar-President, was in the back seat of the vehicle when it was stolen. As officers responded to the scene, the RTCC (Real Time Crime Center) immediately deployed resources, including the drone as a first responder (DFR) and monitoring CCTV cameras, red light cameras, automatic license plate recognition (ALPR) cameras, and live feeds from officers BWC’s to try to locate Giovani and the stolen Kia. Officers from across nearly every division in the department responded to the call and immediately began investigating, interviewing witnesses, and searching the immediate area and the city. The RTCC was able to send a photo of Giovani to all responding officers, as well as an image obtained from an ALPR camera of the stolen Kia Seltos, revealing the vehicle’s last known location, helping officers focus their search efforts in real time.  Just before 5 PM, officers from CSPD's Tactical Enforcement Unit, who were searching for the vehicle, came across the vehicle and found Giovani still in the back seat in his carseat and unharmed. The suspect had abandoned the vehicle and was no longer at that location. CSPD is continuing to investigate this incident, develop potential suspect information, and their possible whereabouts. Anyone with information that can help this investigation; to include anyone in the area of the 2700 block of King Street who may have home security video that captured anything unusual they are willing to share, is asked to contact CSPD at 719-444-7000.

Any updates will come from the CSPD PIO office

Title: Other
Summary: CSPD & CSFD personnel responded to the 2500 block of E. Dale St. for reports of an explosion. CSPD Shift I & Shift II personnel located the source, which was an outbuilding that quickly became fully engulfed by fire. No injuries were reported. Police officers helped a resident to safety and evacuated residents of neighboring houses. A drone was deployed for public & responder safety, general surveillance, & for the identification of any unknown hotspots. CSFD extinguished the fire before it spread beyond the involved address & no residences were burned. CSFD investigators are still on scene and police personnel will re-open the 2500 block once all fire apparatus and personnel are clear. No criminal investigation or proceedings are anticipated.

Title: TEU - K9 Activity
Summary:
On 01/18/2026 at 0211 hours, CSPD received a report of a domestic disturbance at a residence in the 2000 block of Whitman Rd. Officers responded and contacted a victim outside the home who had non-life-threatening injuries. The victim reported that she had been assaulted by a male suspect, who was inside the home. During the investigation, the male suspect briefly exited the residence, but then returned inside. Officers communicated with the suspect over the course of several hours, but he refused to exit. The Tactical Enforcement Unit was notified and has responded to assist with apprehension of the suspect, who is wanted for felony assault charges related to the incident. 

**** Update   Suspect was taken into custody*****

Title: Disturbance
Location: 500 block of S. El Paso St.
Summary: 01/18/2026 at approximately 3:25 a.m., officers from the Gold Hill Division responded to a reported disturbance in the 500 block of S. El Paso St. The investigation determined the incident stemmed from an ongoing dispute that began at a downtown bar. Upon arrival, officers located evidence indicating a firearm had been discharged multiple times; however, no injuries were reported. All known individuals involved have been identified, and the investigation remains ongoing. No arrests have been made at this time.

Title: Burglary
Summary: On January 16, 2026, at 5:23 PM, the public safety communications received the report of a home invasion in the 4500 block of Gold Medal Pt. The RP/victim stated a male suspect forced entry into the residence and tried to stab the victim with a butcher knife. Officers responded and located a male fitting the suspect's description and detained him. Witness statements and a field identification confirmed the detained male as the suspect. The suspect was taken into custody on an active felony warrant and multiple charges stemming from this incident including first degree burglary, menacing, and weapon offenses. The suspect was booked into the El Paso County Jail.

Title: Arrest
Location: N. Powers Blvd and Constitution Ave
Summary: On 01/17/2026 at approximately 1205 hours, Stetson Hills Division patrol officers were dispatched to Powers Boulevard and Constitution Avenue reference a male passed out in his vehicle.    Upon the officers’ arrival, they located the vehicle and male stopped in the lanes of traffic facing northbound on Powers Blvd. at Constitution Avenue.   The vehicle was still in drive and officers could not wake up the male.  Officers forced entry into the vehicle via the driver’s side front window.  The male was taken into custody for an unrelated arrest warrant, along with traffic and weapon charges.  A firearm was recovered from the vehicle.  The male was later transported to the EPSO CJC.  Several northbound lanes of traffic were stopped on Powers Blvd. for approximately 90 minutes.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On January 17, 2026, at approximately 2:03 AM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a single-vehicle collision on Galley Road and Wooten Road. Emergency personnel arrived on scene and located the driver, who had sustained serious injuries.  The driver was rushed to a local hospital and is in critical condition.  Based on the circumstances, the CSPD Major Crash Team responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation. The initial investigation indicates the vehicle was traveling westbound on Galley Road and departed the roadway to the north, striking a fixed object. At this time, impairment is suspected, and speed is still being investigated. The intersection of Galley Road and Wooten Road was closed for several hours while investigators processed the scene.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On January 16, 2026, officers were dispatched to the 3300 block of Bunker Hill Dr for a reported family disturbance.  Upon arrival, officers learned a male had damaged the property of family members. The male jumped out the back window and fled from officers into the neighborhood.  Approximately two hours later, the male returned to the residence.  The male refused to follow officer’s commands but was eventually taken into custody.  The male was charged with felony criminal mischief.

Title: Robbery
Summary: Officers of the Sand Creek Divsion were dispatched to a Robbery involving a weapon at a business in the 2700 blk of Janitell Rd. An employee at the business observed 2 suspects stealing items in the store. When confronted by the employee, the suspects both produced weapons. The suspects left the area and were not located by officers.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On January 16, 2026, at approximately 4:14 AM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a vehicle off the roadway with debris in the roadway north of the intersection of Marksheffel Road and Drennan Road. Emergency personnel arrived on scene and located the driver, who had been ejected and was deceased.  Based on the circumstances, the CSPD Major Crash Team responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation. The initial investigation indicates the vehicle was traveling southbound on Marksheffel Road and drifted off the west side of the road.  The vehicle rolled at least once, and the driver was ejected.  At this time, impairment is not suspected, although speed does appear to be a contributing factor. Marksheffel Road was closed for several hours while investigators processed the scene.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On 1/15/2026 at approximately 1110 hours, Officer D. Mork was driving in the area of E. Fountain Bl/S. Circle Dr when he observed two motorcycles traveling westbound on E. Fountain Bl. At least one of the motorcycles was a dirt bike that did not have an affixed license plate.
The Colorado Springs Police Department’s Real Time Crime Center was notified for drone and camera support.  The Colorado State Patrol surveillance airplane (AIR-1) was also requested and responded to the intersection to monitor the motorcycle rider’s activity. The plane observed the motorcycles travelling into Memorial Park, where they got off the motorcycles and walked to a nearby picnic table.
As officers approached the riders, one of the riders got up off the table, ran to the dirtbike, and attempted to ride away.  Officers ordered him off the motorcycle.  He dropped the motorcycle and started to run along the shoreline of Prospect Lake.  While he was running, officers observed the handle and magazine of a black semi-auto handgun protruding from the male’s waistband. The male then ran out about 10-20 yards into the lake, squatted down, and then stood back up.  He was ordered out of the lake, to which he complied.  He did not have the weapon on him.
The Colorado Springs Fire Department Dive Team was requested to respond and check the area of the lake where he was observed squatting down. They were able to locate a black Springfield XD-9 9mm handgun.
The suspect was taken to Memorial Hospital, where he was treated for hypothermia.  He was then booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center for Possession of a Weapon by a Previous Offender, Obstruction, and a traffic violation.

Title: Select Incident Type
Summary: On 01/13/26 Officers were dispatched to the intersection of North Circle Drive and Galley Road for a reported auto ped.  When officers arrived on scene and began their investigation, which revealed a dark colored Ford pick-up made a right-hand turn from northbound North Circle Drive to eastbound Galley Road, striking the pedestrian crossing northbound across Galley Road.  Witnesses reported there was no driving behavior change by the vehicle after contact.  The pedestrian suffered serious bodily injury and was transported to a local hospital.  Officer Dasilva and Officer Silva conducted neighborhood follow-up and located the run vehicle.  Officers collected a full confession from the run driver.  The driver was taken into custody and booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center. Alcohol and Speed were not factors.

Title: MVT
Location: South Hancock Avenue and East Costilla Street
Summary:
On Monday December 22nd, 2025, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a motor vehicle theft which occurred in the 5600 block of Barnes Road. A teal 2022 Hyundai Kona was taken without permission.
On Tuesday, January 13, 2026, Detectives of the motor vehicle theft unit conducted surveillance on this Hyundai and attempted to stop the stolen car with a police vehicle; however, the driver Michael Pena’ age 44, fled detectives at a high rate of speed. Detectives continued surveillance and saw Pena’ pull into the alleyway of the 1000 block of East Marino Avenue.  Pena and the passenger, Chelsea Guido age 30, were later seen walking northbound on Hancock Boulevard. Detectives detained both individuals at the intersection of South Hancock Avenue and East Costilla Street. Pena was charged with Motor Vehicle Theft and Felony Eluding. Guido had an outstanding arrest warrant for failure to appear in court for a misdemeanor. Both individuals were later transported to the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center.
Adults Arrested: Michael Pena’ age 44 & Chelsea Guido age 30

Title: Disturbance
Summary:
*** UPDATE: The suspect in this investigation is identified as 25-year-old Jonathan Lee Sovine. Mr. Sovine was arrested at an off-campus location on 1/13/25 at approximately 11 AM. Detectives with CSPD's Assault Unit, Armed Violent Offender Unit, and the Real Time Crime Center worked in collaboration to take Mr. Sovine into custody without incident. He was arrested on Attempted Murder, Attempted Assault in the First Degree, Menacing, and Disorderly Conduct. Mr. Sovine's booking photo will be released on the CSPD X Account. ***

**** UPDATE: As of 01/13/2026 at 11:00AM, the suspect in this investigation is in custody. The suspect's identification is not being released at this time, due to ongoing investigation. ****

On 1/12/2026 at approximately 10:23 pm, multiple CSPD officers from the Falcon Division were dispatched to the 4000 Block of Clyde Way on the UCCS campus after multiple reports of shots fired were called in to the CSPD communications center. UCCS campus police also received reports and responded to the area. Initial information indicates an unknown male subject confronted a student on campus outside a building. The suspect produced a handgun and fired at least one round before fleeing the area on foot. 

CSPD and UCCS officers canvassed the area on foot with the assistance of a drone provided by the Real Time Crime Center but the suspect was not located. UCCS police conducted a brief lockdown on the UCCS campus while the immediate area was searched and the lockdown has since been lifted.  No one was injured in the incident and the investigation is ongoing. The investigation is being conducted in a coordinated effort between the Colorado Springs Police Department and UCCS campus police at the request of UCCS officials. Surveillance footage and other records are being reviewed to attempt to identify the unknown male who fired the handgun.

Title: MVT
Summary: On Monday, January 12, 2026, during a BATTLE deployment, members of the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office, Colorado Springs Police Department, and Colorado State Patrol conducted a BATTLE operation in the City of Colorado Springs and El Paso County. We recovered two vehicles and arrested two people. Suspects Dominic Bryan age 31 and Zhane Molina age 26 were arrested and charged with Aggravated Robbery, Motor Vehicle Theft, Felony Menacing and vehicle assault. Additionally, Bryan had a felony violation of parole warrant for his arrest. Both EPSO and CSPD tactical units worked together and systematically captured both of these dangerous suspects. Detectives from EPSO are the case agents for these two individuals and later transported them to the Criminal Justice Center for booking.
Adults Arrested: Dominic Bryan age 31 and Zhane Molina age 26

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On 01-10-2026 at approximately 1654hrs officers from the Falcon Division were dispatched to the 5000 Block of N. Nevada Ave. to investigate a robbery.  An unknown male and female, wearing masks, entered the business and demanded money.  The male suspect grabbed an unknown amount of money from the clerk and he and the female left the area, northbound on foot.  During the incident the male held his hand in his pocket as if he was armed, but a weapon was not seen.  None of the employees were injured during the incident.

Investigation Continues.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 1/8/2026, at approximately 1042pm hours, Stetson Hills Division Officer T. Deines and Officer C. Maiava were in their marked patrol cruiser with their overheads activated at Tutt Blvd and Alderney Way with a stalled vehicle on a hill when a Toyota Tacoma slid into the rear bumper of Police Cruiser 2551.  A DUI investigation occurred and the driver was placed into custody for DUI.  Due to the damage, the cruiser was dead lined. Risk Management Notified.

Title: Cold Accident Reporting
Summary:
****UPDATE*****


As of 01-11-2026 at 11:00 AM CSPD is no longer on Crash Alert


The Colorado Springs Police Department is on Crash Alert status as of 01/08/2026 at 3:00 PM due to road conditions. Drivers are reminded if they are involved in a traffic accident with no fatality or injury requiring medical attention, no driver involved is suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, all drivers are present with license, registration and insurance information, and no damage to public property such as a street sign or utility pole has occurred, drivers should exchange their information and follow the procedure for completing a counter (cold) report. When damage to any vehicle requires a wrecker, a counter report can still be made providing the previous criteria is met. Drivers can either pick up a traffic accident report form at any of the police department substations or they can go online at coloradosprings.gov/police, click on “Online Reporting” and select “Cold Reporting: Report a Minor Traffic Accident” for instructions. Drivers should also be reminded to take the appropriate steps to avoid being involved in a traffic accident during adverse weather and road conditions. Drivers should check road and travel conditions before heading out and leave more time to get to their destination. Drivers need to travel at a safe speed for the conditions and leave an increased and safe following distance from the vehicle in front of them. Drivers should use streets and roadways less hazardous in winter driving conditions. Drivers also need to remove snow and ice obstructing their vision and have adequate snow tires on their vehicles

Title: Assault
Summary:
Units were dispatched to a reported Robbery at a business in the 600 block of North Murray Blvd. They were advised the suspect had fled and when he did, he struck a Security Guard with his vehicle. A marked patrol unit saw the suspect vehicle south on Murray Blvd at Airport Road. The unit turned around to attempt a traffic stop and the vehicle accelerated away as the Patrol Officer saw it turn on to Wentworth from Chapman Drive which leads out to Academy. The suspect continued to flee North on Academy Blvd where he attempted to go around several citizens cars at the intersection of Airport Road and ended up crashing into two vehicles, disabling his vehicle.  A K9 Officer assigned to the call saw the suspect and attempted to engage him and as he did the suspect fled on foot. Another unit assigned to the call saw the suspect fleeing east and pursued him to get him in custody. 
The suspect had stolen approximately $100 worth of items from the business. The Security Guard is currently being treated at a local hospital for injuries that are non-life threatening. No one in the cars the suspect crashed into were injured. The suspect was not injured in the crash or being taken into custody. 
The suspect is charged with Felony Vehicular Assault, Felony Eluding, Obstruction, Petty Theft and several traffic charges.

Title: Suspicious Circumstances
Summary: On January 4, 2026, at approximately 1:30 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) received a report regarding a threat directed at Russell Middle School. After several days of investigation involving various units, School Resource Officers contacted a suspect on January 6, 2026. It was determined that the post in question was made by a juvenile who claimed it was intended as a joke. The young individual may face repercussions from both CSPD and the school. The School Resource unit emphasizes the importance of parents discussing with their children the serious implications of posting anything that could be interpreted as a threat to a school. District 11 security officers and the SRO unit worked diligently together to ensure the safety of all students.

Title: MVT
Location: Airport Road and Sand Creek Drive
Summary:
On Wednesday, December 17, 2025, at approximately 4:00am, a red 1999 Jeep Cherokee was taken from the 100 blk of North Garo Avenue without permission. 
On Wednesday, January 7, 2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department Motor Vehicle Theft detectives saw this red Jeep parked in the 400 blk North Murray Boulevard and Mr. James Haynes, age 33, was in the driver’s seat. James was later seen driving eastbound on East Platte Avenue, where a marked police vehicle attempted to stop the Jeep. James did stop, however, when the officer began their contact with him, James fled in the Jeep at a high rate of speed, eluding uniformed officers. Detectives located the vehicle in the 5100 blk of Airport Road, but the vehicle was unoccupied. Detectives canvassed the area and saw James walking westbound on Airport Road near Sand Creek Drive. Detectives contacted James and later transported him to the Criminal Justice Center where he was charged with Motor Vehicle Theft and Felony Eluding. James is currently out on bond for several MVT charges.

Title: TEU - K9 Activity
Summary:
On January 6, 2026, around 10:00 p.m., a business in Fountain, Colorado, was robbed by a suspect armed with a handgun. The suspect left the scene in a dark SUV. Detectives from the Fountain Police Department asked for help from the Colorado Springs Police Department’s Real Time Crime Center (RTCC) and the Metro Motor Vehicle Theft Unit (MVT) to identify the vehicle seen on the business’s security cameras.
RTCC detectives used the Flock License Plate Reader system to locate an image of the SUV. MVT detectives confirmed the SUV had been reported stolen in Colorado Springs. With this information, Fountain detectives identified the suspect as 32-year-old Tyson Dunckel and obtained a warrant for his arrest.
On January 7, 2026, around 1:00 p.m., Fountain detectives, along with Colorado State Parole Officers and Metro detectives, found Dunckel driving the stolen SUV. They requested assistance from CSPD’s Tactical Enforcement Unit (TEU). TEU officers, CSPD K9, and the RTCC drone unit responded to the area. The drone operator located the SUV on the 3700 block of Betty Drive and saw Dunckel enter a business wearing a paintball mask and ski goggles.
When Dunckel left the business and walked toward the SUV, TEU officers moved in. Upon contact Dunckel ran east on foot but fell near Half Turn and Academy Boulevard. When he hit the ground, a handgun fell from his waistband. Dunckel stayed on the ground and was taken into custody. The handgun was recovered and turned over to Fountain detectives.
At the SUV, officers found 23 year old Reanna Edsall in the driver’s seat. She tried to run but was detained. Edsall had active felony warrants in El Paso County. Both Dunckel and Edsall were booked into the Criminal Justice Center.
This case stands as an excellent example of multiple agencies and specialized units in the El Paso County region working together to safely apprehend an armed and dangerous suspect with minimal force.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On January 7th, 2026, HSI agents were attempting to contact a wanted suspect who had a federal warrant for his arrest. Charges for this suspect consisted of racketeering conspiracy, murder in aid of racketeering, aiding and abetting, and conspiracy. When HSI attempted to make the arrest the suspect fled on foot, jumping fences in the surrounding neighborhood. Sand Creek Patrol officers were requested to assist in finding the suspect. Detectives from ICAC were requested to help to help and later VOFTF detectives responded to assist by setting up a perimeter in the area. CSPD K-9 conducted a neighborhood search while the Real Time Crime Center drone provided invaluable aerial surveillance to find and locate this dangerous suspect. TEU arrived and contacted the residence associated with the suspect and while speaking with the residents the suspect was located in a shed in a nearby backyard by containment officers. The suspect was arrested without incident and booked into the El Paso County Jail.

Title: Other
Summary: On this date the Colorado Springs Police Department issued a press release to the media regarding Sexually Violent Predator (SVP), 43-year-old Nathan John Zaborski. Zaborski is registered as living at 3688 Iguana Dr. Please refer to the CSPD web page for further information. Title: Narcotics - Vice Activity
Summary: On January 2, 2026, at 11:22PM Officers with the CSPD Gold Hill Division were contacted by a security guard at a downtown parking garage. The security Guard said there was a male slumped over the steering wheel parked inside the garage. On contacted officers observed the male to be unconscious. Upon waking the male up officers noticed the male told officers he had methamphetamine in the car and a gun in his waistband. The male complied with orders and was detained without incident. After a subsequent search of the male a handgun was recovered. A search of the vehicle revealed a large quantity of illegal narcotics and additional firearms. The male was charged and booked into the El Paso County Jail on felony charges.

Title: Other
Summary: On 01/03/2026 at approximately 0409 hours, Sand Creek Shift III officers responded to the 2500 block of S. Academy Blvd for a weapons call for service. Investigation revealed that the suspect had an argument with another vehicle and stepped out of his vehicle and fired several shots into the air. He then knocked on a business window while still holding the firearm, frightening the employees.  The suspect vehicle was located several blocks away and a traffic stop was initiated. A firearm and one spent shell casing was located in the suspect vehicle.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 01/03/2026 at approximately 0334 hours, Sand Creek Shift III officers responded to the 3600 block of E Uintah St for a robbery. Investigation determined that the victim had met the suspect at a bar and had communicated further on a social media platform. She had been riding in a vehicle with the suspect when he produced and offered the victim drugs. The victim declined the offer and knocked the drugs out of the suspect’s hand. She then tried to get out of the vehicle and the suspect reached across her and closed the door so she couldn’t get out of the vehicle. The suspect then took her purse with contents and drove away while the victim was trying to get her purse back. The victim was able to get out of the vehicle without her purse. The suspect kept the contents of the purse and discarded the purse. The victim’s purse was located approximately one-half block away.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Location: intersection of E Espanola St and N Cascade Ave
Summary:
On January 2, 2026, at approximately 5:47 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a vehicle versus pedestrian crash near the intersection of East Espanola Street and North Cascade Avenue.

Emergency personnel arrived on scene and located the pedestrian, who was deceased. The driver of the involved vehicle remained on scene and cooperated with emergency responders.

Based on the circumstances, the CSPD Major Crash Team responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation. The initial investigation indicates the vehicle was traveling northbound on North Cascade Avenue approaching East Espanola Street, which is a single-lane roadway. The pedestrian was crossing North Cascade Avenue near East Espanola Street when the collision occurred.

At this time, impairment is not suspected, and speed does not appear to be a contributing factor. The intersection of East Espanola Street and North Cascade Avenue was closed for several hours while investigators processed the scene.

The identity of the deceased pedestrian will be released by the El Paso County Coroner’s Office once next of kin has been notified.

Title: Other
Summary: On 1/1/2026 at approximately 10:48pm, Sand Creek Patrol Officers were flagged down by a citizen walking along E Bijou St. and informed of a possible structure fire. Officers quickly found the fire was rapidly spreading across the bottom floor of an abandoned building in the 2200 block of Platte Pl. Officers secured the scene and notified CSFD, who later responded and took control of the scene. No injuries or occupants were found in the building.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On 12/23/2025, CSPD investigators arrested a juvenile suspect for 2nd Degree Burglary (of a business) stemming from a burglary which occurred on 11/23/2025. On 01/01/2026, detectives with CSPD’s Burglary Unit executed a search warrant in the 1400 block of Tamarisk Dr. and took a second juvenile into custody pursuant to the same investigation. 

During the burglary, two juveniles stole a vehicle and crashed that vehicle into a business in the 3600 block of Galley Road causing an estimated $30,000 in damage. The two juvenile suspects entered the business and stole collectibles valued at nearly $10,000 before fleeing the scene in a getaway vehicle being driven by an adult co-conspirator.

Contemporaneous to the service of the search warrant, investigators located the adult male suspect in the area of Tamarisk Drive and attempted a traffic stop. The suspect fled the scene in a vehicle but was later located and taken into custody for burglary and contributing to the delinquency of a minor. 

One of the juvenile suspects had been previously charged with offenses related to similar smash-and-grab burglaries and is currently on probation for aggravated robbery with a firearm.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: E Woodmen Rd / N Marksheffel Rd
Summary: On 01/01/2026 at 9:56 AM, CSPD received multiple reports of a traffic crash in the intersection of E Woodmen Rd and N Marksheffel Rd. Upon arrival, officers located a two-vehicle crash with one driver trapped inside a vehicle. After extrication by CSFD, the driver was transported to a hospital with serious, non-life-threatening injuries. The other driver was not seriously injured. Investigation revealed that a vehicle was making a left turn from westbound Woodmen Rd onto southbound Marksheffel Rd when it was struck by another vehicle traveling eastbound on Woodmen Rd. All lanes of the roadway were reopened at 11:43 AM. Investigation is ongoing.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 01/01/2026 at 0156 hours, Sand Creek and Falcon officers were dispatched to the area of Mount View Lane and Stanton Street in reports of a disturbance where shots were fired at a victim.  Officers located the victim, who was the niece of the suspect, and learned a disturbance took place in a residence in the area of 1000 Magnolia Street.  The victim was reporting the suspect was intoxicated and during an argument pulled a handgun from his waistband and pointed it at her.  The suspect then fired at least one shot in the air from inside the residence.  The victim told officers the suspect’s girlfriend was present during this and attempted to wrestle the gun away from the suspect.  During this altercation, the suspect pointed the handgun at his girlfriend as well and possibly fired additional shots in the air.  When officers arrived at Magnolia Street, they set up a crisis entry team and contained the residence.  Contact with the suspect’s girlfriend was made by phone and she was convinced to exit the residence while the suspect remained inside, possibly asleep on top of the firearm.  Officers learned there were two other tenants upstairs in the joined unit.  Contact was made with them, and they were able to exit safely.  Once the suspect was isolated downstairs, TEU and UAV were notified and responded.  With consent from the owner and confirmation the suspect did not have standing in the residence a drone was used to confirm the suspect’s location.  TEU and officers were able to make entry into the residence and take the suspect into custody without any incident.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 12/30/25 at 6:45PM CSPD received multiple reports of a traffic crash at the intersection of S Powers Bl and Astrozon Bl. Upon arriving on scene, officers located a three-vehicle crash with multiple injured parties. Investigation revealed a vehicle traveling northbound on Powers turned left in front of a truck traveling southbound on Powers, with the occupants of the northbound vehicle being ejected following the crash. All parties were transported to local hospitals for treatment of injuries. The investigation is continuing at this time.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On December 29, 2025, at 7:52PM, an aggravated robbery was reported at a business in the 700 block of Village Center Drive.  Three parties approached the business and demanded money, while at least one of the suspects brandished a firearm.  The suspects, two males and a female, left on foot.  All suspects are described as white, 20-40’s.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Location: N. Powers Blvd / E Platte Ave
Summary: On 12/27/2025 at 6:16 PM officers from the Colorado Springs Police Department Sand Creek Division responded to a crash on Powers Blvd south of Platte Ave. On arrival officers located a victim trapped in one of the involved vehicles.  The victim was extricated and transported in critical condition. The victim was pronounced deceased a short time later.  Due to the nature of the crash, the Major Crash Team responded and assumed the investigation. The initial investigation revealed a SUV was northbound, crossed the center median and struck a sedan that was southbound.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On December 27, 2025 at 1129 hours CSPD received a report of a possible burglary in progress occurring at a condemned building in the 4300 block of E Pikes Peak Ave. A short time later, a caller reported that weapons were involved in the incident. Multiple Sand Creek and Stetson Hills Officers responded. Upon arrival, Officers learned that a Security Officer had contacted parties attempting to enter the condemned building. The Security Officer was assaulted by multiple adults using bats and tools. The Security Officer was hospitalized with serious injuries and two adults were taken into custody. The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 12/27/25 Officer from CSPD Shift II and DART II observed a physical fight in the zero hundred block of Tejon St. While making contact, Officers identified themselves and ordered 3-4 males fighting to stop. On contact one suspect looked directly at an officer, took a fighting stance and punched him in the head. The suspect attempted to reengage the original fight when he was tased and taken into custody. The Officer sustained a minor injury and returned to duty. The suspect was treated at a local hospital for minor injuries before being booked into the El Paso County Jail.

Title: Robbery
Summary: An unknown party attempted what appeared was going to be a robbery at a local financial institution. The unknown party was clothed in what was described as a painters’ coverall suit along with mask and gloves. The party was prevented entry by an employee who saw the party approaching and locked the entry doors. The party fled in a black SUV that had red and white wrapping paper covering portions of it. Members of CSPD`s Robbery Unit responded to the scene.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary:
On 12/16/2025 officers were dispatched to a three-vehicle crash in the 0 block of Academy Blvd. The investigation revealed that one of the drivers possibly had a medical episode and was transported to a local hospital due to this episode, but without obvious injuries. 
On 12/22/2025 the El Paso County Coroner's Office was notified by the hospital that the driver who had caused the crash passed away as a result of the incident. No other details were released. Neither speed nor alcohol are considered a factor in this crash.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
*****Update to ETACS #35841*****

SIU detectives requested the assistance from the Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force (VOFTF) to apprehend the prolific juvenile suspect involved in the robbery and motor vehicle theft that occurred at the Emily Griffith Center on 12/21/25. VOFTF was assisted by detectives from Robbery and STIC and the stolen vehicle was observed being driven by a suspect matching the juvenile suspect. The vehicle was parked in a neighborhood in the 1600 block of S. Circle Rd. and the suspect was observed entering some stores in the area. CSPD TEU, K-9, and UAS responded to assist with the apprehension and as he was walking in the parking lot the juvenile was taken into custody without incident. The stolen vehicle was recovered by detectives and towed to the POC for further investigation.

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On 12/21/25, Gold Hill Officers were dispatched to Emily Griffith Center, regarding a motor vehicle theft in progress. Officers established probable cause that a Prolific Juvenile Offender (suspect), had forcibly removed a set of car keys from his caretaker’s person, ran out of the facility, and stole a blue 2016 Ford Escape, bearing CO plate #CZTM41.
The suspect has been arrested for multiple motor vehicle thefts, two (2) attempted 1st degree assaults against police officers by pointing handguns at officers, felony menacings, and an attempted homicide incident, when a registered owner was shot at after confronting the suspect and other juvenile associates about stealing his vehicle.
Due to these circumstances, the suspect should be considered armed and dangerous, has a high propensity for violence, is a flight risk, and is known to obtain handguns by committing motor vehicle burglaries and motor vehicle thefts. These offenses are commonly done by breaking into Kia and Hyundai vehicles. The suspect is highly proficient in the methods and means of stealing these types of cars, widely known as the “Kia Boyz Challenge.” 
If contact is made with the suspect, use extreme caution and notify Metro if necessary. A pending E-warrant has been submitted for Robbery and 2nd Degree Motor Vehicle Theft related to this incident.

Title: Assault
Summary: On December 21, 2025, at approximately 6:45 pm, the Colorado Springs Police Department was notified of an individual who sustained a stab wound and he was located in the 1700 block of Aeroplaza Drive.  Officers responded to the location and located a male with a stab wound to his chest. The male was transported to a local hospital in critical condition.  This is an active investigation, if anyone has any information, please contact CSPD at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you can call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 12/20/2025 at 8:09 PM officers were dispatched to the 600 blk of Erie Rd for a report of a domestic disturbance in which a party was attempting to gain entry into the home and one person had been stabbed.  Officers located a male party with a stab wound and he was transported to an area hospital with serious but non-life-threatening injuries. Due to the nature of the investigation, detectives from the Domestic Violence/Adult Sexual Assault unit responded and assumed the investigation.  Agustin Sanchez Flores was taken into custody for various domestic violence related charges.  The investigation is on going.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: At 4:23 PM, CSPD responded to a call to 911 regarding a traffic crash on Voyager Parkway and Cypress Semi Drive involving a pedestrian and one vehicle. When officers and medical personnel arrived, they found a male unresponsive on scene. Medical aid was rendered, but the male did not respond. The CSPD Major Crash Team responded and has assumed the investigation. The driver of the vehicle remained on scene and is cooperating with the investigation. At this point in the investigation, speed, alcohol, or drugs are not considered factors in this crash.

Title: TEU - K9 Activity
Summary:
On 12-19-2025, at approximately 1100 hours, Officer Mueller with the Downtown Area Response Team (DART) observed Angel Pena Lopez (36) walking in the area of 1500 South Nevada Avenue. Officer Mueller was aware that Pena had an active felony arrest warrant issued by the State of Colorado Parole Division and that he was being actively investigated by the U.S. Marshals Service Southern Colorado Violent Offenders Fugitive Task Force.
The U.S. Marshals Task Force advised that Pena had a history of violent crimes, was known to flee from police contact, and was considered armed and dangerous.
Officer Mueller requested assistance from CSPD K9 Officers Ferguson and Lofgren, as well as the CSPD Tactical Enforcement Unit (TEU) Tac team officers. Officers from the Homeless Outreach Team also responded to assist.
Officers attempted to contact Pena, who immediately fled on foot. Pena ignored multiple verbal commands from K9 officers to stop or a police service dog would be deployed. Pena changed direction and ran toward Nevada Avenue, which had heavy traffic.
Officer Lofgren deployed K9 Riggs, who pursued Pena. During the pursuit, Pena removed a handgun from his left waistband area and began to raise it. K9 Riggs engaged Pena on the left upper arm, knocking Pena to the ground and dislodging the firearm.
Officers quickly took control of Pena, and K9 Riggs was disengaged within seconds of the takedown.
Pena sustained minor abrasions to his left triceps and a laceration to his head as a result of striking the ground. He was transported to a local hospital, treated for injuries, and later booked into the Criminal Justice Center on his outstanding arrest warrant.
Pena will also be charged with motor vehicle theft, resisting arrest and possession of a weapon by a previous offender.
All officers involved should be commended for their coordinated response and professionalism in apprehending an armed felon who was actively attempting to evade arrest. Their disciplined use of tactics likely prevented a shooting and minimized the risk of injury to Pena and to citizens in the surrounding area.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 12/19/2025 at 0309 hrs, officers were dispatched to a domestic disturbance. Upon arrival, officers observed the subjects involved to potentially have serious bodily injuries and both were transported to the hospital.  The CSPD Domestic Violence (DVASA) Unit responded and will continue the investigation.

Title: Other
Summary:
***UPDATE***

On December 18th, members of the Regional Explosives Unit contacted witnesses related to the fire at 3170 Century St. The witnesses identified 32 year old Demetri Medina from the person of interest media release. Based on the witness identification, and video surveillance recovered probable cause was established  for Medina’s arrest.  Medina will be charged with 4th Degree Arson, 2nd Degree Arson and Harassment.  

At approximately 10:30PM, Medina was taken into custody in the 2800 block of N Nevada Ave, by Falcon Division Patrol Officers.

Initial CSFD Media Release

On Saturday, December 13, at 2:43 p.m., the Colorado Springs Fire Department (CSFD) responded to multiple reports of a structure fire at 3170 N Century St. CSFD Fire Investigators responded and have determined the fire appears to have been intentionally set. The Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) Regional Explosives Unit was notified and has assumed responsibility for the investigation.

Initial information was that a building was on fire and producing heavy smoke, visible across Colorado Springs. CSFD Engine 6 was in the area and immediately called a working structure fire and responded. Upon arrival at 2:48 p.m., Engine 6 personnel found a large pile of styrofoam and construction materials burning in an outdoor area of the involved business. Due to the size of the fire and the need for additional resources, a second alarm was quickly requested.

Firefighters were able to contain the fire to the pile and prevent it from spreading to nearby buildings. Heavy equipment was brought in to break apart the pile to ensure no active fire remained. The fire was knocked down approximately 45 minutes after arrival. No injuries were reported.

The CSFD Fire Investigations Unit and CSPD Regional Explosives Unit work side by side to investigate any fire that appears to be criminal in nature.

All further information regarding the fire and investigation will come from the CSPD Public Affairs Section. This is an ongoing and active investigation; there is no further information to share at this time.

Title: Other
Summary: On 12/18/2025 at approximately 3:40PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department’s Communication Center received a 911 call for found explosives.  The reporting party located a suspicious package in the area of Indianpipe Cr and Westwood Blvd. Officers responded and closed the intersection.  Members of the Regional Explosives Unit responded and evaluated the package.  The package was rendered safe and removed from the area.  The intersection was re-opened about 5:17PM.  The investigation into the package is ongoing.

Title: Burglary
Summary:
On Sunday, December 14th, 2025, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a residential burglary located in the 7000 block of Sun Shimmer View and burglary of a motor vehicle in the 2500 block of Tenderfoot Hill. During the investigation, Detective M. Black was able to develop probable cause for the arrest of Dylan Hickman, age 24 as the burglary suspect. The Metro Theft and Motor Vehicle Theft units conducted surveillance at Dylan place of employment which was located in the area of Troy Hill Road and were aware he would be driving a gold 2010 Dodge Ram 1500.
At approximately 5:30 PM, Mr. Hickman was observed entering his vehicle and leaving his place of employment. He was then seen going northbound on North Powers Boulevard near Galley Road. Detective Baughman, who was in a police vehicle equipped with emergency lights and siren, attempted to stop Mr. Hickman; however, Mr. Hickman fled at a high-speed, eluding law enforcement. 
Detectives continued surveillance and learned Mr. Hickman entered a black Ford Focus, which was later seen pulling into the parking lot of the Maverick located at 500 Wooten Road. Dylan was contacted and arrested without incident. Detectives obtained a search warrant for Dylan’s residence and his vehicle. During the searches of the various locations, numerous items of stolen property were recovered along with a handgun which did not have a serial number.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 12/17/2025 at approximately 5:40PM officers with the Gold Hill division were dispatched to the 3600 block of West Colorado Ave on a report of a burglary of a residence in progress. Upon arrival officers encountered the suspect. The suspect attempted to exit the residence shoving the officers and caused minor injury to an officer. Further investigation found the suspect had taken mail from the front porch of an attached residence. The suspect was taken into custody and later transported to El Paso County CJC on felony burglary and assault charges.

Title: Warrant
Summary:
On 12/17/25 at approximately 1550 hours, officers with the Colorado Springs Police Department responded to the area of 3100 Institute St. regarding follow-up for a family disturbance that occurred on 12/16/25, which resulted in two felony warrants for the suspect. Upon arrival, the officer heard the suspect in the home, and when waiting for cover units, observed the victim of the prior disturbance arrive home. When officers attempted to contact the suspect, he ran in and out the back and front of the residence, saw officers, and ran back inside. The victim then told officers to leave and refused to come out. 

Once additional resources arrived, officers, with assistance from TAC units and K9, breached and entered the residence to remove the victim and suspect. The suspect was taken into custody on his warrants without further incident.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 12/17/2025 at about 06:00 am a CSPD officer was on his way to work when he was rear-ended in his personal vehicle by another motorist. The motorist was driving an unregistered vehicle with a fictitious plate, and his identity could not be verified immediately. When pressed about his identity, the male became uncooperative. Further investigation revealed that he was using another male's identity. A subsequent search to determine his actual identity revealed numerous forged documents, stolen ID's, stolen mail, burglary tools, and narcotics. After his identity was uncovered, he was found to have numerous warrants for his arrest.

Title: Other
Summary: On this date the Colorado Springs Police Department issued a press release to the media regarding Sexually Violent Predator (SVP), 66 year old Mitchell Wayne Bishop. Bishop is registered as transient staying around the area of the “Springs Rescue Mission” at 5 W Las Vegas St. Please refer to the CSPD web page for further information. Contact Detective JR Brown at 719-444-7672 if you have questions regarding Mitchell Bishop or this notification.

Title: Death
Summary: On December 15th, 2025 at approximately 8:53PM, officers were dispatched to the 1600 block of Lorraine St. for an urgent assist.  Upon arrival officers found an adult female deceased inside the residence.  The CSPD homicide unit responded and assumed the investigation.

Title: Assault
Summary: On December 15, 2025, at 11:41 PM, the public safety communications center received multiple calls about a shooting in the 1000 block of Moffat Circle. Officers responded and located an adult male in the 1100 block of Moffat Circle suffering from a gunshot wound. The male was transported to a local hospital for medical treatment and is expected to survive. Officers located the scene of the shooting and made contact with the occupants of the single family residential home. Detectives with the Assault Unit responded to assist with witness interviews and execution of a search warrant. The investigation is in the early stages. No arrests have been made at this time.

Title: Other
Summary: On 12/14/25 at 2:01PM, a sergeant from the Sand Creek Division noticed a large plume of black smoke in the area of Bijou St./Circle Dr. The sergeant investigated and found a semi-truck cab was fully engulfed in fire in a parking lot of a tow company in the 2500 block of Gunnison St. The Colorado Springs Fire Department quickly responded and extinguished the fire before it spread to a neighboring building or other vehicles. The cause of the fire is unknown at this time. There were no injuries reported.

Title: Assault
Location: Integration Loop and Peak Innovation Pkwy
Summary: On 12/14/2025 at approximately 2:23 AM, CSPD Officers were dispatched to a local hospital for a shooting, where two victims had been dropped off at the Emergency Department. One juvenile male and one adult male were treated for non-life-threatening injuries. The incident was related to a shot-fired call for service in the area of Integration Loop and Peak Innovation Parkway that occurred at approximately 2:00 AM during a car meet. The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 12/13/25 at approximately 10:08 Colorado Springs Police Department received a call about a bank robbery at financial institution in the 1800 block of S. Nevada Ave.  Patrol officers and detectives from the Robbery Unit responded to the scene.  There officers learned that a male suspect entered the bank and demanded money from a teller.  The suspect then fled the bank in an unknown direction.  The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: N. Academy Blvd at Palmer Park Blvd
Summary: On 12/13/25 at 1:32AM, a patrol officer from the Stetson Hills Division was in the area of Palmer Park Blvd and N. Academy Blvd when she located a traffic crash that occurred just moments prior. The crash involved two vehicles and resulted in one adult male passenger being transported by ambulance to a local area hospital with serious but non-life-threatening injuries. The intersection was closed for eastbound, westbound, and northbound traffic during the traffic crash investigation. All traffic lanes were re-opened at approximately 4:45AM. Impairment is believed to be a factor in the crash.

Title: Disturbance
Summary: On 12/12/2025 at approximately 1:51 AM, the Colorado Springs Communications Center received a call for a shooting that just occurred near the intersection of N. Tejon St. and E. Boulder St. CSPD Officers arrived on scene and found one party suffering from a non-life-threatening gunshot wound. Approximately five minutes later, Officers learned that a second party with a non-life-threatening gunshot wound arrived at a local hospital. The investigation showed that these incidents were related to a disturbance in the downtown area. All parties involved in the disturbance have been contacted by law enforcement. The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Assault
Summary: On the above date and time, officers with the Colorado Springs Police Department were dispatched to the area of 1600 S. Nevada Ave on a reported shooting. Upon arrival, officers discovered one victim with a single gun shot wound to his lower leg (non life threatening). The suspect had fled prior to PD arrival. According to witnesses on scene, this shooting may have been a dispute over money. At this time, detectives with the assault unit are developing suspect information. It is believed this is an isolated incident and no other victims were reported.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Monday, December 8, 2025, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated the theft of a gray 2005, Suzuki Aerio, which was taken from the 1000 block of South Chelton Road.
On Tuesday, December 9, 2025, at approximately 8:30pm, the Motor Vehicle Theft Unit located the Suzuki in the area of Constitution Avenue and Akers Drive. Detectives tried to stop this vehicle; however, they fled and eluded law enforcement. 
Detectives conducted surveillance of this stolen vehicle and followed them to the intersection of East Pikes Peak Avenue and Academy Boulevard.  While in this area, detectives grappled the vehicle and arrested all three occupants. Eric Diaz age 24 was charged with motor vehicle theft. Alejandro Ruiz age 28 was charged with eluding, motor vehicle theft and possession of a stolen firearm. The rear passenger juvenile was arrested for an outstanding domestic violence FTA warrant.  
During a search of the vehicle, detectives located a 22 caliber loaded handgun which was also stolen.
Adults Arrested: Eric Diaz age 24, Alejandro Ruiz age 28

Title: Assault
Summary: On December 9, 2025, at approximately 4:38 AM, Officers from the Stetson Hills Division were dispatched to an injured male who was located on the ground, outside of a residence, in the 4000 block of Hollow Road.  The male had blunt force trauma wounds to his body and was transported to a local hospital for treatment.  It was determined by medical personnel that his wounds are severe, but non-life-threatening.  Officers identified an address on Hollow Road where the assault may have taken place.  When they attempted contact at the address, no one answered the door.  Given the nature of the call and the information obtained from the victim, the Tactical Enforcement Unit responded to assist.  The Assault unit was notified and also responded to assist with the investigation.  Eventually, two individuals exited the residence and were briefly detained but not taken into custody.  The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Burglary
Summary:
On December 8, 2025, at 1:25 PM, a disturbance was reported in the 2100 block of Ridgeway Avenue. A female had knocked at the door, and the workers let her inside, where she confronted them and assaulted one of the workers.  Officers arrived on scene as the female suspect exited and drove out of the driveway, striking a fully marked, unoccupied police cruiser, 2923, parked away from the residence.  The female left at a high rate of speed. Officers did not pursue.  

At 3:28 PM, another caller reported a disturbance in the 900 block of Pacific Hills Point, in which the female had arrived demanding a baby, made threats to kill, and entered another property without permission.  The female was left in the same vehicle that had fled the earlier scene.

Officers responding to the second call observed the female’s vehicle going eastbound on Cheyenne Meadows Road.  Officers attempted a traffic stop when the suspect fled into oncoming traffic at a high rate of speed.  Officers observed the vehicle suddenly stop, the driver/suspect exit, and move toward officers; however, the vehicle was not in park, and began to roll.  The driver returned to their vehicle, driving away in the wrong lanes of travel, narrowly missing another cruiser.

Officers responded to the suspect’s home, contacted the suspect, and took her into custody without further incident.  The suspect was booked on multiple felony charges for the two incidents.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On 12/08/2025, at approximately 1552 hours, CSPD's Communications Center received numerous calls involving a rollover traffic crash at the intersection of N.
