Carefree Cir. and Inspiration Dr. Call screen notes indicated there was a driver ejected and trapped underneath one of the vehicles. Medical personnel arrived on scene and were able to free the trapped party, a 20 year old female and sole occupant of the vehicle. The party was transported via ambulance emergent to a local hospital where she succumbed to her injuries. The other driver was transported to a local hospital with minor injuries. CSPD's Major Crash Team responded and assumed control of the investigation. Drugs/alcohol and speed do not appear to be contributing factors in this crash. However, it appears the deceased party was not wearing a seatbelt at the time of the crash. Their  identification is pending next of kin notification from the El Paso County Coroner's Office and the investigation is ongoing at this time.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On December 7, 2025, at 10PM, a vehicle was attempting to cross the railroad tracks south of the designated railroad crossing at Conejos and Las Animas Street, when it was struck by a southbound train.  The BNSF Train crew saw the vehicle and started to slow.  No one was injured in the crash.  The train stopped and the crossing was closed for several hours while BNSF Inspection team responded to verify the safety of the train, tracks and crossing.  The driver of the vehicle was cited and released.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 12/07/2025 at approximately 03:34 AM Sand Creek Officers were dispatched to the 1900 block of south Chelton Rd for a shooting call for service. The investigation revealed that unknown suspects in a light-colored minivan drove by the victim and fired two shots from a shotgun at the victim who was struck in both arms. The victim was treated at a local hospital for minor injuries. The Assault unit was notified and took over the investigation.

Title: Select Incident Type
Summary: CSPD is now off accident alert status.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On the evening of 12/05/2025, officers from CSPD’s Stetson Hills division responded to a burglary in progress at a residence in the 8000 block of Hardwood Circle. When officers arrived, they observed an Asian male subject in the back yard of the residence who immediately fled on foot upon seeing police. The suspect was apprehended after a brief foot pursuit and was found to be in possession of property consistent with items stolen from the residence. 

This incident is believed to be related to an active pattern burglary investigation. Detectives with CSPD’s Metro Burglary Unit responded to assist. The investigation into this and related burglaries is ongoing.

Title: Sex Assault
Summary: On 12/5/2025 at approximately 1200 hrs., a registered sex offender contacted CSPD to self-report unlawful behaviors. He was identified as 34-year-old Kendon Ray. Among his confessions, he reported that on 12/1/25, he had attempted to lure a young boy into a bathroom at a retail location in the 3200 block of East Platte Avenue. This incident had not been initially reported to the police department. Officers began working to identify the child victim utilizing various resources. Through a combination of resources, the victim was ultimately identified and a forensic interview was conducted by CSPD’s Forensic Interview Unit. Crimes Against Children detectives took over the investigation and arrested Mr. Ray for Enticement of a Child, a Class Four Felony. He will be booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 12/04/2025 at approximately 0935 The Colorado Springs Fire Department responded to southbound I-25 to assist a CSO on a multi vehicle crash just south of the MLK bypass. While emergency personnel were out checking on involved drivers, a motorist traveling southbound failed to safely slow in traffic. The motorist struck a civilian vehicle, continued further southbound and collided with the CSO vehicle before striking a firefighter in full structural gear. Both the Fire truck and CSO vehicles were fully marked and had emergency lighting equipment activated at the time of the collision. The fire fighter was transported from the scene to a local hospital for medical evaluation. The two leftmost traffic lanes of I-25 were shut down for investigation purposes for approximately 2 hours. CDOT responded with plows to assist with debris and snow removal upon completion of the on-scene investigation. Driver charges are pending the completion of the investigation.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On Thursday December 4, 2025, at 2:14 AM, the public safety communications center received the report of a robbery with a weapon in the 6400 block of Issaquah Dr. The victim, a convenience store clerk, reported a male suspect entered the business, showed a pistol, and demanded cash/merchandise. The victim was not injured. The suspect fled on foot. The investigation is in the beginning stages. No arrests have been made at this time.

Title: Cold Accident Reporting
Summary:
**************As of 3:30PM on December 5th, 2025 CSPD is no longer on Accident alert*************************


The Colorado Springs Police Department is on Crash Alert status as of 12/03/2025 at 7:25 AM due to road conditions. Drivers are reminded if they are involved in a traffic accident with no fatality or injury requiring medical attention, no driver involved is suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, all drivers are present with license, registration and insurance information, and no damage to public property such as a street sign or utility pole has occurred, drivers should exchange their information and follow the procedure for completing a counter (cold) report. When damage to any vehicle requires a wrecker, a counter report can still be made providing the previous criteria is met. Drivers can either pick up a traffic accident report form at any of the police department substations or they can go online at coloradosprings.gov/police, click on “Online Reporting” and select “Cold Reporting: Report a Minor Traffic Accident” for instructions. Drivers should also be reminded to take the appropriate steps to avoid being involved in a traffic accident during adverse weather and road conditions. Drivers should check road and travel conditions before heading out and leave more time to get to their destination. Drivers need to travel at a safe speed for the conditions and leave an increased and safe following distance from the vehicle in front of them. Drivers should use streets and roadways less hazardous in winter driving conditions. Drivers also need to remove snow and ice obstructing their vision and have adequate snow tires on their vehicles

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On 12/02/25 at approximately 0106 hours, officers from the Gold Hill Division were dispatched to the 500 block of S. Nevada Avenue in regard to a robbery. The victim initially reported being assaulted and had items taken from their business. The victim was able to provide a suspect description. 

As officers arrived in the area, they located an individual that matched the description of the suspect that was provided by the victim. The suspect was taken into custody without incident. The victim reported that he confronted the suspect, who had concealed items on his person. The suspect threatened the victim and a physical altercation ensued. After the assault occurred, the suspect took items from the business and left the scene on foot. He was contacted by officers a short time later. The suspect was charged with Robbery and Assault in the 3rd Degree.

Title: Assault
Summary: On November 29, 2025, at 8:37PM, a felony domestic assault was reported in the 800 block of South Circle Drive.  A few minutes later a gun shot victim arrived at a local hospital.  Officers learned during the investigations the two incidents were related.  The DV assault occurred in the parking lot, and three good Samaritans attempted to intervene to stop the DV assault, when the suspect brandished a firearm.  The good Samaritans jumped into their vehicle and the suspect fired at their vehicle and struck one of the good Samaritans, causing serious but not life threatening injury.  The suspect fled and was located at his work location and arrested without incident.  Officers recovered a firearm.  The suspect was booked into CJC related to this investigation.

Title: Cold Accident Reporting
Summary:
The Colorado Springs Police Department is on Crash Alert status as of 11/30/25 at 6:15PM due to road conditions. Drivers are reminded if they are involved in a traffic accident with no fatality or injury requiring medical attention, no driver involved is suspected of driving under the influence of alcohol or drugs, all drivers are present with license, registration and insurance information, and no damage to public property such as a street sign or utility pole has occurred, drivers should exchange their information and follow the procedure for completing a counter (cold) report. When damage to any vehicle requires a wrecker, a counter report can still be made providing the previous criteria is met. Drivers can either pick up a traffic accident report form at any of the police department substations or they can go online at coloradosprings.gov/police, click on “Online Reporting” and select “Cold Reporting: Report a Minor Traffic Accident” for instructions. Drivers should also be reminded to take the appropriate steps to avoid being involved in a traffic accident during adverse weather and road conditions. Drivers should check road and travel conditions before heading out and leave more time to get to their destination. Drivers need to travel at a safe speed for the conditions and leave an increased and safe following distance from the vehicle in front of them. Drivers should use streets and roadways less hazardous in winter driving conditions. Drivers also need to remove snow and ice obstructing their vision and have adequate snow tires on their vehicles.


*****As of 11:15 AM on 12/1/2025 CSPD is no longer on Accident Alert*****

Title: TEU - K9 Activity
Summary: On Wednesday, November 26, 2025, at 9:15 AM, officers responded to the 7300 block of Legend Hill Drive on a reported family disturbance. When officers arrived on scene, other members of the household met officers outside the home. The male suspect also exited the home and refused to cooperate with officers, and went back inside the house. The suspect refused lawful orders to exit the house and was threatening violence against officers. Officers negotiated with the suspect for several hours, and at about 2:30 PM, the suspect peacefully walked out of the house and surrendered without incident. The male suspect was taken to CJC and is charged with 2nd degree assault and other charges. The suspect's name is not being released at this time pending further investigation.

Title: Other
Summary: On this date the Colorado Springs Police Department issued a press release to the media regarding Sexually Violent Predator (SVP), 26-year-old Kailan Rodriguez Marion. Marion is registered as homeless staying by the area of 5 W Las Vegas St. Please refer to the CSPD web page for further information. Contact Detective JR Brown at 719-444-7672 if you have questions regarding Kailan Marion or this notification.

Title: TEU - K9 Activity
Summary:
On November 24, 2025, at approximately 2:30 p.m., Detectives from the Lakewood Police Department’s Fugitive Apprehension Unit, working with the U.S. Marshal's Southern Colorado Violent Offenders Fugitive Task Force, located a suspect with multiple felony warrants and a history of violent crimes involving firearms. The suspect was riding a stolen motorcycle.

The Colorado State Patrol Aircraft Unit arrived and maintained surveillance until the suspect stopped near the 3600 block of Michigan Avenue. CSPD Unmanned Aerial Systems Officer Sunday provided real-time updates on the suspect’s movements to tactical officers in the area.

At that point, officers from the CSPD Tactical Enforcement Unit and CSPD K9 Unit moved in to make the arrest. The suspect fled on foot and dropped a stolen handgun while running. After jumping a residential fence, he was confronted by TEU and K9 officers and surrendered. When taken into custody, he was found to be carrying a large amount of methamphetamine.

The suspect was booked into the Criminal Justice Center on his active felony warrants.

His name is being withheld due to an ongoing investigation involving stolen firearms and narcotics.

Detectives with the Metro Armed Violent Offender Unit responded to continue the investigation.

This incident shows the cooperation between local, state and federal units to safely and effectively take a dangerous subject with violent tendencies into custody.
Adults Arrested: Not being released at this time

Title: Assault
Summary: On 11/22/25 at approximately 6:00 AM CSPD officers were dispatched to a business located in the 700 block of N 19th St for a  domestic disturbance.  On arrival officers located a male and 2 female parties.  The male had sustained serious but non-life threatening injuries and was transported to an area hospital for treatment. Detectives from the Domestic Violence/Adult Sexual Assault unit were notified and assumed the investigation.  The investigation showed a second disturbance involving the same parties occurred a few blocks away.  One of the females was taken into custody for felony domestic violence assault charges and the other female was taken into custody for felony assault charges.  The investigation is on going.

Title: Other
Summary:
On November 22, 2025, at 12:17 AM, a call for service was received regarding a domestic disturbance at a residence in the 5200 block of Meadowgreen Dr. The reporting party indicated the disturbance involved a female and her ex-boyfriend, and the ex-boyfriend had a handgun and was trying to gain access to the residence.

As officers were responding, the reporting party indicated the suspect had left, and it was unknown where the female half of the disturbance was located. The reporting party was able to give a vehicle description of the suspect. As officers arrived in the area, the suspect vehicle was located nearby. Victor Hernandez was located in the vehicle, as was the female half of the disturbance. Additionally, officers located a handgun in the vehicle.

After investigation by officers, Victor Hernandez was arrested for a multitude of charges, including 2nd Degree Kidnapping and was lodged in CJC.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 11/20/2025 at approximately 2325hrs, a report of a single vehicle traffic accident was reported in the 4200 block of Astrozon Blvd. Officers arrived in the area at the same time that calls were being received by the Communications Center and observed 2 females inside the vehicle. Officers began to order the driver of the vehicle out who was non-compliant. A short struggle ensued, during which time an officer was kicked by the suspect twice. The driver was charged with 2nd Degree Assault, DUI, as well as numerous other traffic offenses.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 11/20/2025, VOFTF detectives continued their investigation to locate wanted party Glen Frost. Frost had an active arrest warrant for 1st degree burglary, 3rd degree assault, and criminal mischief. Detectives received information Frost was living in a shed with his girlfriend, Brittany Lopez who had 4 misdemeanor warrants for her arrest. Coordinating with TEU and CSPD K-9 a contain and call-out was conducted on the residence. After the introduction of chemical munitions into the shed, Lopez eventually exited the shed but denied Frost was inside. TEU officers introduced additional chemical munitions and Frost exited the shed and fled on foot. Frost attempted to jump a fence but was apprehended by TEU officers who were positioned on rear containment. Frost was combative with rear containment officers but was taken into custody after a brief struggle.

Title: Assault
Summary:
On 11/19/2025 at approximately 1317 hours, a black Land Rover drove off the right side of the roadway and struck landscaping and a metal bus bench on Fillmore Street. The vehicle then re-entered the roadway and continued westbound on Fillmore Street. Two additional calls came in at approximately 1327 and 1331 hours, from witnesses stating the location of a vehicle speeding and driving without a left front wheel and tires smoking in the area of Centennial and 30th. The driver then exited the vehicle and was stumbling around. 

During the investigation of DUI, once the driver was told they were under arrest, they struck an officer in the face and took off running. They were subsequently tased. The driver was charged with Assault in the 2nd Degree, DUI, Careless Driving, Resisting Arrest, and Duty to Report Accidents.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 11/19/2025 at approximately 0900 hours Officers with Metro RPM Organized Crime Unit identified a prolific offender and found a related plate number for him. Working with RPM detectives, they researched leadsonline.com and found the suspect to be pawning a large quantity of “used tools.” Further investigation revealed the suspect has been stealing from multiple retail stores, removing the brand new in box items and literally throwing some dirt on the tool so it looked used. The suspect would then pawn the item. After reaching out to multiple retail stores, RPM learned the suspect had stolen over $10,000 in tools over the past 3 months. Detectives located the suspect’s residence and took him into custody, getting a full confession during the interview. This suspect was one of Metro RPM’s top ten prolific organized retail crime offenders.

Title: MVT
Summary: On 11/18/25 at 5:30AM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a call from a victim of motor vehicle theft in the 1200 block of Cancun Ct. The victim had started their vehicle to warm it and an unknown suspect had taken it. (2025-00439514) At 7:05AM, another citizen called in from the 1000 block of S. Chelton Rd. reporting they believed a stolen vehicle was left in the parking lot. Officers arrived and found the vehicle stolen at 5:30AM. (2025-00439568) At 7:21AM another motor vehicle theft was called in from 1000 S. Chelton Rd, where the victim had also started their vehicle and it was stolen by an unknown person. (2025-00439586) At 7:42AM a new reporting party called in to report a vehicle in the 3300 block of Afternoon Cir. had broken windows. (2025-00439610) At 7:45AM, an officer from the Sand Creek Division was checking the area of 1100 Cancun Ct. when they discovered the vehicle stolen from 1000 S. Chelton Rd. and a male exiting the vehicle. The suspect was detained after a short struggle. At 8:46AM a new victim called in from the 1100 block of S. Chelton to report their vehicle had been broken into and someone had attempted to steal it. (2025-00439685) Arrested was KYLE SANDOVAL (26yo), who admitted to stealing two of the vehicles. He is currently on probation for Motor Vehicle Theft. Officers are working to link him to the other vehicle break-ins.

Title: Pursuit
Summary: Officers of the Sand Creek Division were dispatched to a Felony Menacing involving a firearm in the 1600 blk of Jet Wing Drive. 3 victims had been threatened by a suspect with a gun during an event. The suspect was identified during the investigation. The suspect vehicle was also identified during the investigation. As the event was ending, the suspect was seen getting into the suspect vehicle. The suspect then fired multiple rounds in a crowded parking lot.  Officers who were in the area initiated a pursuit with the suspect. The suspect refused to stop. A vehicle intervention was utilized to stop the suspect. The 3 occupants of the vehicle were detained. Two of the suspects were arrested based on the investigation (Felony Menacing X3, Felony Eluding, Prohibited Use of a Weapon, Reckless Endangerment). Evidence was collected to include at least one gun. No shooting victims were located in the parking lot. No occupants of the suspect vehicle were injured. No officers were injured.
Adults Arrested: Matthew Burgos (19 yrs) Zaquan Johnson (21 yrs)

Title: Assault
Summary: On 11/15/2025 at about 09:53am officers from the Stetson Hills Division were dispatched to a family disturbance in the 5400 block of Sunset Ridge Dr. During their investigation, the officers took the suspect into custody for the violation of a protection order. After she was transported to a cruiser, the female attempted to kick out the windows of the vehicle. Officers put leg shackles on the suspect when the suspect kicked one of the officers. The officer only sustained very minor injuries and was able to continue his duty. The suspect was taken to CJC.

Title: Assault
Summary: Officers of the Sand Creek division were dispatched to a Shooting where the victim was being transported to the hospital. Officers were able to locate a scene (evidence) in the 3300 blk of Portal Drive. One victim suffered a gunshot wound and is in stable condition. The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Assault
Summary:
On November 14, 2025, at 10:30 PM, a shooting was reported at a residence in the 1300 block of Ginny Ln. Upon arrival at the residence, officers located a male victim who had been shot multiple times. This victim was transported to a local hospital where he is being treated for non life threatening injuries.

The possible suspect was not on scene when officers arrived, but is known to the victim. Due  to the complexities of the incident, the Assault Unit responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation.

Title: Other
Summary: On November 14, 2025, at 6:43PM, residents in the area of 1900 West Uintah Street reported an explosion and fire in an alley.  CSFD and CSPD responded.   CSFD found an incendiary device on scene.  Arson Investigators and Regional Explosive Units responded and recovered evidence and will be continuing investigation.  No suspect information at this time.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 11/14/25, Metro Theft detectives developed new vehicle information for Miles Nordahl. Nordahl is a prolific offender known for his involvement in identity theft, motor vehicle theft, and other property crimes. Nordahl had been assigned to the Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force (VOFTF) due to his criminal history and his propensity to flee and be armed with weapons. After receiving the new vehicle information, VOFTF detectives located his truck in the parking lot of the Baymont Hotel located in the 1000 block of Kelly Johnson Blvd. Detectives coordinated with CSPD K-9 and MVT detectives who observed Nordahl in the vehicle. During the apprehension, Nordahl attempted to push out of the vehicle block, but he was unable to break free due to the block being securely set. Realizing he could not flee, Nordahl surrendered peacefully and was taken into custody. Nordahl's girlfriend, Victoria Stevenson was contacted in a hotel room and arrested for an unrelated felony warrant.

Title: Other
Summary: On Thursday, November 13, 2025, at 11:28 PM, CSPD received a call for service of a burglary in progress, potentially involving a firearm, in the 500 block of South Nevada Avenue. When officers arrived on scene, they were attempting to contact the person who had called 911, when three individuals exited an apartment. One of those individuals, a male, had a rifle and raised it toward the officer. At that time, the officer fired multiple shots, striking two individuals. The suspect was taken into custody and detained without further incident. Officers began rendering medical aid to an adult male and an adult female, who both had at least one gunshot wound. Both were transported to a local hospital in stable condition with non-life-threatening injuries. In accordance with Colorado Law, the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office is the lead investigative agency for the officer-involved shooting, and all investigative updates will come from them. Within 21 days, CSPD will release a significant event briefing video that includes body-worn camera footage and other releasable facts about the case.

Title: Warrant
Summary:
On 11/12/2025 officers from the Gold Hill Division were dispatched to the area 2300 W. Kiowa Street regarding a disturbance involving a machete.  An investigation revealed the suspect felony menaced the victim, who was his neighbor with a 24-inch blade machete.  When the suspect was asked about the incident by his father who is defined as an At-Risk Adult per Colorado Revised Statute (18-6.5-102), he grabbed him by his shirt lapels which caused fear.  After a thorough search of the area and the suspect’s residence, officers were unable to locate him.

On 11/13/2025 at approximately 0745 hours, officers were called back out to the residence after neighbors saw the suspect running between his and the neighbor’s residence with the previously mentioned machete.  Officers, including the drone unit, responded to the area and observed the suspect retreating into his residence.  The suspect refused to exit after multiple announcements were given to do so.  A tactical response was initiated, and the suspect eventually exited the residence peacefully.

Title: Other
Summary: On 11/12/25 at 6:34AM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communication Center began receiving 911 calls regarding an explosion and fire in the 200 block of Swope Ave. A Deputy from EPSO also radioed in after seeing smoke and discovering a fire. One of the 911 callers reported hearing someone yelling for help. Multiple fire apparatus from CSFD responded and began fighting the fire, which appears to have started in a shed where trespassers were staying. The fire spread to one other residence. No one was seriously injured. The firefighting efforts are ongoing, as is the investigation. Christopher Phillips (35yo) was arrested on multiple arson charges.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 11/11/2025 at approximately 10:45am officers from the Stetson Hills Division as well as motorcycle officers were dispatched to a rollover crash involving a semi-truck in the 5600 block of Stetson Hills Blvd. Upon arrival the officers observed the semi-truck after it rolled over in the southbound right turn lane onto westbound Stetson Hills Blvd where it hit pickup truck which was stopped at a red light. Both drivers sustained minor injuries. Impairment does not appear to be a factor in this crash. All westbound lanes of Stetson Hills Blvd at Powers Blvd are completely blocked. A notification will be sent out once the lanes are back open.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On November 11, 2025, at approximately 7:30am, officers from the Gold Hill Division were dispatched to the area of NB I-25 and N. Nevada Ave to investigate a reported auto vs. pedestrian traffic accident. When officers arrived on scene, it was determined that a female had been in the northbound lanes of I-25 when she was struck by a vehicle. The female sustained serious, but non-life-threatening injuries and was transported to a local hospital.  At the time of this entry, all lanes of I-25 are open.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 10/28/2025, The Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) responded to a reported business robbery in the 3500 Block of North Academy. Officers learned a male suspect armed with a handgun entered the store, pointed the handgun at the clerk and demanded money from the register. The CSPD Robbery Unit took over the investigation and on 11/10/2025, Detective R. Blank/2879 obtained an arrest warrant for the suspect identified as Mr. Blessed Omoobajesu. Mr. Omoobajesu committed a violent offense and engaged in conduct that posed a clear and immediate threat to public. Mr. Ommobajesu was taken into custody by the Tactical Enforcement Unit and was found to have a loaded handgun concealed in his waistband.

Title: Suspicious Circumstances
Summary: On Monday, 11/10/2025 at approximately 0702 hours, Officer Henderson/7780, Officer Dussan/7086 and Officer Mork/1763 were dispatched to the 3000 block of E. Bijou St. regarding a burglary in progress call for service. Upon arrival, officers witnessed two individuals step out of a white Ford Bronco and enter a residence for which they were dispatched. The vehicle was later determined to be reported stolen. Officers set up containment and with the assistance of TEU, K-9 and the drone unit provided verbal commands to the occupants of the residence. Numerous people exited the residence; officers arrested five individuals on outstanding felony and misdemeanor warrants and recovered one firearm. Officer Mork worked diligently speaking to the individuals as they exited the residence and established probable cause to arrest an additional two individuals responsible for the stolen vehicle.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 11/08/25 at approximately 3:00AM, patrol officers from the Colorado Springs Police Department’s Stetson Hills Division were dispatched to the area of the 5100 block of Barnes Road on a report of a single vehicle crash involving a motorcycle. It was determined that the operator of the motorcycle was travelling westbound on Barnes Road when the crash occurred. The operator of the motorcycle was transported to a local area hospital with serious but non-life-threatening injuries. Barnes Road was closed temporarily for eastbound traffic at Iron Horse Trail during the traffic crash investigation and was re-opened for all lanes of traffic on 11/08/25 at approximately 4:45AM.

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On November 6th, 2025 at 10:26 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department Communications Center received a call for service for a robbery at a gas station in the 2700 block of West Colorado Ave. Officers arrived and discovered that the suspect had left in a vehicle after threatening the clerk and stealing merchandise from the store. 

At approximately 10:39 PM, Ofc. Pieper conducted a traffic stop on a vehicle traveling on S. Nevada Ave. and E. Brookside St. with license plates that had expired in 2024. The driver, 59-year-old Sean Harvey, was arrested for an unrelated felony warrant. During his arrest, officers located methamphetamine in Harvey’s possession. Harvey was arrested for the warrant and new charges related to the Unlawful Distribution of a Controlled Substance (methamphetamine). During the traffic stop, officers learned that the vehicle had been used in the robbery.  The passenger, 46-year-old Ronald Dyer, was arrested for his involvement in the robbery.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 11/06/2025 at 3:28 AM, members of the Colorado Springs Police Department, Falcon Division were dispatched to the intersection of Nevada Ave. and Dublin Blvd. regarding a traffic crash.  Initial information indicated a single vehicle was southbound on Dublin at a high rate of speed.  The passenger of the vehicle was transported with serious injuries.  Due to the nature of the crash, the Major Crash Team was notified and responded to the scene and assumed the investigation.  The investigation is ongoing but the initial investigation indicates speed is a contributing factor.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 11-04-25, at approximately 1611 hours, a truck entered the parking lot at 1700 W. Uintah Street, made a right turn, and drove eastbound towards the building.  The vehicle accelerated to a high rate of speed and drove through a wall of the building. 5 people were injured and transported to local area hospitals with non-life-threatening injuries.  The driver was also transported to a local area hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. The driver of the vehicle was fully cooperative with officers, and our investigation indicates there was no criminal intent and this was an accident. Our investigation is ongoing.

Title: Burglary
Summary:
On November 3, 2025, at 3:04PM, the protected person in a domestic violence related protection order called the police reporting the restrained party had returned to the home and was damaging the home and assaulting the victim.  The victim locked themselves in a room and the suspect was attempting to break down the door.  Officers responded emergently, upon arrival the suspect would not open the door and officers forced entry.  The suspect did not comply with commands and force was used to restrain him and take him into custody.  The victim was located inside the home and treated on scene for injury.  The suspect was arrested for felony and misdemeanor charges related to the domestic incident and booked into CJC. 

During the initial contact with the suspect an officer was injured and transported to an area hospital for treatment for non-life threatening injury.  The officer was released.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 11/03/25 at approximately 03:23 AM, Sand Creek Shift III officers were dispatched to the 1100 block of Quivira Dr in reference to a domestic disturbance call for service. Upon arrival, officers heard an active disturbance in the basement and had to force entry into the residence. When officers entered the basement, they encountered a naked female victim and the suspect, who was sitting behind her. Officers gave the suspect commands, but he was uncooperative with officers. He was taken into custody and walked out to a marked police cruiser. While walking outside, the officers noticed the strong odor of gunpowder on his person. A live 10mm round was located on the floor next to where the male and female were located. A stolen 10mm Glock firearm was located near the couch that the victim and suspect were seated on. Officers were unable to determine where the suspect had fired the firearm. The suspect arrested was 32-year-old Donte Mayo.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 11/02/2025 at about 01500 hours officers from the Stetson Hills Division were dispatched to a call for help by medical personnel in the 4800 block of Briargate Pkwy. A patient had assaulted a paramedic before trying to commit suicide by jumping off the Powers Blvd bridge which had to be shut down southbound as well as eastbound Briargate Pkwy. Officers were able to talk him down and take him into custody.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 11/02/25 at 7:32AM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a call regarding multiple businesses in a shopping center had broken windows. When officers from the Sand Creek Division arrived they determined the damage was caused by gunfire. A similar situation occurred in this shopping center during the early morning hours of 10/26/25 (case #2025-00409888). Officers are actively working to identify suspects and their vehicles. It is unknown if anyone was injured in the shoot out. At least three businesses sustained damage from the gunfire.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 11/02/25 at approximately 0356 hours, officers were dispatched to a local hospital regarding a shooting. A male victim had walked into the Emergency Department, and it was discovered the victim had sustained at least gunshot wound. The victim was unable to provide additional details regarding the incident due to needing immediate medical care. The victim is currently in critical condition and the investigation is ongoing.

Title: Disturbance
Location: 1400 block of N. El Paso St
Summary:
**UPDATE**
As the result of this investigation, 33 year old Matthew Regnier was arrested for the charges of Murder in the First Degree, 2 counts of Criminal Attempt-Murder in the First Degree, 2 counts of Assault in the First Degree, and one count of Child Abuse.

On 10/31/2025 at approximately 11:52 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department Communication Center received a call about a disturbance in the 1400 block of N. El Paso Street.  When officers arrived they came in contact with one adult male, and two adult females who had all suffered stab wounds.  They also contacted an additional male and a young child inside the residence.  All of the people that were stabbed were transported to a local hospital for treatment.  The male party succumbed to his wounds at the hospital.  The two females were both treated for serious, life threatening injuries.  The second male was also transported to a local hospital for minor injuries and he was later transported to the Police Operations Center for further investigation.  The young child was not injured.
All parties involved in this incident have been contacted by police.
The CSPD Homicide Unit responded and assumed the investigation, which is still on-going.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: I-25 N, just south of Garden of the Gods Road
Summary: On 11/1/2025 at approximately 0043 hours, CSPD Falcon Division officers were assisting a disabled vehicle in the northbound lanes of traffic on Interstate 25 just south of Garden of the Gods Road. A vehicle traveling northbound struck the rear driver side quarter panel of a marked police cruiser that had its emergency lights activated. No one was injured as a result of the collision. The driver of TU1 was determined to be DUI and was cited.

Title: Robbery
Summary: At approximately 8:50pm on 10/31/2025 Officers with the Sand Creek division responded to a robbery in the 3800 block of E Pikes Peak Ave. A suspect entered the business and forcefully took a cash register containing various amounts of marked bills before leaving. No injuries were reported.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 10/31/2025 at approximately 03:20AM, officers with the Sand Creek Division responded to the 3100 block of Michigan Ave for a disturbance.  Upon arrival, officers were told someone had been shot inside the residence.  A suspect and victim were located inside the home.  The investigation showed no one had been shot.  Lorenzo Avila, a 42 year-old male, was arrested and booked at CJC for Attempted First Degree Murder and additional felony charges.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 10/30/2025, at approximately 5:04pm, officers from the Stetson Hills Division, were dispatched to the 3500 block of Cowhand Dr. to investigate a stabbing. Prior to officer arrival, the victim was transported to a local hospital with serious injuries. Investigation revealed the female victim was stabbed by the son of a male acquaintance. The suspect was identified as 36-year-old Christopher Sandoval. When officers arrived on scene, the suspect failed to exit the residence. Detectives responded and assumed the investigation. A search warrant was obtained for the residence, and an arrest warrant was obtained for the suspect. The suspect was taken into custody without incident by members of the Tactical Enforcement Unit and transported to the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 10/30/2025 at 12:15pm several Colorado Springs Police Department units, including Armed Violent Offender, Strategic Investigations, Burglary, Robbery, K9, and Tactical Enforcement, assisted in an investigation stemming from a shooting in 2023.  Joshua Saucier DOB: 09/30/2005 was taken into custody by detectives from the Armed Violent Offender Unit and K9 unit for 11 counts of attempted first degree murder and unlawful discharge of a firearm.  Several other detectives and officers served search warrants related to this investigation.  A handgun and other weapons related items were recovered.

Title: Assault
Summary:
On 10-29-2025 at approximately 1807hrs officers from the Falcon Division were dispatched to the 7100 Block of Commerce Center Dr. to investigate a stabbing.  Initial information indicated an unknow person had been stabbed, while a possible suspect remained inside the building.  The stabbed male was contacted and later identified as 45-year-old Vaughn Oseth.  He was transported to a local hospital for treatment of non-life-threatening injuries.  Officers later contacted the other male inside the building and detained him after a brief struggle.  Investigation revealed Oseth had physically assaulted the other male with a deadly weapon before being stabbed.

Oseth was later booked into CJC for Second Degree Assault.  CSPD AVO was notified of the investigation.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Tuesday, October 28, 2025, at approximately 9:00pm, The Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a motor vehicle theft which occurred in the 500 blk West of Garden of the Gods Road. The owner of a dark silver, 1998 Toyota Corolla, left his vehicle in this area running, with the key in the ignition. When he returned, the vehicle had been stolen.
On Wednesday, October 29, 2025, at 5:45pm, Detectives of the MVT unit located the above stolen vehicle in the area of East Fountain Boulevard and South Chelton Road. Detectives began following the vehicle and later identified the driver as Nickolaus Taylor age 33. When Taylor parked the vehicle behind an abandoned building in the 2900 blk of Mallard Drive, detectives stopped Taylor and arrested him for Motor Vehicle Theft and Felony Eluding. Taylor was transported to the Criminal Justice Center for booking.

Title: MVT
Summary: On 10/28/25 at 7:23AM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a call from a victim of a motor vehicle theft which had occurred in the 2300 block of Willow Tree Grove. The victim had left their unoccupied vehicle running assuming if the key fob was not with the vehicle it couldn't be taken. Officers from the Sand Creek Division responded to investigate the theft. They located the vehicle near Tulane/Fairmont, and after doing a neighborhood canvas the officers narrowed down where the suspect(s) could be in the 100 block of University Dr. While completing the neighborhood canvas officers learned that the victim's debit card which had been in the vehicle, had been used at a couple of businesses near Pikes Peak/Academy. Following up with those businesses resulted in getting photos of two suspects. Officers made contact with an apartment in the 100 block of University Dr. and arrested two people. Ashley Steele (40) was arrested for identity theft. Richard Deanda (25) was arrested for motor vehicle theft, identity theft and resisting arrest. Deanda was a 7-time convicted motor vehicle thief, had an outstanding warrant for motor vehicle theft and was on parole for motor vehicle theft. With winter weather bearing down on us, this situation is a good reminder to know the limitations of your vehicle's key fobs and/remote start systems.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 9/14/25, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) responded to a reported personal robbery in the 3600 block of West Colorado Ave.   There officers learned two male suspects, one armed with a handgun, attempted to carjack an elderly female, but when they could not take her car, they robbed her of personal items.  On 9/15/25, CSPD responded to another report of an armed carjacking in the 100 block of West Colorado Ave.  There officers learned that two males again, one armed with a handgun, robbed an adult female of her vehicle.  The CSPD Robbery Unit took over the investigations and identified one suspect as 35-year-old, Michael Mitchell.  Mitchell was arrested for the two above cases on 10/8/25, while he was in custody at the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center on an unrelated charge. The second suspect was identified as 32-year-old, Nicholas Adams.  An arrest warrant was issued for Adams at the beginning of October for the above crimes.  On 10/28/25, members of the Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force developed information on Adams’s whereabouts.  Fugitive detectives worked with members of the Robbery Unit, Tactical Enforcement Unit and K-9 to take Adams into custody without incident.  Adams was arrested in the 3100 block of West Colorado Avenue.

Title: Assault
Summary: Officers from the Stetson Hills Division responded to multiple calls regarding shots fired.  The investigation revealed the victim was entering her vehicle and her estranged husband confronted her in the parking lot.  He fired several rounds in her direction as she fled.  DVASA was contacted and assumed the investigation.

Title: Robbery
Summary: At approximately 12:10 AM on 10/28/2025, officers with the Stetson Hills division responded to a robbery of a business in the 3500 block of N. Academy Blvd. A suspect entered the business with a weapon and demanded money before leaving. No injuries occurred.

Title: Bomb Threat
Summary: Shortly after 03:00 pm, school staff of a school in the 4100 block of Bardot Dr received a phone call with a bomb threat to the school. Officers from the Stetson Hills division as well as the drone unit responded and did not locate any explosive devices. A juvenile suspect was contacted during the investigation and taken into custody for the bomb threat. It is believed that the phone call was only a prank.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 10/27/25 at 9:12AM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a call from a citizen saying their adult daughter was actively involved in a domestic violence situation at a home in the 2700 block of Marcy Ave. The details were limited. When officers from the Sand Creek Division arrived on scene, they contacted the woman involved in the domestic. Her and her children exited the residence to safety. The boyfriend/suspect was shouting suicidal/homicidal statements. The woman informed officers her boyfriend had made comments about committing "suicide by cop". While officers were formulating a plan to arrest the suspect he was seen running from the rear of the home by a CSPD drone pilot. Officers pursued the suspect through the neighborhood. He failed to comply with orders to stop and an officer from the Tactical Enforcement Unit fired pepper ball rounds to gain compliance. Jerry Maclin (28yo) was arrested for assault, harassment, obstructing a peace officer and resisting arrest.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: The CSPD communication center was notified of a traffic collision on Dublin Blvd west of Cloud Dancer Dr. Upon arrival of emergency personnel, it was discovered that TU1 had lost control, exited the roadway and struck a tree in the center median. The single occupant of that vehicle was declared deceased on scene. A second vehicle had been struck via sideswipe by TU1 prior to leaving the roadway. No injuries were reported from the single occupant of TU2. The roadway was shut down for the duration of the on-scene investigation and was reopened at approximately 12:50 A.M.

Title: Warrant
Summary: On Sunday, 10/26/2025 at approximately 0652 hours, Officer Henderson/7780 was conducting proactive policing in the 2400 block of E. Monument St. when he located a silver, BMW belonging to a violent felon who had multiple felony domestic violence charges.  The investigation revealed the suspect entered a residence in the 2400 block of E. Monument St. Containment was set up by Sand Creek Patrol Officers. SGT Reed/5415 took command of the call for service at which point TEU officers were notified of the potential barricaded suspect. TEU officers responded to the address and after the receipt of the search warrant, began providing verbal commands to the occupants of the dwelling. The victim and an innocent bystander exited the residence unharmed. The suspect later exited the residence without further incident.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: E. Woodmen Rd. and Banning Lewis Parkway
Summary: On Saturday, October 25, 2025, at approximately 10:50PM the Colorado Springs Police Department received a call for service regarding an accident that occurred and one of the vehicles involved was on fire. Officers arrived on scene and located a vehicle fully engulfed in flames. The two occupants of this vehicle were able to exit the vehicle with the assistance of citizens who stopped and helped. The passenger of this vehicle was transported to a local hospital with serious injuries.  The driver of the other vehicle involved fled the area on foot prior to police arrival. The crash is still under investigation but it appears both speed and alcohol are contributing factors.

Title: Burglary
Summary: On 10/24/2025 at approximately 02:34AM Sand Creek Shift III officers were dispatched to a burglary call for service in the 2200 block of East Dale St. Investigation determined that the suspect, Fernando Juarez 02/27/1980, came to the residence looking for his son. He forced his way into the residence through the front door and then pointed a handgun at the two victims. He forced the two victims outside into a vehicle at gunpoint where all of the vehicle occupants were contacted by officers. The suspect was charged with 1st degree burglary and kidnapping. The two victims were taken to jail on unrelated warrants.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 10/23/25 at approximately 22:21PM, Sand Creek Shift III officers were dispatched to the 2800 block of Casden Cir in regard to a disturbance call for service. Investigation revealed that the victim, who is the caregiver for one of the residents at the address engaged in a verbal argument with the suspect. The suspect retrieved a baseball bat and swung the bat at the victim’s head. The suspect missed and struck a wall in the residence. The suspect then made threats to kill the victim and other people in the residence before leaving the residence in a vehicle. The suspect was later contacted in a vehicle at a different location and taken into custody without incident.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 10/14/2025 at approximately 1753 hours, Colorado Springs Police Dispatch received multiple calls in reference to a possible head on collision in the area of Platte Ave and Wooten Rd. Upon officers’ arrival it was discovered the two vehicles had collided in the westbound lanes of Platte Ave. The driver of the eastbound sedan was unconscious and transported to a local hospital for further medical evaluation. The driver of the westbound pickup was also transported for medical evaluation. Both drivers were reported to have received serious injuries as a result of the collision. Both directions of Platte Ave. were temporarily shut down, and traffic was diverted while the investigation concluded. At approximately 10:00 PM all traffic lanes were re-opened.

Title: Other
Summary:
On 10/23/2025 at approximately 7:55AM the Colorado Springs Fire Department responded to a call for service in the 700 block of N Tejon regarding a structure fire call for service.  When CSFD arrived, the fire had been extinguished.  Members of the CSFD Fire Investigation Unit, and the Colorado Springs Regional Explosives Unit (REU) were contacted for assistance.  The CSFD Fire Investigation Unit completes preliminary origin and cause investigation to all suspicious fires.   The Regional Explosives Unit is the investigating unit for criminal fires for the Colorado Springs Police Department. 

The preliminary investigation revealed that an unknown suspect intentionally set the fire causing several thousand dollars’ worth of damage.

This is an active investigation, if anyone has any information, please contact CSPD at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you can call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On 10-23-25 at approximately 0402 hours, Officers of the Sand Creek Division were dispatched to a personal Robbery. The victim had been Robbed and Assaulted by several subjects. Officers were able to locate the suspects and vehicle a short time later. The suspects fled in the vehicle when officers tried to stop them. Officers did not pursue. A short time later, the suspect vehicle was found crashed and abandoned. The suspect vehicle was a stolen vehicle taken from El Paso County. Officers were able to set up a perimeter in the area and eventually located 3 suspects with the assistance of UAV and K9. All 3 suspects were juveniles and arrested for several charges. 
The 3 suspects were also suspects in a Burglary to a Motor Vehicle in the Gold Hill Division before the Robbery.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Tuesday, October 21, 2025, the Colorado Springs Police Department began investigating a motor vehicle theft. The vehicle was described as a white 2000 Subaru Legacy, which was reported stolen from the 300 block of North Murray Boulevard. 
On Wednesday, October 22, 2025, the motor vehicle theft unit initiated surveillance of the stolen vehicle. Detectives observed the vehicle being driven by an individual later identified as Rico Ramirez, age 37. During the surveillance, detectives observed Ramirez shoplifting from a Marshalls Department store. 
A marked police vehicle attempted to stop the stolen Subaru near the intersection of North Academy Boulevard and Goddard Street, but he failed to stop and fled from officers. 
The stolen Subaru was later parked in the 2800 block of South Circle Drive. Detectives observed Ramirez walking away from the vehicle. He was arrested, then taken to the Criminal Justice Center and charged with Motor Vehicle Theft, Eluding and Shoplifting.

Title: Robbery
Summary: At approximately 01:20AM, officers with the Sand Creek Division responded to a robbery of a business in the 3300 block of E. Fountain Blvd.  A suspect entered the business with a weapon and demanded money before leaving.  No one was injured.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 10/21/25, at approximately 0800 hours, members of the Colorado Springs Police Department Fugitives Unit located, 47-year-old, Maurice Keener in the 1100 block of Interquest Parkway.  Keener was wanted for a bank robbery which occurred on 10/8/25 in the downtown area of Colorado Springs.  Keener attempted to flee from Fugitive Detectives but was taken into custody after a short foot pursuit.  Robbery Detectives responded to assist and served a search warrant on Keener’s hotel room.  There detectives found evidence linking Keener to the October 8th bank robbery as well as evidence linking him to a bank robbery from 10/20/25 that occurred in the 400 block of S. Cascade Ave, documented under ETACS 35707.  These investigations are ongoing.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On 10/20/25, at approximately 1043 hours, officers of the Colorado Springs Police Department were dispatched to a reported robbery at a financial institution in the 400 block of S. Cascade Avenue.  Both patrol officers and detectives from the Robbery Unit responded to the scene.  At the scene law enforcement learned that a male suspect entered the bank and demanded money from the employees before fleeing the scene with an undisclosed amount of cash.  This investigation is ongoing.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 10/19/25 at approximately 10:52PM Sand Creek officers received a call of a traffic crash in the 500 block of South circle drive. Investigation determined that a black Mitsubishi sedan was traveling southbound in the 500 block of South circle drive. A white Chevrolet Corvette was northbound in the 500 block of South circle drive. The black sedan drifted across the center lane of traffic into the northbound lanes and struck the Corvette head on. Both drivers and a passenger in the Corvette were transported by ambulance to a local area hospital for treatment. It was determined that the driver of the black sedan was driving under the influence and the passenger of the Corvette had serious non-life-threatening bodily injury.

Title: Death
Summary: On 10/19/2025 at 4:51 PM officers from the Gold Hill Division were dispatched to a shooting in the 2000 block of Southgate Rd. Upon arrival they located a deceased adult male on scene. Detectives from the Homicide Unit responded to assume the investigation. Multiple witnesses were interviewed and at this time there is no suspect information to provide. This is an active investigation, if anyone has any information please contact CSPD at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you can call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 10/19/25 at approximately 0200 hours, officers from the Gold Hill Division were dispatched to Memorial Hospital Central in reference to a stabbing victim that walked into the emergency room lobby. The victim indicated that he was stabbed by an unknown male suspect at a nearby business; however, he was unable to identify a possible suspect. He was also unable to articulate what the suspect’s motive was. The victim received medical treatment at the hospital and it was determined his injuries were non-life threatening. The investigation into this incident is ongoing.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: N. Powers Blvd at Palmer Park Blvd
Summary: On 10/18/25 at approximately 10:47PM, the CSPD Communications Center was notified of an auto pedestrian traffic crash at the intersection of N. Powers Blvd and Palmer Park Blvd. The adult male pedestrian was transported from the scene by medical personnel to an area hospital with serious but non-life-threatening injuries. Driver impairment is not believed to be a factor in the crash.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Location: E Fountain Bl / Academy Park Loop
Summary: On 10/18/2025 at approximately 2:17 AM, the Colorado Springs Police Department was notified of a traffic crash that occurred at the intersection of E Fountain Blvd and Academy Park Loop. When emergency responders arrived on scene, they found an occupant of one of the vehicles was deceased. Two occupants of the other vehicle were transported to a local hospital with serious injuries. Due to the nature of the crash, the Colorado Springs Police Department's Major Crash Team responded and assumed the investigation.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On October 18, 2025, at 10:43PM, an employee at a business in the 1800 block of South Nevada Avenue reported a stabbing.  On scene investigation revealed a group of suspects entered the store and began stealing merchandise and left the store without rendering payment.  The store employee confronted them and was stabbed by one of the suspects, while another suspect physically assaulted the store employee.  The suspects fled on foot and were not located.  The suspects are described as juvenile white and Hispanic males, 13-18 years of age, wearing dark clothing.

Title: Other
Summary:
On 10/17/2025 at about 6:19PM the Colorado Springs Police Department received a report of shots fired in the area of 4300 Montebello Drive.  While enroute, another call was received indicating that additional rounds had been fired and muzzle flash had been observed in an apartment at 4372 Montebello Drive.   Containment was set up, and dialogue was established with the lone occupant of the apartment.  A shelter in place was sent out for the area and requests were made for the occupant to exit the apartment which he refused.  The Occupant of the apartment was obviously intoxicated and continually refused to exit the apartment or to allow officers to remove the firearms from it.   After approximately 2 hours of negotiating with the suspect, he agreed to exit the apartment and was taken into custody without incident.  The Shelter in place was cancelled at approximately 8:37PM

The investigation revealed that approximately 21 rounds had been fired in the apartment and multiple firearms were collected during the execution of a search warrant.  There were no injuries to anyone during this incident and the suspect was booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center under the charges of Illegal Discharge, a class 5 felony, Prohibited Use of Weapons, a class 2 misdemeanor, and Reckless Endangerment, also a class 2 misdemeanor.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Tuesday, September 16th, 2025, in the 1900 blk of Sussex Lane, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a stolen, 2015 red Yamaha motorcycle which was taken without permission. 
On Monday, September 22nd, 2025, detectives from the Motor Vehicle Theft Unit were conducting a BATTLE deployment. During this deployment, detectives located the stolen Yamaha in the 100 blk of South Brentwood Drive. During surveillance, the driver of this motorcycle was able to avoid capture. 
On Thursday, October 16th, 2025, at approximately 10:00 PM, members of the MVT unit located the stolen Yamaha in the 2100 block of Lelaray Street. Through the investigation, detectives were able to charge Deen Kellams age 40, and his brother Tomasz Kellams age 39, with motor vehicle theft. Furthermore, detectives learned Tomasz is a multi-convicted, motor vehicle theft prolific offender.

Title: MVT
Location: North Powers Boulevard and North Carefree Circle
Summary:
On Wednesday, October 15, 2025, the Pueblo Police department investigated a 2013 white Hyundai Accent which was stolen out of their jurisdiction.  This stolen vehicle traveled to the Colorado Springs area where detectives of the Motor Vehicle Theft unit located the vehicle near the intersection of North Carefree Circle and North Academy Boulevard. 
Detectives conducted surveillance of this vehicle and followed them to North Powers Boulevard and North Carefree Circle. Once in this area, the MVT unit grappled the stolen vehicle which caused the vehicle to stop. The driver later identified as Gabriel Cervantez age 25, surrendered to detectives; however, the passenger later identified as Dante Salazar age 26, fled on foot but was captured after a short foot pursuit.  Through the investigation, detectives observed both Salazar and Cervantez driving the stolen vehicle. Detectives photographed both suspects and charged them both with Motor Vehicle Theft and obstruction. Cervantez is a convicted MVT offender, and both were transported to the Criminal Justice Center for booking.
Adults Arrested: Gabriel Cervantez age 25  & Dante Salazar age 26

Title: Other
Summary: On 10-15-25, at approximately 1315 hours, the subject, Henry Gibson, was walking in the area of E. Cheyenne Rd/Warren Ave.  He was carrying a handgun in a bag when the handgun discharged and struck him in the leg.  Gibson was transported to a local area hospital for non-life threatening injuries.  During the investigation, probable cause was established to charge him with felony and misdemeanor charges.  All involved parties have been accounted for.

Title: Other
Summary:
On Wednesday, October 15, 2025, at approximately 10:43 a.m., officers with the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) responded to a report of a domestic violence protection order violation at a residence in the 7400 block of Gorgeted Quail Grove.


Upon arrival, officers encountered a suspect who barricaded himself inside the home. Tactical Enforcement Unit (TEU) and K9 personnel were deployed to assist in safely resolving the situation.


During the entry into the residence, K9 Roam and his handler, along with TEU officers, made contact with the suspect. During the confrontation, K9 Roam was stabbed multiple times. He was immediately transported to a local emergency veterinary facility, where he remains in critical condition and is undergoing surgery.


No officers were injured during the incident. The suspect was later struck with a chemical irritant and tased before he was able to be taken into custody. Due to the severity of the attack on K9 Roam, the CSPD Homicide Unit is leading the investigation into the attempted killing of a police service animal.

The suspect has been identified as 37-year-old Anthony Bryant

Title: Assault
Location: N Murray Blvd & S Carefree Cir
Summary:
UPDATE: On the morning of Wednesday, October 15, the Armed Violent Offenders (AVO) Unit assumed responsibility for the ongoing investigation. With support from the Strategic Investigations Unit (SIU), detectives identified a suspect connected to the recent FFL burglary in El Paso County.

On October 16, AVO detectives obtained a search warrant for an apartment located at 1690 N. Murray Boulevard. During the search, investigators located a second of three juvenile suspects believed to be involved in the burglary. The suspect was wearing clothing that matched what was seen in surveillance footage from the incident.

Further investigation revealed that the primary suspect associated with the apartment was attending school at the time. Detectives coordinated with school administrators to locate the juvenile, who was offsite on a field trip. Upon returning to campus, the suspect was alerted by another student and fled on foot. 

During the ensuing foot pursuit, the suspect attempted to carjack multiple occupied vehicles. A school employee, who had followed detectives in an effort to assist, was approached by the suspect. The juvenile entered the employee’s vehicle and attempted to drive away. The employee resisted, preventing the vehicle from being put into gear. Detectives quickly intervened, removed the suspect from the vehicle, and took him into custody without further incident.

The juvenile taken into custody at the apartment was charged with second-degree burglary, motor vehicle theft, and criminal mischief. In addition to these charges, the juvenile who fled from detectives was also charged with attempted robbery and kidnapping.

Investigation into the shooting which preceded the burglary is still under investigation. AVO detectives also located a third stolen vehicle used during the burglary and recovered it on October 16th. 

(Original Blotter Entry)
On Wednesday, October 15, 2025, at 3:07 AM, the public safety communications center received the report of a shooting near the intersection of N Murray Blvd and S Carefree Cir. Officers contacted the reporting party in the 3800 block of Maizeland Rd. The reporting party (victim) advised that he observed suspicious activity involving two vehicles in the parking lot on the northeast corner of the intersection of Maizeland Rd and N Academy Blvd. The victim believed the occupants to be "casing" one of the businesses in preparation for committing a burglary when they were spooked by the victim, who was observing their actions. The suspect vehicles then fled the area east on Maizeland Rd. The victim followed in his vehicle. The suspects then made a left onto northbound N Murray Blvd. The victim followed onto N Murray Blvd. As the victim approached S Carefree Cir on Murray, he observed one of the vehicles (a dark sedan) turn left and head west on S Carefree Cir. The other vehicle, a dark colored boxy vehicle, was stopped in the Murray roadway. The driver fired multiple gunshots at the victim. Officers observed multiple defects in the engine compartment and the front driver and passenger compartments. The victim was not struck but had a defect in the top of his baseball cap, indicating he was nearly struck by one of the projectiles. Officers responded to the intersection of Murray and S Carefree and located/recovered multiple fired cartridge casings.

While attempting to identify the suspect vehicles, officers learned of a smash-and-grab burglary at a commercial business with an FFL license in the 300 block of Main St (Security, CO). Deputies from EPSO responded and secured the scene. The dark sedan had been used to back into the business, crashing through the front door. Officers responded and impounded the dark colored sedan. 

The investigation is in the early stages. No arrests have been made at this time.

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On Tuesday, October 14, 2025, around noon, Custer County Sheriff’s Office investigated a car jacking in the 1100 blk of Broadway Street, Silver Cliff Colorado. The suspect who was armed with a handgun took the victim’s gray, 2022 Mitsubishi Outlander.
The suspect drove to Colorado Springs and continued the crime spree. At approximately 1:23pm, near the intersection of South Murray Boulevard and East Fountain Boulevard the suspect pointed the gun at a CDOT employee who was working on the roadway. At approximately 2:30pm, in the 200 Blk of North Union Boulevard, this suspect pointed the handgun at the victim and attempted another carjacking. At approximately 3:37pm, in the Memorial Park area, the suspect attempted another carjacking, again pointing the gun at the victim. At approximately 4:09 pm, near the intersection of East Fountain Boulevard and South Circle Drive, the suspect pointed his gun at several people as he was trying to get another vehicle. The suspect subsequently crossed the street and carjacked a car from a mother and her three children after he fired his gun at the mother. Sand Creek Patrol entered the area and after a short Vehicular pursuit, the suspect was taken into custody in the 1100 Blk of Academy Park Loop. 

The juvenile suspect, age 15, was taken to a detention facility after the Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives investigated this incident. 
The juvenile was charged with the following:
1)	Attempted First Degree Murder 
2)	2 counts of Aggravated Robbery
3)	3 counts Attempted Aggravated Robbery
4)	2 counts of Motor Vehicle Theft
5)	10 counts of Felony Menacing 
6)	Vehicular eluding
7)	Illegal Possession of a Handgun by Juvenile 
8)	4 counts of Reckless Endangerment
9)	Obstruction

Title: Assault
Summary:
Officers from the Sand Creek Division responded to a domestic violence call for service.  While en route an additional call came in reporting a shooting.  When officers arrived they located a adult female suffering from a gunshot wound.  She was transported to a local hospital for treatment of serious but not life threatening injuries.  The suspect fled the scene and has not been located at this time. 
Detectives from the Domestic Violence Unit responded to the scene and assumed the investigation.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 10/12/2025 at 9:27PM the Colorado Springs Police Department received a call regarding a single vehicle crash in Memorial Park involving a vehicle versus a tree.  Responding officers located the scene and found an unconscious victim that had suffered life threating SBI.  Due to the nature of the crash, the MCT was notified and responded to the scene and assumed the investigation.  The investigation is ongoing but speed appears to be a contributing factor in the crash.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 10/11/2025 Officers with the Gold Hill Division of the Colorado Springs Police Department were dispatched to the 1000 block of E Vermijo Ave for a report of shots fired from a residence. Officers made contact with the subject who exited the residence and surrendered peacefully to officers. A subsequent search warrant was executed of the residence securing multiple firearms from the home. The suspect was transported to a local hospital for treatment while officers completed the investigation. Charges are pending at this time.

Title: Other
Summary: On 10/11/2025 a Colorado Springs Police Officer was working in the area of 1200 block of Interquest Parkway when the approached a white male who was attempting to pick pocket patrons. When the officer contacted the male, he attempted to disarm the police officer of his gun. The male was taken into custody without further incident.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On 10/10/25 at approximately 2345 hours, the CSPD Communications Center received a call about a traffic crash involving a vehicle striking a house.  Responding officers located the scene and discovered the driver of the vehicle was deceased.  Due to the nature of the call, the MCT was notified, responded to the scene and assumed the investigation. There were no injuries to any of the residents in the occupied house.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Monday, October 06, 2025, Community Service Officer T. Taylor investigated a motor vehicle theft of a gray, 2000 Toyota Tundra. This vehicle was taken from the victim’s driveway with the doors unlocked and the key left with the vehicle. 

On Wednesday, October 08, 2025, at 10:40pm, an Officer with the Sand Creek Division attempted to stop this stolen vehicle; it fled the officer at a high rate of speed and turned off the vehicle’s headlights. 

On Thursday, October 9, 2025, Officers Cropper and Mibert located this stolen vehicle near the intersection of Astrozon Boulevard and Hancock Expressway.  These two officers, with the assistance of tactical units, followed the suspect later identified as Kameron Martinez age 24, to the 1900 Blk of South Chelton Road. After a brief struggle, Martinez was taken into custody. 

MVT Detective Gonzalez, interviewed Martinez who is a multi-motor vehicle theft prolific offender with several convictions of MVT. Martinez was transported to the Criminal Justice Center for the charges of MVT, felony eluding, obstruction and distribution of narcotics  fentanyl.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Tuesday, September 02, 2025, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a stolen 2022 Chevrolet Trailblazer which was taken from the 200 blk of North Academy Boulevard. During the investigation, officers learned the keys were left in the vehicle. 
On Wednesday, October 8, 2025, the Motor Vehicle Theft unit located the 2022 Chevrolet in the 1900 blk of Aerotech Drive. An officer tried to stop the driver, later identified as Mario   Rascon age 31, with a marked police unit; however, Rascon fled officers in this stolen vehicle. Detectives began surveillance of the stolen vehicle which drove to the area of Irving Lane and Ridenour Drive, where he was contacted by MVT detectives. He also had an outstanding warrant for felony menacing with a deadly weapon.  Rascon was later transported to the Criminal Justice Center for the charges of Motor Vehicle Theft and vehicular eluding.

Title: Assault
Summary: On October 8, 2025 at approximately 0240 hours, Officers checking the area located a wanted person.  When they went to detain him, he fled on foot from officers who quickly caught up to him.  As officers grabbed him, they saw he had a knife on his person and he began to physically resist the officers, striking two of them, causing bodily injury.  Officers took the suspect into custody and he was transported to a local hospital for treatment for injuries sustained during his arrest.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 10/8/25 at 12:17am, officers from the Falcon Division were dispatched to the 8100 block of N Academy Blvd for a report of subjects passed out in a vehicle. AMR arrived on scene and the vehicle left the area and was observed by responding officers southbound on N Academy. Officers caught up to the vehicle westbound on Woodmen Rd and attempted a stop. The vehicle fled the stop and crashed into another vehicle at I25 and Woodmen Rd. The driver of the suspect vehicle attempted to flee on foot but was apprehended on scene.

Title: MVT
Summary: On Tuesday, October 7, 2025, detectives of the Colorado Springs Police Department, Motor Vehicle Theft Unit, with the help of a K-9 officer, located a stolen motor vehicle in the 700 blk of Citadel Drive East. The driver, later identified as Arthur Tolley age 52, was operating the stolen 2025 Kia Telluride which was reported stolen on 10/03/2025. Through the investigation, detectives discovered this vehicle was involved in numerous other criminal incidents, which included multiple events of theft from retail stores. Detectives conducted surveillance on the Kia, which led to a traffic stop in the area of Galley Road and North Academy Boulevard, where the Kia failed to stop and fled from the officer. Detectives were able to continue surveillance as the vehicle eventually traveled to the area of East Jackson Street and Wahsatch Avenue. Tactical Officers attempted to contact the stolen Kia; however, the Kia fled from this contact where it traveled to the area of Rebecca Lane and North Academy Boulevard. Detectives located the vehicle and found Mr. Tolley and a female, Barbara Tolley age 50, a short distance away. Arthur was arrested for Motor Vehicle Theft and two counts of Vehicular Eluding.

Title: MVT
Location: 3900 blk of East San Miguel Street
Summary:
On Thursday, October 02, 2025, members of the Colorado Springs Police Department responded to a carjacking in the 500 blk of South Nevada Avenue. The vehicle stolen during the robbery was a blue, 2006 Jeep Liberty. 
On Monday, October 06, 2025, members of the Colorado Springs Police Department, El Paso County Sheriff’s Office and the Colorado State Patrol participated in a BATTLE operation. During this BATTLE deployment, a detective of the Motor Vehicle Theft unit located the stolen Jeep near the intersection of East Platte Avenue and North Murray Boulevard. Surveillance units responded and followed the vehicle to the 3900 blk of East San Miguel Street. Tactical Units responded to this location as well and after developing a systematic plan, all four individuals were taken into custody. 
The three robbery suspects were, Travis Butler, age 37, Brittany Flores, age 38, and Tervon Harvey, age 35. Butler was charged with Robbery, Motor vehicle Theft, and Possession of a Weapon by Previous Offender, Flores was charged with Robbery and Motor vehicle Theft, and Harvey was charged with Robbery, Motor vehicle Theft and felony possession of fentanyl. Andre Burns, age 45, was arrested for an outstanding arrest warrant. 
Several items of evidence were recovered during this arrest which included a 9mm semi-automatic handgun.
Adults Arrested: Travis Butler, age 37, Brittany Flores, age 38, and Tervon Harvey, age 35 and Andre Burns, age 45

Title: Suspicious Circumstances
Summary:
On 10/6/2025 at about 7:16AM, the Colorado Springs Police Communications Center received a call for service regarding a suspicious package in the 2100 block Southgate Rd.  Officers responded and secured the area.  Detectives from the Regional Explosives Unit responded and discovered that the suspicious package was a hoax device.  Detectives will continue to investigate to identify the person responsible for leaving the package.

This is an active investigation, if anyone has any information, please contact CSPD at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you can call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: Death
Summary: On October 5, 2025 at approximately 1:58AM, officers were dispatched to the 3500 block of North Academy Bl in regards to a shooting.  Upon arrival officers located two adult males suffering from at least one gunshot wound. One of the males was transported to a local hospital with life threatening injuries.  The other male was deceased on scene.  Detectives from the homicide unit responded and assumed the investigation to include the development of suspect information.  This is an active investigation, if anyone has any information please contact CSPD at (719) 444-7000. If you wish to remain anonymous, you can call the Crime Stoppers Tip Line at (719) 634-STOP (7867) or 1-800-222-8477.

Title: Other
Summary: On 10/04/25 at 8:28AM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a call regarding a road rage situation. The caller/victim reported a male had thrown a soda at their vehicle and also brandished a handgun. Officers from the Sand Creek Division conducted an investigation, locating the suspect in the Widefield area. During a search of the suspect's vehicle a handgun was recovered. Dominic Vigil (33yo) was arrested for Felony Menacing.

Title: Assault
Summary:
On 10/4/2025 at approximately 0445 hours, Officers responded to an in progress family disturbance in the 4900 block of Mark Dabling Blvd. While arriving in the area, the suspect was seen walking away from the residence. The subject was not compliant with officer instructions and became combative when detained. A struggle ensued and an officer was punched in the face by the suspect. A taser was utilized during the struggle but was not effective.  Additional officers arrived on scene, and the subject was taken into custody. 

Further investigation revealed the suspect had strangled another family member and was fleeing the scene when contacted by officers. The suspect was charged with two counts of second-degree assault and obstruction of a peace officer and booked into the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center. The injured officer was treated at a local hospital and released.

Title: Pursuit
