Summary: On 10/04/2025, at approximately 01:06 AM, the Colorado Springs Police Department Dispatch received a report of a shooting in the 2400 block of N. Union Blvd.  When Stetson Hills Division Officers arrived on scene, they located a female who sustained a gunshot wound to her abdomen.  A description of the shooter and the associated vehicle was identified, and last reported traveling southbound from the business. At approximately 01:21 AM, Officers from the Sand Creek Division located the related vehicle and the shooting suspect.  When officers attempted a traffic stop, the shooting suspect and sole occupant of the car fled officers.  A force stop method was authorized.  Sand Creek Officers conducted a PIT maneuver near E. Pikes Peak Ave and S. Roosevelt St., causing the vehicle to come to a stop in the 00 100 block of S. Roosevelt St.  The driver/shooting suspect was taken into custody without incident.  The victim was transported to a local hospital, where she was treated for non-life-threatening injuries and released.  The CSPD Assault Unit responded to the scene to assume the investigation.

Title: MVT
Summary:
On Tuesday, September 30th, 2025, officers of the Aurora Police Department investigated a stolen Gray 2006 Toyota Corolla. This theft occurred in the Aurora city limits, and the vehicle was left unlocked with the keys in the ignition. 
On Friday, October 3, 2025, Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives located this vehicle in the area of East Fountain Boulevard and South Chelton Road.  A marked police vehicle attempted to stop this stolen vehicle; however, the vehicle fled at a high rate of speed failing to stop for police.
Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives conducted surveillance and located the stolen vehicle near the intersection of Boggs Place and Preuss Road. Detectives contacted 3 individuals, the driver Tianna Quin age 25, and passengers, Mandy-Lee Miller age 26 and Lela Edwards age 55. Quin and Miller were both charged with Motor Vehicle Theft and Edwards had multiple warrants for her arrest. All three were transported to the Criminal Justice Center for booking. Quin is a convicted MVT prolific offender.
Adults Arrested: Tianna Quin age 25, and passengers, Mandy-Lee Miller age 26 and Lela Edwards age 55

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary:
On 10-03-2025at approximately 1533hrs Stetson Hills officers were dispatched to the area of N. Powers Blvd. and Research Pkwy. to investigate a traffic crash.  Upon arriving on scene it was determined a single vehicle was southbound N. Powers Blvd. approaching Research Pkwy. when the driver lost control and left the west side of the roadway.  The vehicle rolled several times and the  34 year old male driver and sole occupant was ejected.

  The driver sustained serious bodily injury and was transported to a local hospital for treatment.  The crash is still under investigation but it appears speed and alcohol are factors.

Charges are pending.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 10/3/2025, Officer Dan Mork located a Ram 1500 pick-up for wanted suspect Anthony Kent in unincorporated El Paso County. Ofc. Mork requested the assistance of the Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force to assist with surveillance on Kent’s vehicle. The vehicle moved from this location and travelled throughout various locations of Colorado Springs before parking in an alley off Security Blvd. Using the assistance of the EPSO drone, CSPD Tac Team, and CSPD K-9 an apprehension plan was put in place. When it was advantageous, units moved in to contact Kent. Kent was observed pulling out a handgun but then dropped it and fled on foot. Kent was apprehended after a short foot pursuit. During the subsequent search of Kent’s vehicle an additional 2 handguns, 1 short-barreled rifle, 1 rifle upper, and a semi-automatic shotgun were recovered. One of the handguns recovered was reported stolen under CSPD case #25-280703. Multiple large capacity magazines, holsters, and ammunition were found inside of the vehicle. A locked safe with a baggy sticking out of the side was also recovered. A small amount of illegal narcotics were recovered inside of the vehicle.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: N. Powers Blvd and Barnes Rd
Summary: On 10/03/2025 at approximately 1240 hours, officers from the Stetson Hills Division were dispatched to N. Powers Blvd north of Barnes Rd. for a traffic crash investigation. Upon officers' arrival, it was determined that a vehicle traveling north, stalled in the left lane resulting in three separate crashes with a total of 12 vehicles involved. No one was transported to a hospital and no serious injuries were reported.

Title: Other
Summary:
The Colorado Springs Police Department will make all reasonable efforts to ensure that Bishop registers as a sex offender and that he understands his responsibilities as such.  

Bishop’s criminal history reveals that in Denver County, Colorado, he was convicted in 2007 of Attempted Sexual Assault on a Child, and in the same case, Child Abuse. In 1995, he was also convicted of Domestic Violence- Harassment. 

Bishop is currently registered as living at 329 Swope Ave #3. He is described as being a white male, 65 years old, 6’0” tall, 248lbs, with gray hair and brown eyes. 

Bishop is one of twenty-one (21) “Sexually Violent Predators” currently registered with the Colorado Springs Police Department who reside in this community. The Colorado Springs Police Department takes his presence in our community very seriously and is intent on insuring he conducts himself in a lawful manner and that our citizens remain safe and aware.

Anyone with further questions about this SVP and this Community Notification process can contact Detective J.R. Brown of the Colorado Springs Police Department Registered Sex Offender Unit at 719-444-7672.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On October 2, 2025, Sand Creek Officers conducted a traffic stop.  The driver of the traffic stop was not cooperative and fled from officers.  After conducting follow up investigation, the suspect's vehicle was located at his residence.  The suspect was found to have a felony warrant on top of his new charges.  Officers negotiated with the suspect, who refused to exit his apartment and submit to arrest.  Officers eventually forced entry and the suspect continued to refuse to follow commands, instead barricading in his living room.  Officers introduced OC into the residence, and the suspect continued to disobey orders.  Officers were eventually able to convince the suspect to exit, and he was arrested without further incident.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On 10-02-2025 at approximately 1640hrs officers from the Stetson Hills Division were dispatched to the 4000 Block of Maizeland Rd. to investigate a traffic crash.  Upon arriving on scene it was determined a single motorcycle was travelling eastbound on Maizeland Rd. when it lost control. Medical personnel attempted life saving measures but the 26 year old female rider succumbed to her injuries.  The CSPD Major Crash Team responded to the scene to investigate.  The crash is still under investigation but neither speed nor impairment appear to be a factor at this time.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 10/02/25 the Homeless Outreach Team (HOT) were around the 2700 hundred block of South Academy Blvd checking on numerous complaints regarding camp and trespass violators. While in the area HOT Officers received information regarding a stabbing suspect from a previous case seen in the area. HOT Officers located the suspect on a bicycle nearby and requested additional resources. HOT Officers lost sight of the suspect for a moment as containment was set up that included additional HOT Officers, Sand Creek Patrol, K9, and Tactical units. HOT Officers obtained additional information from witnesses that indicated the suspect was still the containment area. A K9 officer located the suspect and a chase ensued. Officers quickly converged on the suspect and were able to take the suspect into custody without further incident. Arrested was Jeffrey Wallace (38), who was transported to the Criminal Justice Center on his Felony Assault Warrant and received additional charges for Obstructing a Police Officer and Possession of Drug Paraphernalia.

Title: Burglary
Summary: DART Officer Mueller noted active trespassing and potential narcotics usage occurring at an abandoned motel in the 1600 block of South Nevada Avenue.  HOT and DART officers coordinated to respond.  RTCC also assisted with the use of a UACV, and they noted several of the motel rooms had opened doors, persons on the property, and the presence of smoke coming from one of the rooms. Officers contacted and cited 11 individuals who were trespassing on the property.  Narcotics paraphernalia was recovered.  Five of the individuals had outstanding warrants.  16 warrants were cleared.  The property was boarded up and additional efforts will be made to mitigate vagrancy and narcotics activity.  A representative from Serenity Recovery Connections was on scene to offer resources to the contacted parties.

Title: Other
Summary: On 10/1/2025, at approximately 04:14am, the Colorado Springs Police Department received multiple calls about a small airplane that had crashed and was leaking fluid near the intersection of N. Powers Bl. and Barnes Rd. CSPD and CSFD responded and located the plane. No serious injuries were reported. CSFD took the lead containing the leaking fluid. This is an ongoing investigation, and citizens should expect lane closures in the area.

Title: Robbery
Summary: On September 29, 2025, at approximately 2:10 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department received a report of an active robbery occurring in the 700 block of North Academy Boulevard.  The suspect fled the scene prior to officer arrival.  During the initial investigation, officers learned a male suspect entered the store with a weapon and stole several items.  At this time, no arrests have been made and the investigation is continuing.

Title: Other
Summary: On 09/28/25 at 12:36PM, the Colorado Springs Public Safety Communications Center received a call from a reporting party who said they had been menaced with a handgun in a wooded area near 900 Technology Ct. Officers from the Sand Creek Division responded and spoke to the victim, who told them they had confronted a person who was illegally camping and during that interaction was threatened with a gun. Officers were directed to the area where the illegal camp was and contacted the suspect without incident/use of force. A stolen handgun was recovered by the officers. FLOYD HEIN (31yo) was arrested for Felony Menacing and possession of a stolen firearm.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On 09/28/2025 at approximately 2335 hours, the CSPD Communications Center received several calls about a possible head-on traffic crash around the MLK bypass and Circle Dr. Responding officers located two involved vehicles just east of Circle Dr. on the MLK overpass. One involved driver, an adult male, was deceased on scene. The second involved male driver was transported to a local hospital in serious but stable condition. At the time of this entry, speed and/or impairments are not known if contributing factors in the crash.

Title: Select Incident Type
Summary:
On 9/26/2025 at approximately 0625 hrs. officers were dispatched to the area of 11000 Voyager Pkwy. to investigate a male party trespassing on property and damaging property. The suspect was arrested and resisted officers. While at a local hospital and upon reaching the El Paso County Jail, the suspect assaulted two Colorado Springs Police Officers and one El Paso County Deputy.
  
46-year old male arrested on misdemeanor and felony charges.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On 09-27-2025, at approximately 10:00pm Falcon Patrol officers responded to a report of a domestic violence related felony menacing with a weapon in the 2500 block of Centennial Blvd. Before officers arrived, the suspect set fire to the victim’s belongings and fled. When officers and Fire Department personnel arrived, they provided aid to the victim and worked to contain the fire, which threatened nearby homes.

While this was taking place, the suspect began breaking windows of nearby businesses and vehicles, causing thousands of dollars in damage. During the incident, Officer Layshock and K9 Officer Lofgren heard breaking glass and located the suspect, later identified as Jordan Ahola, 25 yoa, behind businesses at 3400 Centennial Blvd. Jordan was holding a crowbar and refused to comply with officer commands to drop the weapon.

Jordan raised the crowbar in defiance and advanced toward officers. Officer Lofgren issued several warnings that if Jordan did not comply, K9 Riggs would be deployed. Jordan then attempted to flee, at which point Riggs was released. Riggs pursued Jordan, secured his arm, and forced him to the ground, causing Jordan to drop the crowbar. Officers quickly moved in and placed him in handcuffs.

Jordan sustained minor abrasions and puncture wounds from the K9 contact. He was transported to a local hospital for medical clearance and later booked into CJC. No officers, including K9 Riggs, were injured during the arrest.

Title: Narcotics - Vice Activity
Summary: On April 5, 2026, at approximately 7:52 am, officers observed a suspicious vehicle in the 3600 block of Austin Bluffs Parkway.  Officers contacted the occupants of the vehicle.  Officers located illegal narcotics and drug paraphernalia. Officers identified two locations in the area where individuals were selling illegal narcotics.  During the investigation, officers recovered illegal narcotics, firearms, and US currency.  Two males were arrested for distribution.  The investigation is continuing and additional arrests are possible.

Title: Assault
Summary: Officers were dispatched to a family disturbance involving the suspect, Forrest Macleod.  The initial investigation revealed there was no probable cause to arrest Mr. Macleod but officers offered him a ride to another location to separate parties to diffuse the situation for the night. Mr. Macleod agreed to the ride and went inside to pack a bag. While inside, Mr. Macleod became more agitated and called his girlfriend and threatened her. Officers tried to contact Mr. Macleod again and he discharged a firearm inside while inside his house. Officers continued to contact Mr. Macleod and eventually got him to surrender peacefully and he was taken into custody.

Title: Other
Summary:
On 4/4/2026 at approximately 10:57pm, a Metro Task Force comprised of officers from the Colorado Springs Police Department and a deputy from the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office were in the 1200 block of North Academy Blvd. conducting proactive enforcement. While there, officers observed a suspicious vehicle occupied by an adult female and an adult male, whom they were previously aware of. After initiating contact with the vehicle and identifying themselves as Police, the adult male, who was the vehicle’s driver, attempted to flee from officers. While attempting to flee, the suspect vehicle became high centered. Officers gave multiple verbal commands to the suspects to exit the vehicle, they informed the male he was under arrest, and they informed him that deadly force could be used. The female exited the vehicle, while the male refused and he failed to comply with the directions the officers provided. While directing the male suspect to surrender, he fired at least one shot towards officers. In response, three CSPD officers fired at least one round from their duty weapons, striking the suspect. The EPSO deputy did not fire their weapon. Officers then removed the male suspect from the vehicle and administered medical aid until medical personnel arrived. The male suspect was subsequently transported to a local hospital where he died due to his injuries. The female was contacted, interviewed, and her involvement in this incident is still under investigation.

In accordance with Colorado Law, the El Paso County Sheriff’s Office is the lead investigative agency for the officer-involved shooting, and all investigative updates will come from them. Within 21 days, the CSPD will release a significant event briefing video that includes body-worn camera footage and other releasable facts about the case.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 4/4/2026 at approximately 2038 hours a Mountain Metro bus was struck while stationery at a bus stop in the 2800 block of North Academy Blvd. The bus driver and driver of the vehicle that struck the bus were transported to a local hospital to be evaluated for injuries. No passengers on the bus were injured. Bus route #25 was delayed while a second bus was dispatched to complete the bus route. Based on statements from witnesses’ speed was likely a factor in this crash.

Title: Assault
Summary: At 2:30AM on 4/2/26 CSPD officers were dispatched to a business in the 500 block of South Nevada Avenue to investigate a trespassing call for service. Officers contacted a male suspect on scene and detained him without incident. During the course of their investigation, officers established probable cause to charge the suspect for trespassing and theft. As officers prepared to serve and release the suspect on a citation, he attempted to escape from custody and then assaulted an officer. After a brief physical confrontation, the suspect was returned to a cruiser and transported to the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center where he was booked on updated charges which included escape and second-degree assault.

Title: Disturbance
Summary:
On April 1, 2026, at 5 PM, a menacing was reported in the 6500 block of South Academy Boulevard.  The male suspect pulled a firearm after being cautioned to slow down.  Officers obtained video surveillance and were able to identify the suspect vehicle. At 6:20 PM a report was received of a male walking around with a gun and yelling in an  apartment complex a few blocks from the original scene.  Officers identified the suspect, but he was not located.    

At 6:54 PM a witness reported an attempted carjacking in the area of Cheyenne Meadows Road and Highway 115, when a male armed with a gun ran towards a vehicle, but the driver left before the suspect could reach the car.  

Multiple officers responded to the area and located the suspect in the apartment complex of the previous call and learned he lived in the complex.  The suspect entered his apartment, was contacted by officers and taken into custody without incident.  Two firearms were recovered on scene.

Title: Robbery
Summary:
On April 1, 2026, at approximately 10:54 a.m., Colorado Springs Police Department officers were dispatched to the 3700 block of East Pikes Peak Avenue regarding a robbery in progress. Upon arrival, officers learned that an unidentified male had entered a local business and attempted to complete a transaction, which the business declined. The suspect then jumped over the sales counter and stole an undisclosed amount of merchandise before fleeing the scene in a vehicle.

Investigators with the CSPD Robbery Unit responded and assumed control of the investigation. The case remains in its early stages and is ongoing.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
*** UPDATE *** Through the course of the investigation, officers have confirmed that the weapon involved was a BB Gun, made to look like a replica handgun. 

******************

On March 31, 2026, at 1:21 PM, CSPD officers responded to the 1100 block of Mercury Drive to a report of a juvenile brandishing a weapon on school grounds.  The juvenile fled the area, and a shelter-in-place was ordered for the immediate vicinity.  The juvenile and the discarded weapon were subsequently located.  The investigation is ongoing, and there is no ongoing threat to the community related to this incident.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 03/30/26 at approximately 2:00PM, CSPD officers from Peak Station Shift 1 responded to a report of an assault in progress at a city park, located in the 1100 block of S. Nevada Avenue. Upon arrival, officers located a victim with a serious but non-life-threatening injury to his head. An investigation was conducted and it was determined the victim was struck in the head with a large rock. A suspect was located on scene and placed in custody for Second Degree Assault. During the initial investigation, video footage was recovered that captured the incident. No additional suspects are believed to be involved in the altercation.

Title: Assault
Summary: On March 29, 2026, at 7:35PM, an assault with a weapon was reported in the 800 block of North 19th Street.  The male patient was transported to an area hospital for serious but not life threatening injuries.  The suspect is known to the victim and is identified as another resident in the complex who was not located.  Investigation ongoing.

Title: Other
Summary:
Missing Adult 
Jonathan Stephens is a 20-year-old white male last seen wearing a white button-up shirt, black jeans, black belt, black shoes, and wears eyeglasses. He was last known to be at his place of employment in the 100-block of Troy Hill Road on 3/28/2026 around 7:45PM.  He has a mental health condition that causes a greater concern for his safety.

Title: Assault
Summary: On April 8, 2026, officers responded to the 1600 of Zebulon Drive regarding a family disturbance. Officers contacted the parties involved and learned a felony crime had been committed. Officers advised the suspect he was in custody, but the suspect resisted officers’ efforts to be placed in the custody and a struggle ensued. Additional officers responded to assist, and the suspect was taken into custody and removed from the residence. Officers and suspect sustained minor injury.  The suspect was booked into CJC related to this case.  Officers remained on duty.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 4/7/26 an Officer was assaulted while making an arrest.  While the officers were effecting an arrest, the suspect headbutted an officer to the face.  The officer received minor injuries to the face and hands.  The officer remained on duty.  The suspect was booked into the Criminal Justice Center for Second Degree Assault on a Peace officer and other misdemeanor offenses.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: In the morning of 04/07/2026 officers were dispatched to a motorcycle vs. pickup truck crash in the 6800 block of Corporate Dr. Upon arrival the officers contacted the motorcycle rider who had sustained severe injuries during the crash. He was transported to a local hospital where he later succumbed to the injuries he sustained during the crash. The driver of the pickup only sustained minor injuries and was treated on scene. No arrests were made at this time. Speed is currently investigated as a contributing factor for this crash.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On 04/12/2026 at 10:06 pm CSPD dispatch received a call about a motorcycle vs SUV crash in the 3400 block of Hancock Expy. Officers arrived shortly after the call was placed and discovered the motorcycle rider was deceased as a result of the crash. The Major Crash Team was advised and responded to take over the investigation. No arrests have been made in this case. Speed is considered a contributing factor in this crash.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary: On 04/12/2026 at 01:10 pm, officers from the Falcon Division were dispatched to a single vehicle rollover crash in the 2300 block of Dublin Blvd. Upon arrival, they observed a pickup truck that had rolled over. The single occupant of the vehicle was not responsive and was transported to an area hospital, where he succumbed to the injuries sustained during the crash. The Major Crash Team was contacted and responded to the scene to take over the investigation. Speed is considered a contributing factor in this crash. The eastbound lanes of Dublin Blvd were closed for several hours.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Location: Dublin Blvd. / Gambol Quail Dr. W
Summary:
Members of Falcon Patrol were dispatched to the area of Dublin Bl and Gambol Quail Dr. reference a rollover traffic crash.  One person was reported injured and medical personnel responded to the scene.  Upon patrol's arrival, medical personnel were conducting life saving measures for the injured driver.  The driver was transported to the hospital where he was pronounced deceased. 

Members of the Major Crash Team responded to the scene and assumed investigative responsibilities.

Title: Downtown
Location: North 300 block between Tejon Street and Nevada Avenue
Summary: Peak Station Officers were notified of fires in the alleyway south of East Boulder Street between Tejon Street and Nevada Avenue.  Upon arrival, officers located several separate fires that had been started in that area.  With the assistance of Gold Hill Patrol Officers, a canvas was completed of the area.  The investigation is ongoing.

Title: MVT
Location: North Carefree Circle and Peterson Road
Summary: On 01/08/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a stolen gray Audi RS3 from the 1800 block of South Nevada Avenue. On 04/10/2026, CSPD Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives located the stolen Audi occupied by a male driver, Malik Caco, age 24. Detectives attempted a traffic stop on the stolen Audi, but Malik eluded detectives. Detectives located the stolen Audi minutes later near the intersection of North Carefree Circle and Peterson Road. Detectives utilized a police grappler and successfully stopped the stolen Audi. Malik was taken into custody and charged for motor vehicle theft and felony vehicular eluding.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 04/10/26 at 4:32PM, an officer from the Sand Creek Division was driving westbound on Platte Ave. approaching Auburn Dr. when he observed an eastbound motorcycle collide with a truck and see the rider of the motorcycle airborne. Multiple citizens called 911, and also stopped to render aid. Members of the Colorado Springs Fire Department and American Medical Response quickly arrived on scene and took over medical care. The rider was transported by ambulance to an area hospital. [UPDATE] Roadway back open at 5:45PM. [UPDATE]The rider of the motorcycle had serious but non-life threatening injuries. As the days become nicer, please be extra aware of people out on motorcycles, bikes, etc.

Title: Arrest
Summary:
On the above date and time, members from the Colorado Springs Police Department Internet Crimes Against Children and Human Trafficking Unit, arrested 33-year-old, Theodore Mendoza on an arrest warrant for charges relating to possession and distribution of child sexual abuse material. Mendoza was arrested without incident.  After his arrest a search warrant was served at his residence to recover evidence related to the investigation. 
The Colorado Springs Police Department is the lead agency for the statewide Internet Crimes Against Children Task Force, which consists of local, state and federal law enforcement agencies.

Title: Other
Summary: On this date the Colorado Springs Police Department issued a press release to the media regarding Sexually Violent Predator (SVP), 34-year-old Dusting Cole Rottinghaus. Rottinghaus is registered at 3718 Lander Ln. Please refer to the CSPD web page for further information. Contact Detective JR Brown at 719-444-7672 if you have questions regarding Dustin Rottinghaus or this notification.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: S. Nevada Ave./ E. Costilla St.
Summary: On 4/10/26 at approximately 12:46 AM, Officer Waggoner was northbound in the 400 block of S. Nevada Ave when he observed a vehicle involved in a recent traffic accident blocking the southbound lanes of traffic on S. Nevada Ave. Officer Waggoner contacted the driver, and lone occupant of the vehicle, as he initially attempted to flee the scene on foot. The driver was found to have struck landscaping and decorative concrete work along the southwest corner of the intersection prior to rolling over and disabling the vehicle in the roadway. Southbound S. Nevada Ave remained closed at E. Costilla St for approximately two hours.

Title: Traffic Crash
Location: Peterson Road and N. Carefree Cir
Summary: On Friday, 4/10/2026, at approximately 0010 hours, the Colorado Springs Police Department received a report of a two-vehicle traffic crash in the intersection of Peterson Road and N. Carefree Circle. It was determined that a blue Ford Fusion was traveling northbound at a high rate of speed on Peterson Road had failed to stop for a solid red light and struck a black Jeep causing the Jeep to roll over multiple times.  All parties involved were transported to the hospital.  At least one person had serious injuries related to the crash.  Impairment and speed appear to be a factor in this crash.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On April 9th, 2026, at approximately 1256 PM, the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) was notified of a pedestrian vs vehicle crash at the Walmart located at 3201 E Platte Ave. Preliminary investigation revealed that a vehicle lost control, struck two pedestrians as they were walking through the parking lot, then struck two parked vehicles while trying to regain control. The pedestrians were transported to an area hospital with serious but non-life-threatening injuries.  At this time, speed and impairment do not appear to be factors.

Title: Bomb Threat
Summary: An unknown male, possibly with a "middle-Eastern" accent, made threats that he was going to go to the Temple Shalom with an AR-15 and a pipe bomb and shoot and blow the place up. Officers responded and found no credible threat

Title: Bomb Threat
Summary: On Thursday, April 9, 2026, at 9:49 a.m., CSPD officers were dispatched to CommonSpirit Penrose Hospital in the 2200 block of North Nevada Avenue to investigate reports of a bomb threat, and Penrose Hospital went on controlled access status. The first officers on scene began initial sweeps of the parking garage and located 2 suspicious items. The parking garage was evacuated, and the Colorado Springs Regional Explosives Unit (REU) was dispatched. A shelter-in-place was issued for the residential area 1 block north of the hospital, between East Jackson Street and East Van Buren Street, North Cascade Avenue, and North Nevada Avenue. The REU arrived on scene and determined that neither item was an explosive.  Out of an abundance of caution, K9 and EOD sweeps were conducted in the parking garage, and no other suspicious items were found.  The shelter-in-place was lifted, and the hospital lifted its controlled access status, returning to normal operations. CSPD's investigation into the circumstances of this call is ongoing. All individuals believed to be involved in this incident have been accounted for.

Title: Traffic Crash - Fatality
Summary:
On April 22nd, 2026, at approximately 2:39 p.m., the Colorado Springs Police Department (CSPD) received multiple calls regarding a serious vehicle crash near the intersection of N. Academy Blvd. and Lehman Dr. Preliminary investigation revealed that one vehicle was northbound Academy completing a left turn at Lehman, and the other was traveling at a high-rate of speed southbound on Academy. The southbound vehicle reportedly failed to stop for the red signal light and entered the intersection where it collided with the turning vehicle. All occupants from both vehicles were transported from the scene to local area hospitals with serious reported injuries. Both north and southbound lanes of Academy Blvd. were closed for approximately three hours, and traffic was diverted while the crash scene was processed.
Due to the circumstances of the crash, the CSPD Major Crash Team responded and assumed responsibility for the investigation. Currently, speed is suspected of being a contributing factor in this crash. During the latter part of the scene investigation notification was received that one of the injured parties had succumbed to their injuries while at the hospital. No positive identification has been made at this time.
No formal charges have been filed, and this is an ongoing investigation which remains open and active.

Title: Other
Summary: On April 22, 2026, at approximately 4:48 AM, a CSPD officer noticed a large amount of smoke in the area of N. Academy Blvd. and Maizeland Rd., near the southeast corner of Palmer Park. Our partners at CSFD were dispatched to the area and began investigating the source of the smoke. At the same time, a Colorado Springs Park Ranger reported a fire burning in Palmer Park near parking lot 12. The Real Time Crime Center (RTCC) deployed a drone to Palmer Park and located an active wildfire. The drone operator was able to direct members of the Colorado Springs Fire Department to the fire. CSFD located and contained two separate fires totaling less than a quarter of an acre.  As CSFD began knocking down the fires, a CSFD drone was deployed to assist with firefighting operations. Firefighters noticed a suspicious person standing on a rock formation near the fires, watching their efforts, and began to monitor the person with their drone. The suspicious person began walking away from the area and was contacted by CSPD officers.  21-year-old Cadence Malkin immediately admitted to officers that she had started the fires.  Candence was placed into custody.  At the time, the fire is out. Palmer Park will remain closed for several hours as CSFD continues to monitor for hot spots.

Title: MVT
Summary: On 04/20/2026, the Colorado Springs Police Department investigated a stolen gray Nissan Titan, stolen from the 300 block of South Murray Boulevard. The suspect was identified by patrol officers as Trevor Gay, age 24. Trevor is a statutory prolific motor vehicle theft offender. On 04/21/2026, patrol officers with the Sand Creek Division received intel on where Trevor could be located. Motor Vehicle Theft Detectives located the stolen Nissan and confirmed Trevor was the driver. Trevor parked in an apartment complex in the 4300 block of East Pikes Peak Avenue. Detectives conducted a contact block after Trevor was observed attempting to escape in the vehicle upon detectives making their approach. Trevor exited the stolen Nissan and ran on foot. Detectives and patrol officers gave chase and took Trevor into custody without incident.

Title: Warrant
Summary: On April 19, 2026, at approximately 2:56 PM, officers were dispatched to the 2100 block of Pheasant Place for a reported domestic disturbance.  Officers made contact with a female, at the Stetson Hills Substation, who stated she was in a disturbance with her boyfriend the previous day and left the residence.  Upon arriving on the 19th, the male displayed a firearm causing her and another party to be in fear.  Officers developed probable cause to arrest the male.  On April 20, 2026, officers executed a search warrant at the residence and took the male into custody. The investigation is on-going.

Title: Other
Summary:
On April 19th, 2026, at approximately 10:00 PM, the Colorado Springs Fire Department and American Medical Response paramedics were dispatched to the 7400 block of N. Academy Blvd to investigate a report of a male party who had possibly been assaulted.  The man was later transported to a local hospital for medical treatment.

At approximately 11:00 PM, the hospital notified the CSPD 911  Communication Center that the man had passed away.

Detectives from the CSPD Homicide Unit were contacted and responded to assume the investigation.

Title: Downtown
Summary: Peak Station Shift Two Officers responded to a call for a weapons display call for service.  Upon arrival, numerous subjects were contacted and detained including some who were not cooperative with police.  While investigating, officers found a total of four handguns and one AR Pistol unsecured in vehicles on scene belonging to some of those involved in the disturbance.  Four adult males were charged with weapons related offenses and others on scene were released.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On 4/19/2026 at approximately 1700 hours, the Colorado Springs Police Department was dispatched to the area of Powers Blvd and Constitution Ave for a six-vehicle traffic crash. One motorcycle rider was transported to a local hospital with serious injuries. Traffic on Powers Blvd was affected for several hours because of the crash. As of 2200 hours, all traffic lanes are now open. Lane splitting, inappropriate lane filtering, and speed were likely contributing factors in this incident. Detectives with the CSPD Major Crash Team were notified and will take over this investigation.

Title: Assault
Summary: On 4/19/2026 at approximately 1232am, Officers responded to the 3600 block of Van Teyligen Dr in response to an assault. Upon arrival, Officers determined that the victim had escaped the residence to a nearby business and placed a separate call for service. Through the investigation it was determined that Mr. Alcorn had met a partner at a nearby club after which they returned to his residence and an altercation took place. It was discovered that the individual who returned to Mr. Alcorn's residence was held against their will and sustained injuries from a knife. The individual was able to later escape the residence and contact police.

Title: Disturbance
Location: 1500 block of E Buena Ventura Street
Summary:
On April 2026 at approximately 11:37 PM, officers from the Gold Hill Division were dispatched to a disturbance in the 1500 block of East Buena Ventura Street.  Initial information included that one person had been shot.  

When officers arrived, they found one male who had suffered at least one gunshot wound to his torso. This male was transported to a local hospital for medical treatment, but he succumbed to his injuries while at the hospital.

Officers also contacted a female inside the residence and learned that she had been involved in a disturbance with the decedent. During that disturbance, the female produced a firearm and shot the male victim.

Detectives from the homicide unit responded to assume the investigation, which is still ongoing. All parties involved in this incident have been identified and contacted by detectives. 23-year-old Danna Corral-Montelongo has been taken into custody and transported to the El Paso County Criminal Justice Center for Murder in the Second Degree.

Title: Pursuit
Summary: A Peak Station Officer was on patrol in the 00 block of Tejon Street when he was flagged down regarding an in-progress motor vehicle theft.  The officer attempted to stop the vehicle, a Jeep Grand Cherokee, but it eluded him and fled the area.  The officer did not engage the stolen vehicle in a pursuit at that time.  Through additional investigation, officers discovered two suspects carjacked the victim while armed with handguns.  A short time later, Gold Hill Patrol Officers located the vehicle and a pursuit was initiated.  The pursuit was terminated a short time later when it was deemed unsafe to the community to continue the pursuit.  Colorado State Patrol was notified of the incident and later located the vehicle.  They initiated a separate pursuit which resulting in the recovery of the Jeep.  As of this time, the suspects have not been located.

Title: Traffic Crash
Summary: On Wednesday, 4/15/2026, at approximately 1727 hours, a Colorado Springs Police Department officer was involved in a two-vehicle traffic crash. The involved vehicles were a CSPD-marked Tahoe and a blue Honda coupe. The crash occurred at the 4300 block of Tutt Blvd. It was determined that the blue Honda coupe made a left turn in front of the CSPD Tahoe, which was traveling southbound. Neither speed nor impairment appears to be a factor related to the crash. The investigation is ongoing.

Title: Other
Summary:
Officers from Stetson Hills Patrol, EOD and TEU executed a search warrant in furtherance of a harassment/ neighborhood dispute investigation.
No arrests have been made at this time, but the investigation is ongoing.

Title: Arrest
Summary: On 4/14/26, Violent Offender Fugitive Task Force detectives were working the apprehension of Crystal Burke. Burke had an active warrant for 2nd degree assault and reckless endangerment involving a firearm. Detectives observed Burke at a residence in the 7500 block of Hickorywood Dr. With the assistance of CSPD K-9 and the Tactical Enforcement Unit, officers and detectives attempted to have Burke surrender peacefully. Burke would not follow officers' orders, so a search warrant was obtained for the residence. Officers continued to attempt to make contact with Burke, but she refused to exit the residence. Officers gradually increased their tactics which ultimately resulted in the introduction of chemical munitions by TEU. Even with the introduction of the munitions, Burke would not exit. TEU officers remained diligent and continued their search of the residence, ultimately locating Burke hiding inside a dry sump pump under the stairs. Burke surrendered after she was discovered and was booked into the El Paso County Jail. During the search of the residence by VOFTF detectives a handgun was recovered from the master bedroom.
